- READY dka_hhs_v1: DKA/HHS or hyperglycemic crisis
  - readiness: Complete validated workup
  - safety 8, history 10, core exam 8, conditional exam 0, tests 10, red flags 6, management 7, evidence/LR 53, catalog gaps 0, retrieved 53, suppressed 5
  - conditional add-ons status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context)
  - module: Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS (hyperglycemia_possible_dka_v1); sources: ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026
  - issues: none
  - review notes: none
  - safety labels: Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Measure respiratory rate; Measure oxygen saturation; Measure temperature; Measure current weight if available; Document mental status; Verify airway protection
  - core exam labels: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration; Test skin turgor; Observe Kussmaul breathing; Test capillary refill; Palpate peripheral pulses; Palpate distal extremity warmth; Palpate abdomen for tenderness; Palpate for abdominal guarding
  - tests/reference thresholds: Point-of-care glucose now; Blood beta-hydroxybutyrate preferred; urine ketones only if blood ketones are unavailable; Basic metabolic panel/electrolytes with sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarbonate, BUN, creatinine, glucose, anion gap, and corrected sodium; Venous pH/blood gas for acidosis severity; arterial gas only when oxygenation/ventilation concern or local protocol requires it; Calculate effective osmolality and measure serum osmolality when HHS/mixed crisis, severe dehydration, altered mental status, or very high glucose is possible; ECG and potassium-focused monitoring if potassium abnormality, weakness, renal failure, or insulin therapy is expected; ...
  - management-changing findings: Management implication - Suspected DKA/HHS is not routine outpatient hyperglycemia; use urgent evaluation with protocolized fluids, insulin, electrolyte replacement, and precipitant treatment.; Management implication - ICU/high-acuity care is appropriate for severe DKA, HHS, shock, altered mental status, critical precipitating illness, severe electrolyte disturbance, or inability to monitor safely.; Management implication - Uncomplicated mild/moderate DKA may be managed in ED observation or step-down with close nursing, serial labs, and IV or protocolized subcutaneous rapid-acting insulin; severe/complicated DKA and HHS need higher acuity.; Management implication - Do not start or continue insulin infusion without potassium-aware protocol monitoring; if K <3.5 mmol/L, replace potassium and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L.; Management implication - For HHS, avoid overly rapid glucose, sodium, or osmolality correction; monitor neurologic status and osmolality closely.; Management implication - Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin, and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion.; ...

  - management-changing findings: Management implication - Elevated IGF-1 plus inadequate GH suppression changes to pituitary MRI/surgical pathway.; Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Management implication - Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency.; Management implication - Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway.

  - tests/reference thresholds: First confirm endogenous Cushing with UFC, late-night salivary cortisol, or 1-mg DST cortisol >1.8 ug/dL.; ACTH: normal/high supports ACTH-dependent disease.; Pituitary MRI.; Inferior petrosal sinus sampling when MRI equivocal/discordant.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Cushing safety tests: use late-night salivary cortisol, 1-mg dexamethasone suppression, or 24-hour urinary free cortisol after excluding exogenous steroids, and assess glucose, BP, potassium, infection/VTE, and bone risk to determine severity and treatment urgency.; ...
  - management-changing findings: Management implication - ACTH-dependent confirmed Cushing plus pituitary localization changes to pituitary surgery pathway; discordance triggers IPSS.; Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Management implication - Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency.; Management implication - Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway.

  - core exam labels: Inspect and press lower leg edema; Test chair-rise strength; Auscultate heart rhythm
  - tests/reference thresholds: Aldosterone, renin, and ARR with potassium correction.; Overt PA example: PRA <0.2 ng/mL/h or DRC <2 mU/L with aldosterone >15 ng/dL by LC-MS/MS or >20 ng/dL by immunoassay.; Adrenal CT and adrenal vein sampling after biochemical diagnosis.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Primary aldosteronism safety tests: check potassium and renal function, aldosterone-renin ratio under interpretable medication/posture/salt conditions, and route confirmatory testing, subtype imaging, and adrenal venous sampling when screening is positive.; Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL.; ...
  - management-changing findings: Management implication - Suppressed renin with clearly elevated aldosterone changes to PA pathway and targeted therapy/subtyping.; Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable.; Management implication - Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning.

  - tests/reference thresholds: TSH, free T4, total/free T3.; TRAb/TSI.; RAIU or Doppler ultrasound when etiology unclear.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Thyrotoxicosis safety data: check TSH, free T4, total T3, TRAb/TSI or RAIU as indicated, ECG for tachyarrhythmia, CBC/liver baseline before antithyroid drugs, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine or teratogenic treatment decisions.; TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules.; ...
  - management-changing findings: Management implication - Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism.; Management implication - TRAb or uptake pattern changes etiology-specific treatment.; Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation.; Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway.

  - tests/reference thresholds: Serum prolactin; repeat/macroprolactin if mild/discordant.; Pregnancy test, TSH/free T4, renal function.; Pituitary MRI after persistent unexplained elevation or mass symptoms.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Prolactin/pituitary mass safety tests: repeat fasting prolactin with macroprolactin, medication, pregnancy, TSH, and renal context, obtain pituitary MRI for persistent or mass-effect concern, and check gonadal/adrenal/thyroid axes for macroadenoma.; Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible.; ...
  - management-changing findings: Management implication - Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement.; Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Management implication - Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency.; Management implication - Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway.

  - tests/reference thresholds: A1c/glucose criteria plus ketones/acid-base if symptomatic.; Beta-hydroxybutyrate, BMP, venous/arterial pH, bicarbonate, anion gap.; Islet autoantibodies: GAD65, IA-2, ZnT8, insulin Ab if needed; C-peptide with concurrent glucose.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Diabetes safety tests: review POC/plasma glucose trend, BMP with potassium/bicarbonate/anion gap and creatinine, beta-hydroxybutyrate or urine ketones when acutely ill, and UACR/lipids for complication-directed therapy.; A1c or plasma glucose criteria for diagnosis/classification, plus CGM/glucometer pattern when available.; ...
  - management-changing findings: Management implication - Ketosis plus acidosis requires urgent DKA pathway.; Management implication - Positive antibodies or low C-peptide supports autoimmune insulin-deficient diabetes.; Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Management implication - Ketosis/acidosis, HHS osmolality, or severe hypoglycemia triggers urgent protocolized treatment rather than routine outpatient adjustment.; Management implication - CKD, albuminuria, ASCVD/HF risk, pregnancy, foot infection, or insulin access barriers change medication choice and follow-up urgency.

  - management-changing findings: Management implication - Low 25(OH)D with symptoms or osteomalacia labs changes from prevention advice to treatment and etiology evaluation.; Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care.; Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.; Management implication - Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency.; Management implication - Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway.

## Structured Workups

### dka_hhs_v1: DKA/HHS or hyperglycemic crisis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: dka_hhs
Installed module: Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS (hyperglycemia_possible_dka_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (dka_measure_bp) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags DKA_HHS; blood_pressure; shock; fluid_strategy; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (dka_measure_hr) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags DKA_HHS; heart_rate; dehydration; potassium_safety; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (dka_measure_rr) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; tags DKA_HHS; respiratory_rate; tachypnea; acidosis; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (dka_measure_spo2) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / Hypoxemic / Escalating oxygen need / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; tags DKA_HHS; oxygen_saturation; hypoxemia; respiratory_support; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (dka_measure_temperature) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Temperature confirms fever or hypothermia and helps frame infection, inflammatory illness, endocrine crisis, or treatment urgency.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; tags DKA_HHS; temperature; infection; sepsis; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Measure current weight if available (dka_measure_weight) - Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS: Anthropometrics support cardiometabolic risk, medication dosing or safety, and longitudinal disease-severity interpretation.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags DKA_HHS; weight; fluid_dosing; insulin_dosing; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
7. Document mental status (dka_assess_mental_status) - encephalopathy, airway-protection risk, or high-acuity disposition need This basic acuity check helps identify severity, airway/safety risk, escalation needs, and safe interpretation of the selected validated workup.; options Baseline/alert / Confused / Somnolent / Agitated / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; mental; status; severity; assessment; mental_status; safety_check; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
8. Verify airway protection (dka_check_airway_protection) - Airway protection is an acuity and safety assessment, not a core diagnostic maneuver; abnormal findings change respiratory support, aspiration precautions, and monitored-care disposition.; options Protecting airway / Questionable protection / Not protecting airway / Unable to assess; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Do not use provocative unsafe maneuvers; interpret with mental status, respiratory pattern, secretion handling, aspiration risk, local airway protocols, and clinical trajectory.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; DKA_HHS; airway_protection; altered_mental_status; respiratory_support; high_acuity; safety_check; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask diabetes history, insulin regimen, and last insulin dose - Clarifies diabetes context, hyperglycemic-crisis probability, severity, and treatment safety for DKA/HHS.; options Diabetes type known / Insulin regimen known / Pump/CGM use / Last insulin dose known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; diabetes; type; known; duration; insulin; regimen; pump; last; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Known diabetes type, duration, insulin regimen, pump/CGM use, and last insulin dose?
2. Ask hyperglycemic-crisis warning symptoms - Clarifies DKA/HHS diagnostic criteria, crisis severity, electrolyte safety, and whether current treatment matches guideline-based monitoring needs.; options Known/reviewed / Hyperglycemia / Ketones elevated / Acidosis or anion gap / Potassium abnormal / Renal/osmolality concern / Not available; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; recent; glucose; values; beta; hydroxybutyrate; urine; ketones; anion; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Recent glucose values, beta-hydroxybutyrate/urine ketones, anion gap, bicarbonate, pH, potassium, creatinine, and osmolality if known?; details Ask specifically about Recent glucose values. | Ask specifically about beta-hydroxybutyrate/urine ketones. | Ask specifically about anion gap. | Ask specifically about bicarbonate. | Ask specifically about potassium. | Ask specifically about creatinine. | Ask specifically about osmolality if known.
3. Ask insulin delivery and access precipitants - Identifies the precipitant or recurrence risk that changes acute correction, infection search, education, and discharge safety planning.; options Diabetes type known / Insulin regimen known / Pump/CGM use / Last insulin dose known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; missed; insulin; reduced; pump; infusion; failure; empty; reservoir; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Missed or reduced insulin, pump/infusion-set failure, empty reservoir, expired insulin, medication access issue, or recent dose change?; details Ask specifically about Missed. | Ask specifically about reduced insulin. | Ask specifically about pump/infusion-set failure. | Ask specifically about empty reservoir. | Ask specifically about expired insulin. | Ask specifically about medication access issue. | Ask specifically about recent dose change.
4. Ask hyperglycemia dehydration symptoms - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; options Polyuria / Polydipsia / Weight loss / Dehydration / Very dry mouth / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; polyuria; polydipsia; weight; loss; dehydration; very; mouth; clinical; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss, dehydration, or very dry mouth?
5. Ask DKA/HHS GI symptoms and oral intake - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; options None yet / Fluids given / Insulin started / Potassium/phosphate addressed / Dextrose or bicarbonate used / Antibiotics given / Monitoring level documented / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; vomit; abdomen; vomiting; abdominal; pain; poor; oral; intake; inability; keep; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Vomiting, abdominal pain, poor oral intake, or inability to keep fluids down?
6. Ask DKA/HHS infectious, ischemic, and medication triggers - Identifies the precipitant or recurrence risk that changes acute correction, infection search, education, and discharge safety planning.; options Fever / Dysuria / Cough / Wound / Line infection / Dental/skin infection / Chest pain / Stroke symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; infection; trigger; fever; dysuria; cough; wound; line; dental; skin; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Fever, dysuria, cough, wound, line infection, dental/skin infection, chest pain, stroke symptoms, pancreatitis symptoms, steroids, or other trigger?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
7. Ask hyperglycemic-crisis neurologic severity symptoms - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; options No / Confusion / Sleepiness / Seizure / Severe weakness / Inability to participate in care / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; mental; status; question; confusion; sleepiness; seizure; severe; weakness; inability; participate; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Any confusion, sleepiness, seizure, severe weakness, or inability to participate in care?; details Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about sleepiness. | Ask specifically about seizure. | Ask specifically about severe weakness. | Ask specifically about inability to participate in care.
8. Ask euglycemic DKA and special-population risks - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; sglt2; pregnancy; inhibitor; postpartum; state; fasting; carb; intake; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question SGLT2 inhibitor use, pregnancy/postpartum state, fasting/low-carb intake, alcohol, toxin exposure, or recent surgery?; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
9. Ask fluid-resuscitation and electrolyte-safety modifiers - Screens for hyperglycemic-crisis severity, euglycemic DKA risk, dehydration, HHS features, and special-population safety constraints.; options Heart failure / CKD/ESKD / Frailty / Cirrhosis / Pregnancy / Other reason to use smaller fluid boluses / Closer electrolyte monitoring / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; comorbidity; fluid; risk; heart; failure; eskd; frailty; cirrhosis; pregnancy; other; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question Heart failure, CKD/ESKD, frailty, cirrhosis, pregnancy, or other reason to use smaller fluid boluses and closer electrolyte monitoring?; details Ask specifically about Heart failure. | Ask specifically about CKD/ESKD. | Ask specifically about frailty. | Ask specifically about cirrhosis. | Ask specifically about pregnancy. | Ask specifically about other reason to use smaller fluid boluses. | Ask specifically about closer electrolyte monitoring.
10. Review hyperglycemic-crisis treatments already started - Clarifies DKA/HHS diagnostic criteria, crisis severity, electrolyte safety, and whether current treatment matches guideline-based monitoring needs.; options None yet / Fluids given / Insulin started / Potassium/phosphate addressed / Dextrose or bicarbonate used / Antibiotics given / Monitoring level documented / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; treatment; started; what; already; been; given; fluids; insulin; route; rate; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; full question What has already been given: fluids, insulin route/rate, potassium/phosphate, dextrose, bicarbonate, antibiotics, and current monitoring level?; details Ask specifically about What has already been given: fluids. | Ask specifically about insulin route/rate. | Ask specifically about potassium/phosphate. | Ask specifically about dextrose. | Ask specifically about bicarbonate. | Ask specifically about antibiotics. | Ask specifically about current monitoring level.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (dka_inspect_mucous_membranes) - Document dry mucous membranes because this changes fluid deficit assessment and monitoring urgency.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; inspect; mucous; membranes; dehydration; fluid; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test skin turgor (dka_assess_skin_turgor) - Document poor skin turgor because this supports clinically important volume depletion and fluid replacement urgency.; options Normal recoil / Reduced recoil / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Gently pinch skin over a clinically appropriate site and observe recoil, interpreting cautiously in older adults or chronic skin changes.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; skin; turgor; fluid; losses; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe Kussmaul breathing (dka_assess_kussmaul_breathing) - Document deep labored respirations because this changes acidosis severity assessment and urgency of crisis management.; options Normal effort / Increased work / Kussmaul/deep respirations / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Observe respiratory rate, depth, accessory muscle use, speech tolerance, and breathing pattern at rest.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; observe; kussmaul; breathing; clinical; presentation; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test capillary refill (dka_check_capillary_refill) - Document delayed refill because this changes shock assessment, fluid urgency, and monitored-care level.; options Normal/warm / Delayed/cool / Mottled/poor perfusion / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Apply brief pressure to a fingertip or toe and record return of color while considering temperature, perfusion, and vascular disease.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; test; capillary; refill; clinical; assessment; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate peripheral pulses (dka_palpate_peripheral_pulses) - Document weak pulses because this changes perfusion assessment and shock concern.; options Symmetric/normal / Diminished / Absent / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; palpate; peripheral; pulses; clinical; assessment; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Palpate distal extremity warmth (dka_assess_extremity_temperature) - Document cool extremities because this changes shock assessment and perfusion monitoring.; options Normal/warm / Delayed/cool / Mottled/poor perfusion / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Inspect and palpate distal extremities for warmth, color, capillary refill, mottling, and symmetry.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; palpate; extremity; warmth; clinical; assessment; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Palpate abdomen for tenderness (dka_palpate_abdomen) - Document focal tenderness because this changes concern for pancreatitis, surgical abdomen, infection, or alternate precipitant.; options Nontender / Tender / Guarding / Rebound/peritoneal concern / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Inspect and palpate the abdomen gently, localizing tenderness and stopping if guarding, rebound, or severe pain occurs.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; palpate; abdomen; tenderness; clinical; presentation; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Palpate for abdominal guarding (dka_assess_abdominal_guarding) - Document guarding because this changes surgical mimic concern and escalation pathway.; options Nontender / Tender / Guarding / Rebound/peritoneal concern / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Inspect and palpate the abdomen gently, localizing tenderness and stopping if guarding, rebound, or severe pain occurs.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags endocrine_metabolic; hyperglycemia_possible_dka_or_hhs; endocrine; metabolic; hyperglycemia; possible; abdominal; guarding; clinical; presentation; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Suspected DKA/HHS is not routine outpatient hyperglycemia; use urgent evaluation with protocolized fluids, insulin, electrolyte replacement, and precipitant treatment. (dka_emergency) - This distinction changes disposition and timing because DKA/HHS management depends on serial labs, potassium-safe insulin, fluid resuscitation, and precipitant control.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; tags DKA_HHS; urgent_evaluation; protocolized_treatment; disposition; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - ICU/high-acuity care is appropriate for severe DKA, HHS, shock, altered mental status, critical precipitating illness, severe electrolyte disturbance, or inability to monitor safely. (dka_high_acuity) - These features change level of care because deterioration, electrolyte shifts, neurologic risk, and treatment intensity require close nursing and serial reassessment.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; tags DKA_HHS; ICU; high_acuity; monitoring; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Uncomplicated mild/moderate DKA may be managed in ED observation or step-down with close nursing, serial labs, and IV or protocolized subcutaneous rapid-acting insulin; severe/complicated DKA and HHS need higher acuity. (dka_mild_moderate_dka) - Severity and monitoring capacity change whether treatment can be safely delivered outside ICU versus needing high-acuity care.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; tags DKA_HHS; mild_DKA; step_down; subcutaneous_insulin; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Do not start or continue insulin infusion without potassium-aware protocol monitoring; if K <3.5 mmol/L, replace potassium and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L. (dka_potassium_rule) - Potassium status changes immediate insulin safety because insulin can worsen hypokalemia and precipitate dangerous arrhythmias.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; tags DKA_HHS; potassium; insulin_safety; arrhythmia; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - For HHS, avoid overly rapid glucose, sodium, or osmolality correction; monitor neurologic status and osmolality closely. (dka_hhs_correction) - Correction rate changes neurologic safety and fluid/insulin strategy in HHS, especially when mental status or hyperosmolarity is prominent.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; tags DKA_HHS; HHS; osmolality; neurologic_monitoring; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin, and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion. (dka_resolution_transition) - Resolution criteria and basal overlap change recurrence risk, hypoglycemia risk, and safe transfer/discharge readiness.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; tags DKA_HHS; resolution; transition; basal_overlap; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
7. Management implication - Before discharge, confirm insulin and diabetes supplies, ketone testing plan, sick-day rules, glucagon when indicated, medication affordability, education, and close follow-up; address recurrent DKA or social/mental-health barriers. (dka_discharge_prevention) - Discharge readiness changes recurrence prevention, medication safety, and follow-up urgency after hyperglycemic crisis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS.; tags DKA_HHS; discharge_planning; sick_day_rules; recurrence_prevention; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (dka_measure_bp_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags DKA_HHS,blood_pressure,shock,fluid_strategy; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (dka_measure_hr_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags DKA_HHS,heart_rate,dehydration,potassium_safety; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure respiratory rate (dka_measure_rr_limitation) - Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; limitations Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; tags DKA_HHS,respiratory_rate,tachypnea,acidosis; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Interpretation caution - Measure oxygen saturation (dka_measure_spo2_limitation) - Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; limitations Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; tags DKA_HHS,oxygen_saturation,hypoxemia,respiratory_support; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Interpretation caution - Measure temperature (dka_measure_temperature_limitation) - Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; limitations Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; tags DKA_HHS,temperature,infection,sepsis; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Interpretation caution - Measure current weight if available (dka_measure_weight_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags DKA_HHS,weight,fluid_dosing,insulin_dosing; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
7. 8. ECG and potassium-focused monitoring if potassium abnormality, weakness, renal failure, or insulin therapy is expected (dka_ecg_potassium) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. CBC with differential plus magnesium and phosphate when crisis is suspected or insulin therapy is expected (dka_cbc_phos_mg) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
34. Precipitant workup guided by presentation: urinalysis/culture, blood cultures, chest imaging, viral testing, pregnancy test, lipase, troponin/ECG, neuroimaging, or other source evaluation (dka_trigger_tests) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
35. Hemoglobin A1c if no reliable result in the prior 3 months or if baseline control is unclear (dka_a1c) - source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
36. If treating DKA/HHS: glucose every 1-2 h; electrolytes, creatinine, phosphate, beta-hydroxybutyrate, and venous pH about every 4 h until DKA resolves; osmolality every 4 h in HHS (dka_serial_monitoring) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
37. Altered mental status, confusion, obtundation, seizure, or coma (dka_mental_status) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
38. Hypotension, shock, severe dehydration, poor perfusion, or inability to tolerate PO (dka_shock_dehydration) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
39. Kussmaul respirations, respiratory distress, or severe acidosis concern (dka_kussmaul_resp) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
40. SGLT2 inhibitor, pregnancy, fasting, or lower glucose with ketotic symptoms (dka_euglycemic_risk) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
41. Hypokalemia, marked hyperkalemia, ECG changes, severe AKI/ESKD, or inability to monitor electrolytes safely (dka_potassium_danger) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
42. Pregnancy, frailty/older adult, heart failure, advanced kidney disease, or major comorbidity affecting fluids/insulin (dka_special_population) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
43. Suspected DKA/HHS is not routine outpatient hyperglycemia; use urgent evaluation with protocolized fluids, insulin, electrolyte replacement, and precipitant treatment. (dka_emergency) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
44. ICU/high-acuity care is appropriate for severe DKA, HHS, shock, altered mental status, critical precipitating illness, severe electrolyte disturbance, or inability to monitor safely. (dka_high_acuity) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
45. Uncomplicated mild/moderate DKA may be managed in ED observation or step-down with close nursing, serial labs, and IV or protocolized subcutaneous rapid-acting insulin; severe/complicated DKA and HHS need higher acuity. (dka_mild_moderate_dka) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
46. Do not start or continue insulin infusion without potassium-aware protocol monitoring; if K <3.5 mmol/L, replace potassium and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L. (dka_potassium_rule) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
47. For HHS, avoid overly rapid glucose, sodium, or osmolality correction; monitor neurologic status and osmolality closely. (dka_hhs_correction) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
48. Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin, and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion. (dka_resolution_transition) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
49. Before discharge, confirm insulin and diabetes supplies, ketone testing plan, sick-day rules, glucagon when indicated, medication affordability, education, and close follow-up; address recurrent DKA or social/mental-health barriers. (dka_discharge_prevention) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
50. DKA: diabetes/prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL, beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+, and pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L; can be euglycemic with SGLT2, pregnancy, fasting, or poor intake (dka) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
51. HHS: glucose >=600 mg/dL, effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, absent significant ketonemia, and no significant acidosis; assess for mixed DKA/HHS (hhs) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
52. Precipitating illness or barrier: infection, insulin omission/access issue, pump failure, MI/stroke, pancreatitis, glucocorticoids/other medications, pregnancy, alcohol, or other physiologic stress (trigger) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
53. Hyperglycemia without crisis: persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL generally needs insulin initiation/intensification, while mild stable T2DM may be managed less intensively per inpatient protocol (noncrisis) - source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Catalog gaps needing review
- none

### suspected_pe_v1: Suspected pulmonary embolism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: suspected_pe; chest_pain

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
