Focused history questions
1. Ask PE severity, VTE risk factors, and bleeding risk - host/exposure risk - PE/DVT probability and treatment safety: cardiopulmonary symptoms, VTE risk factors, prior clot, DVT symptoms, and anticoagulation or thrombolysis contraindications.; options No / Sudden dyspnea / Pleuritic pain / Hemoptysis / Syncope / Prior VTE / Surgery-immobility-travel / Estrogen-pregnancy-postpartum / Cancer / Unilateral leg swelling-pain / Bleeding risk / Other ___; ask when Suspected pulmonary embolism; tags pulmonary_embolism; dvt; vte; clinical_probability; anticoagulation_safety; history_question; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Any sudden shortness of breath, pleuritic chest pain, hemoptysis, syncope, prior VTE, recent surgery/immobility/travel, estrogen or pregnancy/postpartum state, cancer, unilateral leg swelling or pain, or bleeding risk?; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
2. Ask PE severity, VTE risk factors, and bleeding risk - respiratory source - Respiratory severity in suspected PE: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, cyanosis, fatigue, or impending respiratory failure.; options No / Sudden dyspnea / Pleuritic pain / Hemoptysis / Syncope / Escalating oxygen / Cannot speak full sentences / Other ___; ask when Suspected pulmonary embolism; tags pulmonary_embolism; dyspnea; hypoxia; respiratory_status; diagnostic_safety; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ATS_CAP_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Any sudden dyspnea, pleuritic pain, hemoptysis, syncope, escalating oxygen need, or inability to speak full sentences?; details Ask specifically about sudden dyspnea. | Ask specifically about pleuritic pain. | Ask specifically about hemoptysis. | Ask specifically about syncope. | Ask specifically about escalating oxygen need. | Ask specifically about inability to speak full sentences.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Checks work of breathing and alternate pulmonary diagnoses while PE remains on the table.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished; use when Dyspnea; pulmonary disease; infection; technique Auscultate posterior lung fields at least 3 levels bilaterally with diaphragm; avoid scapulae; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; dyspnea; hypoxia; inpatient_bedside; pneumonia; respiratory_support; auscultate_posterior_lung_fields; heent_exam; infection; posterior_thorax; pulmonary_disease; pulmonary_exam; respiratory_status; sepsis; Dyspnea; pulmonary disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.; options Normal / Murmur / Rub / Gallop / Other ___; use when Cardiac exam; chest pain; dyspnea; murmur; technique Use diaphragm and bell over aortic, pulmonic, tricuspid, and mitral areas; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags chest_pain; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; auscultate_heart_with_diaphragm_and_bell; auscultation; cardiac_auscultation; cardiac_exam; dyspnea; hypoxia; murmur; pulmonary_exam; respiratory_status; valvular_disease; Cardiac exam; chest pain; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.; options Not elevated / Elevated / Unable to assess; use when Heart failure; volume overload; shock; technique Stand on patient's right; observe jugular venous pulsations; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypovolemia; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; diuresis; hemodynamics; jvp; neck; peripheral_pulses; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; volume_status; Heart failure; volume overload; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Assesses rhythm, murmur, heart failure/strain, and perfusion clues.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; use when Vascular exam; shock; limb symptoms; technique Palpate radial pulses bilaterally and symmetrically; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypoglycemia; hypovolemia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; hemodynamics; limb_symptoms; peripheral_pulses; radial_pulses; upper_extremity; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; Vascular exam; limb symptoms; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Observe work of breathing (REQ-pe-work-of-breathing) - Suspected PE workups need a direct respiratory-severity check before lower-yield pulmonary maneuvers, because distress and hypoxemia change acuity even before imaging confirms PE.; options Comfortable / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable to speak full sentences / Cyanotic / Unable; use when Suspected pulmonary embolism; technique Work of breathing observation; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags pulmonary_embolism; dyspnea; hypoxia; respiratory_status; diagnostic_safety; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ATS_CAP_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect unilateral leg swelling (REQ-pe-unilateral-leg-swelling-inspection) - Suspected PE workups should compare legs for DVT-compatible findings instead of relying only on history, because a proximal DVT clue changes VTE probability and imaging/treatment framing.; options Symmetric / Right more swollen / Left more swollen / Calf tenderness / Erythema-warmth / Unable; use when Suspected pulmonary embolism; technique Unilateral leg swelling inspection; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags pulmonary_embolism; dvt; vte; lower_extremity; vascular_exam; clinical_probability; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Work of breathing or focal lung findings can change oxygen strategy, imaging review, treatment for alternatives such as pneumonia/pneumothorax, and escalation. (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Respiratory distress, wheeze, crackles, focal diminished sounds, or other findings that point to PE severity or competing lung diagnoses.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - New rhythm, murmur, displaced impulse, or extra sound can change telemetry, ECG/echo need, volume strategy, or escalation. (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - murmur, abnormal heart sound, rhythm irregularity, or S3/S4 clue; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context. (EXAM-098-jvp) - elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Abnormal vitals or perfusion findings can trigger urgent reassessment, resuscitation, sepsis/shock pathway review, or escalation. (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - perfusion deficit, vascular disease, or limb ischemia clue; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Management implication - Increased work of breathing changes oxygen/support strategy, monitoring level, ED/ICU escalation, imaging urgency, and high-risk PE pathway consideration. (REQ-pe-work-of-breathing) - Respiratory severity in suspected PE: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, cyanosis, fatigue, or impending respiratory failure.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ATS_CAP_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Management implication - DVT-compatible findings can increase VTE probability, raise compression ultrasound priority, support anticoagulation documentation, and guide strategy when PE chest imaging is delayed or contraindicated. (REQ-pe-unilateral-leg-swelling-inspection) - DVT-compatible bedside clue: asymmetric calf or leg swelling, focal calf tenderness, erythema/warmth, or an alternate limb process.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Management implication - PE probability (REQ-pe-probability-d-dimer-imaging) - Diagnostic pathway selection: low/intermediate probability with D-dimer rule-out potential versus high probability or unstable PE requiring imaging/escalation.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
8. Management implication - DVT and right-heart strain evaluation (REQ-pe-dvt-rv-strain-workup) - VTE confirmation and PE severity: proximal DVT evidence, right ventricular strain, myocardial injury, hemodynamic compromise, and oxygenation trend.; source ESC_PE_2019; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
9. Management implication - High-risk PE escalation cues (REQ-pe-hemodynamic-escalation-cues) - High-risk PE physiology: shock, hypotension, syncope, severe hypoxemia, altered mentation, persistent tachycardia/tachypnea, RV strain, or arrest.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
10. Management implication - Anticoagulation and thrombolysis safety cues (REQ-pe-anticoagulation-safety-cues) - Treatment-risk context: bleeding risk, recent surgery/trauma, intracranial risk, severe hypertension, pregnancy/postpartum, renal/contrast limitation, fall risk, or pending procedure.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Respiratory distress, wheeze, crackles, focal diminished sounds, or other findings that point to PE severity or competing lung diagnoses.; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Interpretation caution - Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - murmur, abnormal heart sound, rhythm irregularity, or S3/S4 clue; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Interpretation caution - Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Interpretation caution - Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - perfusion deficit, vascular disease, or limb ischemia clue; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Interpretation caution - PE probability and anticoagulation-safety question (REQ-pe-risk-and-bleeding-history) - PE/DVT probability and treatment safety: cardiopulmonary symptoms, VTE risk factors, prior clot, DVT symptoms, and anticoagulation or thrombolysis contraindications.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
6. Interpretation caution - Observe work of breathing (REQ-pe-work-of-breathing) - Respiratory severity in suspected PE: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, cyanosis, fatigue, or impending respiratory failure.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ATS_CAP_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Interpretation caution - Inspect unilateral leg swelling (REQ-pe-unilateral-leg-swelling-inspection) - DVT-compatible bedside clue: asymmetric calf or leg swelling, focal calf tenderness, erythema/warmth, or an alternate limb process.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source ESC_PE_2019; ACEP_VTE_POLICY; ASH_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; CDC_VTE_DIAGNOSIS; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### chest_pain_acs_v1: Chest pain / ACS-style presentation

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: chest_pain
Installed module: Chest pain (chest_pain_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (cp_measure_bp) - Chest pain: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags chest_pain; blood_pressure; shock; aortic_syndrome; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (cp_measure_hr) - Chest pain: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags chest_pain; heart_rate; arrhythmia; monitoring; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (cp_measure_rr) - Chest pain: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; tags chest_pain; respiratory_rate; dyspnea; cardiopulmonary; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (cp_measure_spo2) - Chest pain: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / Hypoxemic / Escalating oxygen need / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; tags chest_pain; oxygen_saturation; hypoxemia; pulmonary_embolism; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Observe general appearance (cp_assess_general_appearance) - Chest pain: Global acuity and perfusion findings identify patients who need urgent escalation before a routine focused workup is completed.; options Well-appearing / Ill-appearing / Toxic/unstable appearing / Unable to assess; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.; tags chest_pain; general_appearance; diaphoresis; distress; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. When did the chest discomfort start, and is it ongoing? - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; options Onset time documented / Ongoing now / Resolved / Recurrent/intermittent / Unknown; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; onset; duration; when; discomfort; start; ongoing; symptom; characterization; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021
2. Ask chest pain quality, location, and radiation - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; options Pressure/heaviness / Sharp or pleuritic / Burning/reflux-like / Reproducible with palpation/movement / Radiates to arm/jaw/back/shoulder / Other ___; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; quality; location; radiation; what; does; feel; like; where; radiate; back; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; full question What does it feel like, where is it, and does it radiate to arm, jaw, back, or shoulder?; details Ask specifically about What does it feel like. | Ask specifically about where is it. | Ask specifically about does it radiate to arm. | Ask specifically about back. | Ask specifically about shoulder.
3. Ask exertional pattern and response to rest - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; options Exertional / Relieved by rest / Similar to prior angina / Non-exertional / Not sure; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; exertional; pattern; relieved; rest; similar; prior; angina; ischemic; symptom; assessment; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; full question Is it exertional, relieved by rest, or similar to prior angina?
4. Ask cardiopulmonary associated symptoms - Screens for PE, pneumothorax, pneumonia, heart failure, and other cardiopulmonary mimics that change testing pathway and disposition.; options No / Shortness of breath / Pleuritic pain / Cough / Hemoptysis / Oxygen requirement / Other ___; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; dyspnea; shortness; breath; pleuritic; cough; hemoptysis; oxygen; requirement; associated; symptoms; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; full question Any shortness of breath, pleuritic pain, cough, hemoptysis, or oxygen requirement?; details Ask specifically about shortness of breath. | Ask specifically about pleuritic pain. | Ask specifically about cough. | Ask specifically about hemoptysis. | Ask specifically about oxygen requirement.
5. Ask ischemic and arrhythmia-associated symptoms - Clarifies associated autonomic or cardiopulmonary symptoms that increase concern for ACS or another high-acuity chest-pain cause.; options No / Diaphoresis / Nausea/vomiting / Palpitations / Marked weakness / Other ___; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; diaphoresis; nausea; vomiting; palpitations; marked; weakness; associated; symptoms; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; full question Any diaphoresis, nausea/vomiting, palpitations, or marked weakness?; details Ask specifically about diaphoresis. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about marked weakness.
6. Ask high-risk chest pain warning features - Screens for high-risk red flags such as syncope, neurologic symptoms, sudden maximal pain, or aortic-syndrome features that require urgent escalation.; options No / Syncope / Presyncope / Neurologic symptoms / Sudden maximal pain / Other ___; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; syncope; presyncope; neurologic; symptoms; sudden; maximal; high; risk; features; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; full question Any syncope, presyncope, neurologic symptoms, or sudden maximal pain?; details Ask specifically about syncope. | Ask specifically about presyncope. | Ask specifically about neurologic symptoms. | Ask specifically about sudden maximal pain.
7. Ask cardiovascular risk history - Clarifies ischemic probability, symptom tempo, risk pathway selection, and urgent ECG/troponin testing needs for chest pain.; options CAD / Prior MI/stent/CABG / Diabetes / CKD / Smoking / Hypertension / Strong family history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; risk; known; prior; stent; cabg; diabetes; smoking; hypertension; strong; family; history; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; full question Known CAD, prior MI/stent/CABG, diabetes, CKD, smoking, hypertension, or strong family history?; details Ask specifically about Known CAD. | Ask specifically about prior MI/stent/CABG. | Ask specifically about diabetes. | Ask specifically about smoking. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about strong family history.
8. Ask medication, stimulant, and thrombotic-risk modifiers - Identifies stimulant, anticoagulant, estrogen, or medication exposures that change ACS, PE, bleeding, and treatment-safety assessment.; options Cocaine/stimulant / Anticoagulants / Estrogen therapy / Medication changes / Other ___; ask when Ask when Chest pain is the selected validated workup or when this answer would change diagnostic probability, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; toxin; recent; cocaine; stimulant; anticoagulants; estrogen; therapy; medication; changes; risk; modifiers; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; full question Recent cocaine/stimulant use, anticoagulants, estrogen therapy, or medication changes?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Auscultate heart rhythm (cp_auscultate_heart_rhythm) - Document irregular rhythm because atrial fibrillation or other arrhythmia changes ECG interpretation, monitoring, and management.; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, murmurs, rubs, or extra heart sounds.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; auscultate; heart; rhythm; focused; exam; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate for cardiac murmurs (cp_auscultate_murmurs) - Document a new murmur because it changes concern for valvular disease, mechanical complication, or aortic pathology.; options No murmur / Systolic murmur / Diastolic murmur / New/changed murmur / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, murmurs, rubs, or extra heart sounds.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; auscultate; murmurs; cardiac; focused; exam; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate for gallop (cp_auscultate_gallop) - Document an S3 or S4 when present because it changes heart failure or ischemic risk framing.; options No gallop / S3 / S4 / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, murmurs, rubs, or extra heart sounds.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; auscultate; gallop; focused; exam; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe work of breathing (cp_assess_work_of_breathing) - Document increased work of breathing because it changes respiratory support needs and cardiopulmonary emergency concern.; options Normal effort / Increased work / Kussmaul/deep respirations / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Observe respiratory rate, depth, accessory muscle use, speech tolerance, and breathing pattern at rest.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; work; breathing; alternative; cardiopulmonary; emergencies; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Auscultate lung sounds (cp_auscultate_lung_sounds) - Document wheeze, crackles, or asymmetric breath sounds because these change heart failure, obstructive lung disease, pneumonia, or pneumothorax concern.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheeze / Diminished/asymmetric / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Auscultate symmetric anterior, lateral, and posterior lung fields as feasible, comparing sides for crackles, wheeze, diminished sounds, or asymmetry.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; auscultate; lung; sounds; alternative; cardiopulmonary; emergencies; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Palpate radial pulses (cp_palpate_radial_pulses) - Document diminished or asymmetric pulses because this changes concern for shock or acute aortic syndrome.; options Symmetric/normal / Diminished / Absent / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; palpate; radial; pulses; life; threatening; causes; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Inspect and palpate extremity perfusion (cp_assess_extremity_perfusion) - Document cool extremities, delayed capillary refill, or poor perfusion because these change shock assessment and escalation.; options Normal/warm / Delayed/cool / Mottled/poor perfusion / Unable to assess; use when Perform when Chest pain is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.; technique Inspect and palpate distal extremities for warmth, color, capillary refill, mottling, and symmetry.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Chest pain; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.; tags cardiovascular; chest_pain; chest; pain; extremity; perfusion; life; threatening; causes; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Use a structured chest pain clinical decision pathway and risk stratification rather than gestalt alone. (cp_structured_pathway) - Structured pathways improve diagnostic safety and determine safe discharge, observation, admission, imaging, or cardiology consultation decisions.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Chest pain.; tags chest_pain; clinical_decision_pathway; risk_stratification; diagnostic_safety; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (cp_measure_bp_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags chest_pain,blood_pressure,shock,aortic_syndrome; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (cp_measure_hr_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags chest_pain,heart_rate,arrhythmia,monitoring; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure respiratory rate (cp_measure_rr_limitation) - Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; limitations Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; tags chest_pain,respiratory_rate,dyspnea,cardiopulmonary; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Interpretation caution - Measure oxygen saturation (cp_measure_spo2_limitation) - Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; limitations Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; tags chest_pain,oxygen_saturation,hypoxemia,pulmonary_embolism; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Interpretation caution - Observe general appearance (cp_assess_general_appearance_limitation) - Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.; limitations Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.; tags chest_pain,general_appearance,diaphoresis,distress; source AHA_ACC_CHEST_PAIN_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Measure temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask cardiopulmonary associated symptoms - Dyspnea/heart-failure source and severity: pulmonary congestion, obstructive/infectious symptoms, ischemic symptoms, oxygen escalation, or volume change.; options No change / Exertional dyspnea / Orthopnea-PND / Cough-wheeze / Chest pain / Leg swelling / Weight change / Oxygen need / Missed diuretics / Other ___; ask when Ask when dyspnea, hypoxia, heart failure, orthopnea, PND, edema, or volume overload is the selected validated intent.; tags dyspnea; heart_failure; volume_overload; pulmonary_exam; history_question; dyspnea_hf; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; JAMA_RCE; SM25; full question What changed with breathing: onset, exertional dyspnea, orthopnea or PND, cough/wheeze, chest pain, leg swelling, weight change, oxygen need, or missed diuretics?; details Ask specifically about What changed with breathing: onset. | Ask specifically about exertional dyspnea. | Ask specifically about orthopnea. | Ask specifically about cough/wheeze. | Ask specifically about chest pain. | Ask specifically about leg swelling. | Ask specifically about weight change. | Ask specifically about oxygen need.
2. Ask respiratory infection-source symptoms - Respiratory severity: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, fatigue, cyanosis, or impending respiratory failure.; options Rest dyspnea / Exertional / Orthopnea / PND / Chest pain / Cough-wheeze / Fever / Leg swelling / Higher oxygen need / Other ___; ask when Dyspnea, hypoxia, or heart failure/volume overload; tags dyspnea; hypoxia; respiratory_status; pulmonary_exam; heart_failure; red_flag; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Is breathing worse at rest, with exertion, lying flat, during sleep, or with chest pain, cough, wheeze, fever, leg swelling, or higher oxygen need?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Auscultate posterior lung fields (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Differentiates congestion, wheeze, consolidation, effusion, or other pulmonary causes.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished; use when Dyspnea; pulmonary disease; infection; technique Auscultate posterior lung fields at least 3 levels bilaterally with diaphragm; avoid scapulae; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags dyspnea; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; hypoxia; inpatient_bedside; pneumonia; respiratory_support; auscultate_posterior_lung_fields; heent_exam; infection; posterior_thorax; pulmonary_disease; pulmonary_exam; respiratory_status; sepsis; Dyspnea; pulmonary disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options Normal / Murmur / Rub / Gallop / Other ___; use when Cardiac exam; chest pain; dyspnea; murmur; technique Use diaphragm and bell over aortic, pulmonic, tricuspid, and mitral areas; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags volume_overload; dyspnea; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; auscultate_heart_with_diaphragm_and_bell; auscultation; cardiac_auscultation; cardiac_exam; chest_pain; hypoxia; murmur; pulmonary_exam; respiratory_status; valvular_disease; Cardiac exam; chest pain; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options Not elevated / Elevated / Unable to assess; use when Heart failure; volume overload; shock; technique Stand on patient's right; observe jugular venous pulsations; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags volume_overload; AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypovolemia; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; diuresis; hemodynamics; jvp; neck; peripheral_pulses; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; volume_status; Heart failure; volume overload; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
4. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options None / Trace / 1+ / 2+ / 3+; use when Heart failure; renal disease; liver disease; edema; technique Apply firm pressure on lower tibia bony surface for at least 5 seconds bilaterally; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags volume_overload; AKI; DKA_HHS; abdominal_pain; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; flank_pain; heart_failure; hypercalcemia; hypovolemia; infection; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; aki; biliary_disease; cholecystitis; diuresis; edema; liver_disease; lower_extremity; peripheral_pulses; pyelonephritis; renal_colic; renal_disease; ruq_pain; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; volume_status; Heart failure; renal disease; liver disease; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Checks congestion, cardiac auscultation, and perfusion relevant to fluid and diuretic strategy.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; use when Vascular exam; shock; limb symptoms; technique Palpate radial pulses bilaterally and symmetrically; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags volume_overload; AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypoglycemia; hypovolemia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; perfusion; shock; hemodynamics; limb_symptoms; peripheral_pulses; radial_pulses; upper_extremity; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; Vascular exam; limb symptoms; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect posterior thorax (EXAM-089-posterior-thorax-inspection) - Assesses respiratory effort and distress before deciding whether bedside support or escalation is needed.; options Normal / Increased work / Asymmetric / Deformity; use when Dyspnea; pulmonary exam; technique Expose the posterior chest as appropriate and inspect from behind for symmetry, deformity, accessory muscle use, retractions, and respiratory effort before auscultation or percussion.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags dyspnea; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; hypoxia; inpatient_bedside; pneumonia; respiratory_support; functional_status; inspect_posterior_thorax; msk_exam; pain; posterior_thorax; pulmonary_exam; respiratory_status; Dyspnea; pulmonary exam; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Observe work of breathing (REQ-dyspnea-work-of-breathing) - Dyspnea workups must start with respiratory effort because distress changes oxygen, ventilatory support, monitoring, and disposition before the etiology is fully proven.; options Comfortable / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable to speak full sentences / Fatigued / Unable; use when Dyspnea, hypoxia, or heart failure/volume overload; technique Work of breathing observation; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags dyspnea; hypoxia; respiratory_status; pulmonary_exam; heart_failure; red_flag; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Palpate point of maximal impulse (EXAM-107-pmi) - Adds apical impulse only when dyspnea overlaps with heart failure, cardiomyopathy, pulmonary hypertension, or volume-overload context.; options Normal / Displaced / Diffuse / Unable; use when Heart failure; cardiomyopathy; technique Palpate point of maximal impulse at 5th ICS left MCL; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags volume_overload; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; cardiac_exam; cardiomyopathy; diuresis; palpate_pmi; precordium; volume_status; Heart failure; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
Conditional add-on status: Conditional add-ons active (active_addons_present) - 1 conditional physical exam add-on activated from the selected validated intent, active modifiers, or source-specific trigger logic.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - New or changed lung findings can change diuresis, bronchodilator, antibiotic, imaging, oxygen/support, or escalation decisions. (EXAM-092-posterior-lung-sounds) - Lung findings: crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, asymmetry, or focal ventilation abnormality.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Congestion or poor perfusion findings can change diuretic/fluid strategy, telemetry/ECG/echo urgency, respiratory support, and disposition. (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Heart-failure/volume clue: elevated JVP, edema, S3/gallop or murmur, irregular rhythm, or perfusion deficit.; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Increased work of breathing can change oxygen/ventilatory support, monitoring location, and urgency of reassessment. (EXAM-089-posterior-thorax-inspection) - Work of breathing: accessory muscle use, retractions, asymmetry, fatigue, or distress.; source Stanford Medicine 25 pulmonary exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Increased work of breathing changes oxygen/ventilatory support, need for urgent reassessment, ED/ICU escalation, and timing of CXR/ECG/labs. (REQ-dyspnea-work-of-breathing) - Respiratory severity: tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, inability to speak, fatigue, cyanosis, or impending respiratory failure.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Management implication - Dyspnea/HF initial tests and reference thresholds (REQ-dyspnea-hf-initial-tests) - HF/dyspnea etiology and safety screen: congestion/cardiomegaly or alternative disease on CXR, rhythm/ischemia on ECG, natriuretic peptide support or exclusion of HF, renal/electrolyte safety, anemia/infection, and myocardial injury.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
6. Management implication - Diuresis and respiratory-support safety review (REQ-dyspnea-diuresis-safety-tests) - Treatment-safety data: oxygen trajectory, renal function, potassium/magnesium/sodium abnormalities, acid-base or ventilatory failure, and response to diuresis or respiratory support.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
7. Management implication - Respiratory failure escalation cues (REQ-dyspnea-respiratory-failure-cues) - Impending or established respiratory failure: severe hypoxemia, escalating oxygen, fatigue, cyanosis, altered mentation, hypercapnia, or acidemia.; source ESC_HF_2021; ATS_CAP_2025; IDSA_CAP_PATHWAY_2019; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
8. Management implication - Shock or acute heart-failure escalation cues (REQ-dyspnea-shock-acute-hf-cues) - High-risk cardiac dyspnea clue: shock, poor perfusion, oliguria, ischemia, dangerous arrhythmia, syncope, flash pulmonary edema, hypertensive emergency, or acute valvular complication.; source AHA_ACC_HFSA_HF_2022; ESC_HF_2021; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
9. Management implication - A displaced or diffuse impulse can support cardiac structural framing and echo/cardiology review when consistent with the rest of the case. (EXAM-107-pmi) - Precordial structural clue: displaced/diffuse apical impulse or visible heave in cardiomyopathy/volume-overload context.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### sleep_apnea_snoring_v1: Snoring or suspected obstructive sleep apnea

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: sleep_apnea_airway

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - SGLT2/fasting risks - OSA severity, safety risk, cardiometabolic comorbidity, medication/alcohol contributors, and treatment readiness.; options None / Drowsy driving-work safety / Resistant hypertension / Atrial fibrillation / Heart failure / Opioid-sedative use / Alcohol near bedtime / Prior sleep study / CPAP used / CPAP intolerant / Oral appliance interest / Other ___; ask when Ask when snoring, witnessed apneas, daytime sleepiness, morning headaches, resistant hypertension, or suspected obstructive sleep apnea is selected.; tags sleep_apnea; snoring; cardiometabolic; treatment_safety; history_question; sleep_apnea_airway; AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; full question Any drowsy driving or work-safety risk, resistant hypertension, atrial fibrillation, heart failure, opioid or sedative use, alcohol near bedtime, prior sleep study or CPAP use, CPAP intolerance, or oral-appliance interest?; details Ask specifically about drowsy driving. | Ask specifically about work-safety risk. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about atrial fibrillation. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about opioid. | Ask specifically about sedative use. | Ask specifically about alcohol near bedtime.
2. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - sleep apnea symptoms - OSA risk phenotype: enlarged neck circumference or upper-airway crowding in a patient with snoring, witnessed apneas, or sleepiness.; options No / Loud snoring / Witnessed apnea / Choking awakenings / Sleepiness / Morning headache / Resistant hypertension / Drowsy driving / Other ___; ask when Sleep apnea or snoring airway-risk evaluation; tags sleep_apnea; snoring; neck_circumference; upper_airway; source AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, waking choking, morning headache, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or drowsy driving?; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about waking choking. | Ask specifically about morning headache. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about drowsy driving.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oropharynx (EXAM-061-oropharynx) - Looks for visible oropharyngeal crowding or oral airway findings that support sleep-apnea risk assessment.; options Clear / Erythematous / Exudate / Swollen / Uvula deviated; use when Sore throat; infection; airway concerns; technique Use tongue blade and light; inspect posterior pharynx, uvula, tonsils; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Use a clean light and tongue blade when needed; stop if gagging, trismus, airway concern, severe pain, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; heent; hypovolemia; infection; inpatient_bedside; thyroid_disease; airway_concerns; cranial_nerve_exam; heent_exam; inspect_oropharynx; mouth; oropharynx; pneumonia; sepsis; sore_throat; stroke; Sore throat; airway concerns; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Measure neck circumference (REQ-osa-neck-circumference) - Neck circumference is a quick bedside OSA risk phenotype item and should be shown as an active exam step rather than a reviewer-only gap.; options Measured ___ cm / Enlarged by local threshold / Not measured / Unable; use when Sleep apnea or snoring airway-risk evaluation; technique With the patient upright and head neutral, place a tape measure horizontally around the neck at the level just below the laryngeal prominence and record the circumference in centimeters.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment tape measure, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags sleep_apnea; snoring; neck_circumference; upper_airway; source AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Crowded airway findings support sleep-study referral, airway-risk documentation, and PAP/oral-appliance discussion in context. (EXAM-061-oropharynx) - Upper-airway risk clue: crowded oropharynx, large tonsils, macroglossia, or other visible obstruction pattern.; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - A high-risk phenotype supports formal sleep testing referral, PAP/oral-appliance discussion, peri-procedural airway-risk documentation, and cardiometabolic follow-up. (REQ-osa-neck-circumference) - OSA risk phenotype: enlarged neck circumference or upper-airway crowding in a patient with snoring, witnessed apneas, or sleepiness.; source AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - OSA diagnostic testing pathway (REQ-osa-testing-pathway) - Objective sleep-disordered breathing diagnosis, severity, and suitability for home versus laboratory testing.; source AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Sleep apnea safety and escalation cues (REQ-osa-safety-red-flags) - High-risk sleep-disordered breathing context: driving risk, severe hypersomnolence, hypoxemia, cardiopulmonary disease, resistant hypertension, or sedative/opioid risk.; source AASM_OSA_DIAGNOSTIC_2017; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
