Focused history questions
1. Ask UTI symptoms and complicated-infection risk - Abdominal source and acuity: biliary, appendiceal, pancreatic, obstruction/ileus, GI bleed, GU/renal, pelvic/pregnancy-related, infectious/inflammatory, ischemic, or nonspecific self-limited pattern.; options Diffuse cramps / RUQ / RLQ / Epigastric / LLQ / Flank-urinary / Fever / Persistent vomiting / Black-bloody stool / Jaundice / Pregnancy possible / Syncope / Obstipation / Severe-worsening / Other ___; ask when Abdominal or GI presentation; tags abdominal_pain; acute_abdomen; peritonitis; biliary_disease; appendicitis; pregnancy; gi_bleed; history_question; source AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Where is the pain worst, what changed over time, and are there fever, persistent vomiting, blood or black stool, jaundice, urinary/flank symptoms, pregnancy possibility, syncope, severe/worsening focal pain, or inability to pass stool/gas?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
2. Ask DKA/HHS GI symptoms and oral intake - Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.; options Diffuse / Focal location ___ / Vomiting / Diarrhea / Constipation / Urinary symptoms / Fever / Bleeding / Other ___; ask when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; inspect_abdomen; inspection; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; full question Where is the pain worst, and is it associated with vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, urinary symptoms, fever, or bleeding?; details Ask specifically about Where is the pain worst. | Ask specifically about is it associated with vomiting. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about urinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about bleeding.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect abdomen (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Flat / Distended / Scar / Lesion / Asymmetric; use when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; technique With the patient supine and the abdomen exposed appropriately, inspect contour, distension, scars, visible peristalsis, pulsations, lesions, and symmetry before palpation.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; inspect_abdomen; inspection; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Percuss abdomen (EXAM-075-abdominal-percussion) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Tympanic / Dullness / Tender / Not done; use when Abdominal pain; ascites/organomegaly concern; technique If pain present, start away from painful area; percuss all 4 quadrants; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; dyspnea; hypercalcemia; hypoxia; inpatient_bedside; pneumonia; respiratory_support; abdomen; abdominal_exam; ascites_organomegaly_concern; core_exam; percuss_abdomen; percussion; Abdominal pain; ascites/organomegaly concern; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate bowel sounds (EXAM-074-bowel-sounds) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Present / Hypoactive / Hyperactive / Absent; use when Abdominal pain; obstruction/ileus concern; technique Auscultate before percussion/palpation with diaphragm in one quadrant at least 10 seconds; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; auscultate_abdomen; auscultation; core_exam; obstruction_ileus_concern; Abdominal pain; obstruction/ileus concern; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate abdomen (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - Defines tenderness pattern, distension, bowel activity, and peritoneal concern.; options Nontender / Tender ___ / Guarding / Mass ___; use when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; technique If pain present, start away from painful area; palpate lightly and deeply in all 4 quadrants; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Use gentle technique; stop if severe pain, guarding, instability, or patient preference limits exam.; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; light_and_deep_palpation; palpation; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Test rebound tenderness (EXAM-081-rebound-tenderness) - Makes peritoneal irritation explicit as a conditional abdominal-pain maneuver instead of hiding it until the user supplies perfect localization terms.; options No rebound or involuntary guarding / Rebound tenderness present / Rigidity or generalized peritonitis concern / Unable to assess; use when Abdominal or GI presentation; technique Palpate gently first, then press slowly away from maximal tenderness and release quickly; document pain worse on release, involuntary guarding, rigidity, or inability to tolerate the maneuver.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags abdominal_pain; peritonitis; acute_abdomen; source MCGEE_EBPD; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test Murphy sign (EXAM-080-murphy-sign) - Keeps biliary disease on the abdominal-pain bedside differential; perform when pain localizes to RUQ/epigastrium or biliary features are present.; options Negative Murphy sign / RUQ inspiratory arrest or positive Murphy sign / Peritoneal or unstable exam concern / Unable to assess; use when Abdominal or GI presentation; technique Press under the right costal margin at the midclavicular line while the patient takes a deep breath; document inspiratory arrest or focal RUQ pain and stop if severe pain or instability occurs.; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags abdominal_pain; ruq_pain; biliary_disease; cholecystitis; source MCGEE_EBPD; SM25; WSES acute calculous cholecystitis guideline; LR+ 2.8, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
Conditional add-on status: Conditional add-ons active (active_addons_present) - 2 conditional physical exam add-ons activated from the selected validated intent, active modifiers, or source-specific trigger logic.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Focal, progressive, peritoneal, obstructive, or distended findings can change imaging urgency, surgical consultation, fluid strategy, and serial abdominal exam needs. (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Abdominal pain initial tests and localization pathway (REQ-abdominal-initial-tests) - Initial abdominal-pain diagnostic data: infection/anemia, renal/electrolyte status, hepatobiliary obstruction/injury, pancreatitis, urinary/stone/pyelonephritis, pregnancy/ectopic risk, inflammatory diarrhea, sepsis, or ischemia.; source AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
3. Management implication - Abdominal imaging pathway by location and risk (REQ-abdominal-imaging-pathway) - Imaging target: appendicitis, diverticulitis, cholecystitis/biliary obstruction, bowel obstruction/perforation, abscess, ischemia, renal colic, pelvic/ectopic disease, or low-risk non-imaging trajectory.; source ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Acute abdomen and surgical escalation cues (REQ-abdominal-acute-abdomen-red-flags) - High-risk abdominal process: peritonitis, obstruction, perforation, ischemia, significant GI bleed, sepsis, or high-risk host with subtle signs.; source AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
5. Management implication - Pregnancy, vascular, and extra-abdominal danger cues (REQ-abdominal-pregnancy-vascular-mimic-red-flags) - Dangerous mimic or adjacent source: ectopic pregnancy, AAA/dissection/mesenteric ischemia, torsion, ACS/PE/lower-lobe pneumonia, cholangitis, obstructed infected stone, or metabolic crisis.; source AAFP_ACUTE_ABD_PAIN_2023; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; ACR_RLQ_PAIN_2022; ACR_RUQ_PAIN_2022; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
6. Management implication - A positive peritoneal sign can change imaging urgency, surgical consultation, NPO/analgesia planning, antibiotics, disposition, and need for serial abdominal exams. (EXAM-081-rebound-tenderness) - Peritoneal irritation or acute abdomen: pain worse on release, guarding pattern, or worsening localized tenderness.; source MCGEE_EBPD; SM25; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Management implication - A positive Murphy sign can change ultrasound threshold, antibiotics/surgical consultation, NPO planning, analgesia strategy, and urgency of reassessment. (EXAM-080-murphy-sign) - Biliary or gallbladder inflammation: inspiratory arrest or focal RUQ tenderness during gallbladder palpation.; source MCGEE_EBPD; SM25; WSES acute calculous cholecystitis guideline; LR+ 2.8, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.

Interpretation caution - Inspect abdomen (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - Abdominal pattern: focal tenderness, guarding, rebound/peritoneal irritation, distension, altered bowel sounds, mass, or ascites clue.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### pelvic_menstrual_pain_v1: Pelvic, menstrual, or lower-abdominal pain

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: pelvic_menstrual_pain

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Verify pregnancy possibility (REQ-pelvic-pregnancy-safety) - Pregnancy possibility changes medication, imaging, ectopic-risk, and escalation decisions in pelvic or lower-abdominal pain.; options Not possible / Possible / Positive test / Negative test / Unknown; use when Menstrual, pelvic, or lower-abdominal pain; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags pelvic_pain; pregnancy; ectopic; diagnostic_safety; source ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACOG_ENDOMETRIOSIS_DIAGNOSIS_2026; CDC_STI_2021; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Measure temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACOG_ENDOMETRIOSIS_DIAGNOSIS_2026; CDC_STI_2021; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask GU/STI symptoms and exposure risk - Pregnancy-related emergency, ectopic-pattern symptoms, PID features, and bleeding severity.; options Pregnancy not possible / Pregnancy possible / Missed period / Positive pregnancy test / Severe unilateral pain / Syncope / Shoulder pain / Fever / Purulent discharge / Dyspareunia / Heavy bleeding / Other ___; ask when Ask when pelvic, menstrual, or lower-abdominal pain is selected, especially with pregnancy possibility, discharge, fever, severe pain, or bleeding.; tags pelvic_pain; pregnancy; ectopic; PID; gynecology; history_question; pelvic_menstrual_pain; ACOG_ENDOMETRIOSIS_DIAGNOSIS_2026; CDC_STI_2021; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACOG_ENDOMETRIOSIS_DIAGNOSIS_2026; CDC_STI_2021; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; full question Could pregnancy be possible, and are there ectopic or PID danger features: missed period, positive pregnancy test, severe unilateral pelvic pain, syncope, shoulder pain, fever, purulent discharge, dyspareunia, or heavy bleeding?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
2. Ask DKA/HHS infectious, ischemic, and medication triggers - Lower-abdominal tenderness pattern, guarding, distension, or bowel abnormality that separates uncomplicated cramps from acute abdomen/GU/pelvic pathology.; options Typical cramps / Focal pain / Urinary symptoms / GI symptoms / Heavy bleeding / Pregnancy possible / Fever or discharge / Other ___; ask when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; inspect_abdomen; inspection; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; full question Where is the pain, is it typical for your period, and are there urinary, GI, bleeding, pregnancy, fever, or discharge symptoms?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
3. Ask pregnancy and ectopic danger features - Pregnancy or ectopic-risk context in pelvic, menstrual, or lower-abdominal pain.; options Not possible / Possible / Late period / Positive test / Heavy bleeding / One-sided severe pain / Syncope / Shoulder pain / Other ___; ask when Menstrual, pelvic, or lower-abdominal pain; tags pelvic_pain; pregnancy; ectopic; diagnostic_safety; source ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Could you be pregnant, is the period late or abnormal, and is there severe one-sided pain, shoulder pain, syncope, or heavy bleeding?; details Ask specifically about Could you be pregnant. | Ask specifically about is the period late. | Ask specifically about abnormal. | Ask specifically about is there severe one-sided pain. | Ask specifically about shoulder pain. | Ask specifically about syncope. | Ask specifically about heavy bleeding.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect abdomen (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - Localizes lower-abdominal tenderness, distension, guarding, or bowel pattern when menstrual cramps could mask pelvic, GI, or GU pathology.; options Flat / Distended / Scar / Lesion / Asymmetric; use when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; technique With the patient supine and the abdomen exposed appropriately, inspect contour, distension, scars, visible peristalsis, pulsations, lesions, and symmetry before palpation.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; inspect_abdomen; inspection; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate bowel sounds (EXAM-074-bowel-sounds) - Localizes lower-abdominal tenderness, distension, guarding, or bowel pattern when menstrual cramps could mask pelvic, GI, or GU pathology.; options Present / Hypoactive / Hyperactive / Absent; use when Abdominal pain; obstruction/ileus concern; technique Auscultate before percussion/palpation with diaphragm in one quadrant at least 10 seconds; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; auscultate_abdomen; auscultation; core_exam; obstruction_ileus_concern; Abdominal pain; obstruction/ileus concern; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Palpate abdomen (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - Localizes lower-abdominal tenderness, distension, guarding, or bowel pattern when menstrual cramps could mask pelvic, GI, or GU pathology.; options Nontender / Tender ___ / Guarding / Mass ___; use when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; technique If pain present, start away from painful area; palpate lightly and deeply in all 4 quadrants; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Use gentle technique; stop if severe pain, guarding, instability, or patient preference limits exam.; tags abdominal_pain; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; light_and_deep_palpation; palpation; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Focal tenderness, guarding, distension, or worsening findings can change urgency, imaging/lab needs, pelvic evaluation, or ED referral. (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - Lower-abdominal tenderness pattern, guarding, distension, or bowel abnormality that separates uncomplicated cramps from acute abdomen/GU/pelvic pathology.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Pelvic pain initial tests and imaging pathway (REQ-pelvic-initial-tests) - Pelvic source: pregnancy/ectopic, PID/STI, UTI, torsion/adnexal pathology, appendicitis mimic, or systemic infection.; source CDC_STI_2021; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; ACOG_ENDOMETRIOSIS_DIAGNOSIS_2026; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
3. Management implication - PID/ectopic red-flag review (REQ-pelvic-pid-ectopic-red-flags) - Pelvic danger pattern: ectopic pregnancy, ruptured ectopic, ovarian torsion, PID/tubo-ovarian abscess, appendicitis mimic, sepsis, or hemorrhage.; source CDC_STI_2021; ACOG_ECTOPIC_FAQ; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Measure temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask UTI symptoms and complicated-infection risk - host/exposure risk - GU/renal source and severity: cystitis, pyelonephritis/systemic UTI, obstructed infected stone, renal colic, AKI/hypovolemia, catheter/procedure-associated infection, pregnancy, or resistant pathogen risk.; options Lower urinary symptoms only / Fever-chills / Flank pain / Nausea-vomiting / Hematuria / Low urine output / Stone history / Pregnancy possible / Catheter-procedure / Immunocompromised / Prior resistant organism / Other ___; ask when GU, AKI, hypovolemia, or flank pain; tags dysuria; UTI; pyelonephritis; AKI; renal_colic; pregnancy; history_question; source IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Are symptoms limited to dysuria/frequency/urgency, or are there fever/chills, flank pain, nausea/vomiting, hematuria, low urine output, stone history, pregnancy possibility, catheter/procedure, immunocompromise, or prior resistant urine culture?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
2. Ask UTI symptoms and complicated-infection risk - infection precipitant - GU/renal source and severity: lower UTI, pyelonephritis, obstructing stone, AKI/hypovolemia, systemic infection, or urine-output change.; options None / Dysuria / Frequency-urgency / Hematuria / Flank pain / Fever-rigors / Nausea-vomiting / Decreased urine / Poor intake / Stone history / Other ___; ask when Ask when dysuria, flank pain, pyelonephritis, AKI, hypovolemia, oliguria, hematuria, or renal colic is selected.; tags dysuria; UTI; pyelonephritis; flank_pain; AKI; hypovolemia; history_question; gu_renal; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; full question Any dysuria, frequency/urgency, hematuria, flank pain, fever or rigors, nausea/vomiting, decreased urine, poor intake, stone history, or kidney disease?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
3. Review hyperglycemic-crisis treatments already started - Complicated UTI, obstructing stone, renal vulnerability, resistant infection risk, and inability to tolerate outpatient therapy.; options None / Pregnancy / Catheter-instrumentation / Retention / Known stone / Solitary-transplant kidney / Immunocompromise / Resistant organism / Recent antibiotics / Cannot keep fluids-meds down / Other ___; ask when Ask when dysuria, pyelonephritis, flank pain, hematuria, AKI, renal colic, or hypovolemia is selected.; tags dysuria; UTI; pyelonephritis; renal_colic; obstruction; AKI; pregnancy; history_question; gu_renal; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE; full question Any pregnancy, catheter or urinary instrumentation, urinary retention, known stone, solitary or transplant kidney, immunocompromise, resistant organism history, recent antibiotics, or inability to keep fluids or medicines down?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Percuss costovertebral angle tenderness (EXAM-084-cva-tenderness) - Looks for pyelonephritis or renal colic when flank or urinary symptoms are present.; options Absent / Right / Left / Bilateral; use when Pyelonephritis; flank pain; AKI; renal colic; technique Place palm at costovertebral angle; strike dorsal surface of hand with ulnar side of fist bilaterally; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags AKI; hypovolemia; infection; DKA_HHS; abdominal_pain; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; flank_pain; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; advanced_maneuvers; aki; cva; cva_tenderness; functional_status; msk_exam; pain; pyelonephritis; renal_colic; Pyelonephritis; flank pain; renal colic; source PubMed study of CVA tenderness in suspected ureteral stone; NCBI acute pyelonephritis review; LR+ 1.3, LR- 0.7; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Inspect oral mucosa (EXAM-060-mouth-exam) - Checks dehydration and bedside perfusion in GU/renal illness without turning uncomplicated urinary symptoms into a heart-failure exam.; options Normal / Dry mucosa / Lesions / Poor dentition / Bleeding; use when HEENT; dehydration; infection; oral symptoms; technique Use tongue blade and light; inspect gingivae, dentition, and subglossal area; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Use a clean light and tongue blade when needed; stop if gagging, trismus, airway concern, severe pain, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags AKI; hypovolemia; infection; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; heent; inpatient_bedside; thyroid_disease; cranial_nerve_exam; dehydration; heent_exam; inspect_mouth; mouth; oral_symptoms; pneumonia; sepsis; stroke; HEENT; oral symptoms; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Palpate radial pulses (EXAM-100-radial-pulses) - Checks dehydration and bedside perfusion in GU/renal illness without turning uncomplicated urinary symptoms into a heart-failure exam.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; use when Vascular exam; shock; limb symptoms; technique Palpate radial pulses bilaterally and symmetrically; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags AKI; hypovolemia; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypoglycemia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; hemodynamics; limb_symptoms; peripheral_pulses; radial_pulses; upper_extremity; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; Vascular exam; limb symptoms; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate abdomen (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - Adds suprapubic or abdominal source assessment when urinary, flank, hematuria, nausea/vomiting, or abdominal symptoms coexist.; options Nontender / Tender ___ / Guarding / Mass ___; use when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; technique If pain present, start away from painful area; palpate lightly and deeply in all 4 quadrants; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Use gentle technique; stop if severe pain, guarding, instability, or patient preference limits exam.; tags DKA_HHS; abdominal_pain; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; light_and_deep_palpation; palpation; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Renal tenderness or flank findings can support urine testing, imaging consideration, antimicrobial review, obstruction concern, or serial reassessment. (EXAM-084-cva-tenderness) - renal colic or pyelonephritis-associated renal tenderness; source PubMed study of CVA tenderness in suspected ureteral stone; NCBI acute pyelonephritis review; LR+ 1.3, LR- 0.7; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Dehydration or poor perfusion changes oral/IV fluid strategy, renal-function monitoring, sepsis escalation, medication safety, and disposition. (EXAM-060-mouth-exam) - Volume/perfusion clue: dry mucosa, poor intake/dehydration, weak pulses, shock physiology, or systemic infection physiology.; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Abdominal or suprapubic findings can change urinalysis/culture priority, imaging threshold, obstruction/retention concern, antibiotics, and escalation. (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - GU/abdominal source clue: suprapubic tenderness, abdominal tenderness, retention concern, or alternate abdominal process.; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Urine and renal-function tests (REQ-gu-renal-urine-renal-tests) - Urine/renal diagnostic data: pyuria/bacteriuria/hematuria, culture/susceptibility, creatinine or electrolyte abnormality, pregnancy status, systemic infection, sepsis physiology, or medication-safety constraint.; source IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
5. Management implication - Renal obstruction and stone imaging pathway (REQ-gu-renal-imaging-pathway) - Imaging target: hydronephrosis/obstruction, ureteral stone, infected obstructed system, renal abscess, urinary retention, or alternate abdominal/pelvic pathology.; source NICE_RENAL_STONES; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; IDSA_CUTI_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
6. Management implication - Urosepsis and pyelonephritis escalation cues (REQ-gu-renal-urosepsis-red-flags) - Systemic urinary infection clue: fever/rigors, flank/CVA symptoms, sepsis physiology, altered mentation, vomiting, pregnancy, immunocompromise, catheter/procedure association, resistance risk, or renal dysfunction.; source IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
7. Management implication - Obstruction, stone, and AKI danger cues (REQ-gu-renal-obstruction-red-flags) - High-risk renal process: obstructed infected system, urinary retention, severe renal colic, solitary/transplant kidney risk, gross hematuria, worsening AKI, or dangerous electrolyte abnormality.; source NICE_RENAL_STONES; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; IDSA_CUTI_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Focused history questions
1. Ask GU/STI symptoms and exposure risk - host/exposure risk - Local GU/STI syndrome and complications: urethritis, cervicitis, PID, epididymitis, genital ulcer disease, pregnancy-related safety, resistant infection, or persistent/recurrent infection.; options None / Dysuria / Discharge / Genital ulcer-lesion / Pelvic pain / Testicular pain / Fever / Pregnancy possible / New partner-STI exposure / HIV-syphilis risk / Recent antibiotics / Persistent symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when penile, urethral, vaginal, or genital discharge; STI exposure; urethritis; cervicitis; genital ulcer; dysuria with STI risk; or STI treatment failure is selected.; tags genital_discharge; sti; urethritis; cervicitis; PID; epididymitis; genital_ulcer; history_question; genital_discharge_sti; CDC_STI_2021; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; CDC_STI_2021; MCGEE_EBPD; full question Any dysuria, urethral or vaginal discharge, genital ulcer or lesion, pelvic pain, testicular pain, fever, pregnancy possibility, new partners, STI exposure, HIV/syphilis risk, recent antibiotics, or persistent symptoms after treatment?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
2. Ask GU/STI symptoms and exposure risk - urinary/flank symptoms - Urethral discharge genital lesion local inflammation or testicular tenderness; options No / Dysuria / Discharge / Sores / Testicular pain / STI exposure / Fever / Other ___; ask when Penile discharge dysuria STI genital lesion or swelling context; tags genital_discharge; sti; gu; dysuria; infection; urethritis; genital_exam; Penile discharge dysuria STI genital lesion or swelling context; Penile or genital discharge dysuria STI lesion or genital pain; source CDC_STI_2021; full question Any dysuria urethral discharge genital sores testicular pain new partners STI exposure fever or pelvic/scrotal pain?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
3. Ask GU/STI symptoms and exposure risk - skin/line source - Tender enlarged or fluctuant inguinal nodes suggesting local infection STI or malignancy clue; options No / Groin lump / Genital ulcer / Painful lesion / Fever / STI exposure / Skin infection / Other ___; ask when Penile discharge genital lesion STI groin swelling or suspicious skin lesion context; tags inguinal_nodes; sti; genital_exam; genital_discharge; skin_lesion; lymphadenopathy; infection; Penile discharge genital lesion STI groin swelling or suspicious skin lesion context; Genital discharge STI genital lesion skin lesion or groin swelling; source CDC_STI_2021; full question Any groin lumps genital ulcers painful lesions fever STI exposure or lower limb skin infection?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. 2. Management implication - Torsion and acute scrotum escalation cues (REQ-acute-scrotum-red-flags) - Testicular torsion, compromised testicular perfusion, epididymo-orchitis with systemic illness, incarcerated hernia, trauma-related injury, or testicular mass.; source AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
3. Management implication - High-riding testis severe focal tenderness swelling or mass changes urology escalation ultrasound urgency analgesia and infection or torsion pathway decisions (EXAM-GU-SCROTAL-EXAM) - Acute scrotum clue from swelling high-riding testis focal testicular or epididymal tenderness mass or hernia concern; source AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Absent reflex supports urgent torsion pathway in context while present reflex does not safely exclude torsion (EXAM-GU-CREMASTERIC-REFLEX) - Torsion-associated absent ipsilateral cremasteric reflex; source EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; LR+ 2.9, LR- 0; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Acute scrotum torsion and infection pathway (REQ-acute-scrotum-torsion-tests) - Testicular torsion, epididymo-orchitis, incarcerated hernia, renal colic mimic, testicular mass, trauma, or referred abdominal/pelvic source.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
2. Interpretation caution - Torsion and acute scrotum escalation cues (REQ-acute-scrotum-red-flags) - Testicular torsion, compromised testicular perfusion, epididymo-orchitis with systemic illness, incarcerated hernia, trauma-related injury, or testicular mass.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
3. Interpretation caution - Inspect and palpate scrotum (EXAM-GU-SCROTAL-EXAM) - Acute scrotum clue from swelling high-riding testis focal testicular or epididymal tenderness mass or hernia concern; limitations Do not delay urgent urology evaluation when torsion concern is high; source AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Interpretation caution - Test cremasteric reflex (EXAM-GU-CREMASTERIC-REFLEX) - Torsion-associated absent ipsilateral cremasteric reflex; limitations Accuracy evidence is pediatric-heavy and a present reflex must not delay urgent torsion evaluation; source EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; LR+ 2.9, LR- 0; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
5. Interpretation caution - Acute scrotum torsion-timing and mimic history (HISTORY-acute_scrotal_pain_v1) - Acute scrotum source and time-sensitivity: torsion, epididymo-orchitis, hernia, trauma, renal colic or abdominal mimic, testicular mass, and testicular salvage timing.; limitations History may be limited by mental status, language, collateral availability, recall, and acuity; reconcile with chart, vitals, exam, and diagnostic testing.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
6. Interpretation caution - Acute scrotum infection (HISTORY-acute_scrotal_pain_v1-acute-scrotum-infection-trauma-safety) - Epididymitis or STI source, viral orchitis, trauma/hematoma risk, malignancy/mass concern, fertility risk, and disposition barrier.; limitations History may be limited by mental status, language, collateral availability, recall, and acuity; reconcile with chart, vitals, exam, and diagnostic testing.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AUA_ACUTE_SCROTUM; EAU_ACUTE_SCROTUM; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.

### fever_sepsis_v1: Fever, infection, or sepsis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: infection_sepsis
Installed module: Fever, infection, or sepsis (fever_infection_sepsis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (fever_measure_bp) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Blood pressure anchors hemodynamic safety and helps identify shock, hypertensive urgency, orthostasis, or medication-risk context before focused diagnosis-specific decisions.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags fever; sepsis; blood_pressure; safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (fever_measure_hr) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Heart rate anchors physiologic severity and can reveal adrenergic state, hypovolemia, arrhythmia, infection severity, medication effect, or decompensation.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags fever; sepsis; heart_rate; safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure respiratory rate (fever_measure_rr) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Respiratory rate and visible breathing effort are early bedside markers of respiratory failure, metabolic stress, sepsis physiology, or endocrine crisis severity.; options ___/min / Normal effort / Tachypneic/labored / Bradypneic/tiring / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; tags fever; respiratory_rate; pneumonia; safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation (fever_measure_spo2) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Oxygenation separates stable outpatient-style assessment from possible respiratory failure, pulmonary source, shock physiology, or need for urgent support.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen / Hypoxemic / Escalating oxygen need / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; tags fever; oxygenation; pneumonia; safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (fever_measure_temperature) - Fever, infection, or sepsis: Temperature confirms fever or hypothermia and helps frame infection, inflammatory illness, endocrine crisis, or treatment urgency.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when When this validated workup is selected and the bedside assessment is clinically safe to perform.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; tags fever; temperature; hypothermia; safety_check; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Document mental status (fever_assess_mental_status) - Sepsis severity or CNS/metabolic involvement: new altered mentation, unsafe cooperation, or deviation from baseline. This basic acuity check helps identify severity, airway/safety risk, escalation needs, and safe interpretation of the selected validated workup.; options Baseline / Confused / Somnolent / Agitated / Unable to assess; use when Perform in every fever or possible sepsis workup, especially when the patient appears ill or high risk.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Mental status is affected by baseline cognition, sedatives, intoxication, pain, language barriers, hypoxia, metabolic disease, and neurologic disease; interpret with glucose, oxygenation, vitals, labs, and collateral history.; tags fever; sepsis; mental_status; diagnostic_safety; safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Focused history questions
1. Ask fever timeline, measurement, and medication exposure - Establishes fever reliability, tempo, pretreatment, medication masking, and host-risk context.; options Unknown / Short duration / Persistent or recurrent / Antipyretic used / Antibiotic already used / Steroid or immunosuppression / Other ___; ask when Ask for every selected fever, infection, or sepsis workup before narrowing the source.; tags fever; timeline; medication; host_risk; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; full question How high was the fever, how was it measured, when did it start, and what antipyretics, antibiotics, steroids, or immunosuppressants have you taken?; details Document maximum temperature and how it was measured. | Clarify fever onset and trajectory. | Ask what antipyretics, antibiotics, steroids, or immunosuppressants were already taken.
2. Ask source-localizing infection symptoms - Localizes the most likely infectious source and identifies sepsis severity clues.; options No localizing symptoms / Cough, sputum, dyspnea, or pleuritic pain / Sore throat, ear, sinus, or dental symptoms / Dysuria, frequency, or flank pain / Abdominal pain, vomiting, or diarrhea / Rash, wound, or line concern / Headache, neck stiffness, or confusion / Hot joint or back pain / Low urine output, fainting, or rapid worsening; ask when Ask for every selected fever workup; this question determines which source-directed exam maneuvers and tests are active.; tags fever; infection; source_localizing_history; pneumonia; UTI; skin; meningitis; sepsis; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; full question What symptoms localize the fever source: cough, sputum, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, sore throat, ear or sinus pain, dental pain, dysuria, flank pain, abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea, rash, wound, line concern, headache, neck stiffness, hot joint, back pain, confusion, low urine output, fainting, or rapid worsening?; details Ask about respiratory source: cough, sputum, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, hypoxia, aspiration risk, and sick respiratory contacts. | Ask about HEENT or dental source: sore throat, ear or sinus pain, oral lesions, dental pain, muffled voice, drooling, or trouble swallowing. | Ask about urinary or flank source: dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, pregnancy possibility, and reduced urine output. | Ask about abdominal or GI source: abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, blood in stool, poor intake, or severe localized pain. | Ask about skin, wound, or line source: rash, cellulitis, abscess, ulcer, drainage, indwelling line/device, surgical site, or procedure-site tenderness. | Ask about CNS danger source: severe headache, neck stiffness, photophobia, confusion, seizure, petechiae, or purpura. | Ask about joint, bone, or spine source: hot swollen joint, focal bone pain, severe back pain, injection drug use, or inability to bear weight. | Ask about host and exposure risks: immunosuppression, pregnancy, recent hospitalization/procedure, travel, outdoor bites, animals, food/water, sick contacts, sexual exposure, injection drug use, and new medications.
3. Ask sepsis severity, hydration, and perfusion symptoms - Screens for sepsis physiology, dehydration, hypoperfusion, encephalopathy, and inability to maintain safe oral intake.; options None / Poor intake or cannot keep fluids down / Dehydration or very dry / Dizzy, fainting, or nearly fainting / Confusion or unusual sleepiness / Low urine output / Severe weakness / Rapidly worsening / Other ___; ask when Ask for every fever, infection, or sepsis workup because severity and hydration symptoms change acuity even before a source is confirmed.; tags fever; sepsis; dehydration; perfusion; mental_status; source_localizing_history; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; full question Any poor intake, inability to keep fluids down, dehydration, dizziness, fainting, confusion, unusual sleepiness, low urine output, severe weakness, or rapid worsening with the fever?; details Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about inability to keep fluids down. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about dizziness. | Ask specifically about fainting. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about unusual sleepiness. | Ask specifically about low urine output.
4. Ask respiratory infection-source symptoms - Screens for pneumonia, viral respiratory infection, aspiration, obstructive lung disease, and respiratory failure.; options None / Cough / Sputum / Shortness of breath / Pleuritic pain / Wheeze / Hypoxia or oxygen need / Aspiration risk / Sick respiratory contacts; ask when Ask for fever even when no source is obvious, because pneumonia and respiratory viral illness are common, management-changing sources.; tags fever; pneumonia; respiratory_source; hypoxia; history_question; source ATS_CAP_2025; full question Any cough, sputum, shortness of breath, pleuritic pain, wheeze, hypoxia, aspiration risk, or sick respiratory contacts?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
5. Ask host-risk and exposure history - Identifies host-risk, exposure, healthcare-associated, travel/vector, animal/food/water, STI, injection-related, and drug-fever contexts.; options None / Immunocompromised / Pregnancy possible / Hospitalization, procedure, line, or device / Travel, outdoor, tick, or mosquito exposure / Animal, food, or water exposure / Sick contacts / Sexual exposure or injection drug use / New medication; ask when Ask for undifferentiated fever and for any fever with unclear source, recurrent course, or high acuity.; tags fever; host_risk; exposure_history; immunocompromised; pregnancy; diagnostic_safety; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; full question Any immunosuppression, pregnancy possibility, recent hospitalization or procedure, indwelling line or device, travel, outdoor bite exposure, animal exposure, food or water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, or new medication?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
6. Ask urinary and flank infection-source symptoms - Distinguishes lower UTI, pyelonephritis, infected obstruction, catheter-associated infection, and renal source risk.; options None / Dysuria, frequency, or urgency / Flank pain / Hematuria / Catheter or procedure / Pregnancy possible / Prior resistant organism / Reduced urine output; ask when Ask when fever has urinary symptoms, flank pain, hematuria, catheter/procedure context, pregnancy possibility, or unclear source with urinary concern.; tags fever; urinary_source; pyelonephritis; flank_pain; history_question; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; full question Any dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, flank pain, catheter or recent urologic procedure, pregnancy possibility, prior resistant urine culture, nausea, vomiting, or reduced urine output?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Observe work of breathing (fever_observe_work_of_breathing) - Document tachypnea, labored breathing, accessory muscle use, speech limitation, or apparent respiratory distress.; options Normal / Tachypneic / Labored / Accessory muscle use / Unable; use when Perform in every fever workup because pneumonia, respiratory viral illness, shock, and hypoxemic respiratory failure can present with fever.; technique Observe the patient at rest before auscultation; note respiratory effort, accessory muscle use, ability to speak, posture, and whether oxygen support is being used.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Work of breathing may be subtle in older adults, immunocompromised patients, neuromuscular disease, obesity, sedation, or early respiratory failure; pair with respiratory rate and oxygenation.; tags fever; pneumonia; respiratory_status; sepsis; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Auscultate posterior lung fields (fever_auscultate_posterior_lungs) - Listen for focal crackles, wheeze, rhonchi, diminished sounds, or asymmetry suggesting pneumonia or another pulmonary source.; options Clear / Crackles / Wheezes / Rhonchi / Diminished / Asymmetric / Unable; use when Perform in every fever workup unless clinically unsafe; prioritize when cough, sputum, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, hypoxia, aspiration risk, or no clear source is present.; technique Auscultate symmetric posterior lung fields from apices to bases when feasible, comparing side to side; include lateral or anterior fields if positioning limits posterior exam.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Normal auscultation does not exclude pneumonia or early lower respiratory infection; body habitus, poor inspiratory effort, ambient noise, positioning, and prior lung disease can reduce reliability.; tags fever; pneumonia; pulmonary_exam; respiratory_source; source ATS_CAP_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect skin for infection source (fever_inspect_skin_source) - Look for rash, cellulitis, abscess, wound, drainage, ulcer, petechiae, purpura, line-site inflammation, bite, or rapidly spreading soft-tissue findings.; options No focal skin source / Rash / Cellulitis / Abscess / Wound or drainage / Line-site inflammation / Petechiae or purpura / Unable; use when Perform in every fever workup because skin, wounds, line sites, bites, ulcers, and soft-tissue infection are common visible sources.; technique Inspect exposed skin and any symptomatic, device, wound, foot, pressure, or procedure sites; ask permission before moving clothing or dressings and document areas not examined.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations A limited exposed-skin exam can miss sacral, groin, foot, device, wound, or pressure-site sources; document areas deferred and inspect higher-risk sites when history supports it.; tags fever; skin; wound; line_site; source_control; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect oropharynx (fever_inspect_oropharynx) - Look for pharyngeal erythema, exudate, tonsillar asymmetry, oral ulcers, dental source, thrush, mucosal lesions, or dehydration clues.; options Normal / Erythema / Exudate / Dental concern / Oral lesion / Dry mucosa / Unable; use when Perform in undifferentiated fever, sore throat, oral or dental symptoms, rash/mucosal concern, dehydration concern, or unclear source.; technique Use a light when available; inspect lips, oral mucosa, dentition, tongue, tonsils, posterior pharynx, and visible hydration clues while respecting patient tolerance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment light source if available, cooperation moderate; limitations Oropharyngeal findings are nonspecific and can miss deep neck, sinus, otologic, or dental sources; airway symptoms, trismus, drooling, or muffled voice need escalation beyond routine inspection.; tags fever; heent; oropharynx; mucosal; dehydration; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate radial pulses (fever_palpate_radial_pulses) - Assess strength, regularity, equality, and whether pulses feel thready or bounding in the setting of fever.; options Strong and equal / Weak / Bounding / Irregular / Asymmetric / Unable; use when Perform in fever or possible sepsis to add a rapid bedside perfusion clue, especially when the patient appears ill, weak, dizzy, hypotensive, or dehydrated.; technique Palpate both radial pulses at the wrist, comparing strength and regularity; pair with perfusion appearance and blood pressure rather than using pulses alone.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Pulse quality is subjective and can be affected by vascular disease, arrhythmia, temperature, anxiety, examiner skill, and cuff timing; use with BP, mental status, capillary refill or perfusion exam, and labs.; tags fever; sepsis; perfusion; shock; pulse; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Escalate unstable fever or suspected sepsis (fever_escalate_if_unstable) - These findings change fever from outpatient/source evaluation to urgent infection, resuscitation, respiratory support, and source-control workflow.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Fever, infection, or sepsis.; tags fever; sepsis; escalation; disposition; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Use outpatient follow-up only when low risk and safety net is clear (fever_outpatient_if_low_risk) - Low-risk fever management still needs explicit safety netting so serious infection, pneumonia, dehydration, and evolving focal sources are not missed.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Fever, infection, or sepsis.; tags fever; outpatient_safety_net; diagnostic_safety; disposition; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (fever_measure_bp_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags fever,sepsis,blood_pressure,safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (fever_measure_hr_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags fever,sepsis,heart_rate,safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure respiratory rate (fever_measure_rr_limitation) - Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; limitations Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.; tags fever,respiratory_rate,pneumonia,safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Interpretation caution - Measure oxygen saturation (fever_measure_spo2_limitation) - Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; limitations Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.; tags fever,oxygenation,pneumonia,safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Interpretation caution - Measure temperature (fever_measure_temperature_limitation) - Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; limitations Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.; tags fever,temperature,hypothermia,safety_check; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Interpretation caution - Document mental status (fever_assess_mental_status_limitation) - Mental status is affected by baseline cognition, sedatives, intoxication, pain, language barriers, hypoxia, metabolic disease, and neurologic disease; interpret with glucose, oxygenation, vitals, labs, and collateral history.; limitations Mental status is affected by baseline cognition, sedatives, intoxication, pain, language barriers, hypoxia, metabolic disease, and neurologic disease; interpret with glucose, oxygenation, vitals, labs, and collateral history.; tags fever,sepsis,mental_status,diagnostic_safety,safety_check; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Interpretation caution - Observe work of breathing (fever_observe_work_of_breathing_limitation) - Work of breathing may be subtle in older adults, immunocompromised patients, neuromuscular disease, obesity, sedation, or early respiratory failure; pair with respiratory rate and oxygenation.; limitations Work of breathing may be subtle in older adults, immunocompromised patients, neuromuscular disease, obesity, sedation, or early respiratory failure; pair with respiratory rate and oxygenation.; tags fever,pneumonia,respiratory_status,sepsis; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Interpretation caution - Auscultate posterior lung fields (fever_auscultate_posterior_lungs_limitation) - Normal auscultation does not exclude pneumonia or early lower respiratory infection; body habitus, poor inspiratory effort, ambient noise, positioning, and prior lung disease can reduce reliability.; limitations Normal auscultation does not exclude pneumonia or early lower respiratory infection; body habitus, poor inspiratory effort, ambient noise, positioning, and prior lung disease can reduce reliability.; tags fever,pneumonia,pulmonary_exam,respiratory_source; source ATS_CAP_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
9. Interpretation caution - Inspect skin for infection source (fever_inspect_skin_source_limitation) - A limited exposed-skin exam can miss sacral, groin, foot, device, wound, or pressure-site sources; document areas deferred and inspect higher-risk sites when history supports it.; limitations A limited exposed-skin exam can miss sacral, groin, foot, device, wound, or pressure-site sources; document areas deferred and inspect higher-risk sites when history supports it.; tags fever,skin,wound,line_site,source_control; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
10. Interpretation caution - Inspect oropharynx (fever_inspect_oropharynx_limitation) - Oropharyngeal findings are nonspecific and can miss deep neck, sinus, otologic, or dental sources; airway symptoms, trismus, drooling, or muffled voice need escalation beyond routine inspection.; limitations Oropharyngeal findings are nonspecific and can miss deep neck, sinus, otologic, or dental sources; airway symptoms, trismus, drooling, or muffled voice need escalation beyond routine inspection.; tags fever,heent,oropharynx,mucosal,dehydration; source MERCK_FEVER_ADULTS; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
11. Interpretation caution - Palpate radial pulses (fever_palpate_radial_pulses_limitation) - Pulse quality is subjective and can be affected by vascular disease, arrhythmia, temperature, anxiety, examiner skill, and cuff timing; use with BP, mental status, capillary refill or perfusion exam, and labs.; limitations Pulse quality is subjective and can be affected by vascular disease, arrhythmia, temperature, anxiety, examiner skill, and cuff timing; use with BP, mental status, capillary refill or perfusion exam, and labs.; tags fever,sepsis,perfusion,shock,pulse; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
