17. Interpretation caution - Sepsis or shock physiology (fever_sepsis_shock_physiology_limitation) - Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Fever, infection, or sepsis.; limitations Absence of this red flag does not exclude serious disease; interpret with trajectory, baseline risk, vital signs, exam findings, labs, and local escalation thresholds for Fever, infection, or sepsis.; tags fever,sepsis,shock,perfusion,red_flag; source SSC_SEPSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

### stroke_focal_neuro_v1: Stroke or focal neurologic deficit

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: neuro_red_flags

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

4. Measure oxygen saturation and support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Measure temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask vision-threatening red-eye symptoms - Focal neurologic localization and time-sensitive eligibility: cortical, brainstem, cranial nerve, motor/sensory, cerebellar, seizure, or hemorrhage-pattern clues.; options Last-known-well ___ / Face droop / Speech trouble / Weakness / Numbness / Vision loss / Diplopia / Ataxia / Seizure / Severe headache / Other ___; ask when Ask immediately when stroke, TIA, seizure with focal deficit, facial droop, aphasia, diplopia, ataxia, or focal weakness is selected.; tags stroke; focal_neurologic_deficit; weakness; vision_change; cranial_nerve_exam; history_question; neuro_red_flags; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; full question When was last known well, and what new focal symptoms occurred: face droop, speech trouble, arm/leg weakness, numbness, vision loss, diplopia, ataxia, seizure, or severe headache?; details Ask specifically about When was last known well. | Ask specifically about what new focal symptoms occurred: face droop. | Ask specifically about speech trouble. | Ask specifically about arm/leg weakness. | Ask specifically about numbness. | Ask specifically about vision loss. | Ask specifically about diplopia. | Ask specifically about ataxia.
2. Ask PE severity, VTE risk factors, and bleeding risk - Thrombolysis and thrombectomy eligibility context, hemorrhage risk, stroke mimic clues, seizure pathway, and glucose-related mimic risk.; options None / Anticoagulant / Recent surgery-bleeding / Prior intracranial hemorrhage / Seizure at onset / Hypoglycemia symptoms / Migraine pattern / Trauma / Rapidly resolving / Other ___; ask when Ask immediately when stroke, TIA, facial droop, aphasia, diplopia, ataxia, seizure with focal deficit, or focal weakness is selected.; tags stroke; focal_neurologic_deficit; anticoagulation; glucose; seizure; history_question; neuro_red_flags; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; JAMA_RCE; full question Any anticoagulant use, recent surgery or bleeding, prior intracranial hemorrhage, seizure at onset, hypoglycemia symptoms, migraine pattern, trauma, or rapidly resolving symptoms?; details Ask specifically about anticoagulant use. | Ask specifically about recent surgery. | Ask specifically about bleeding. | Ask specifically about prior intracranial hemorrhage. | Ask specifically about seizure at onset. | Ask specifically about hypoglycemia symptoms. | Ask specifically about migraine pattern. | Ask specifically about trauma.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test pronator drift (EXAM-018-pronator-drift) - Adds a high-yield upper motor neuron screen when facial weakness, postictal focal deficit, or stroke concern is present.; options Absent / Present right / Present left / Unable; use when Weakness; stroke; myelopathy; neuro exam; technique Ask the patient to hold both arms extended forward, palms up, eyes closed if safe, for at least 10 seconds; document downward drift, pronation, asymmetry, or inability to participate.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; weakness; motor; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pronator_drift; upper_extremity; upper_motor_neuron; Weakness; neuro exam; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test visual fields (EXAM-002-visual-fields) - Localizes visual pathway, ocular motor, vestibular, brainstem, or postictal findings when vision, diplopia, focal deficits, vertigo, or ataxia are present.; options Full / Deficit right / Deficit left / Deficit bilateral / Unable; use when Vision change; pituitary/suprasellar lesion; stroke; neuro exam; technique Examiner covers opposite eye; hands 1-2 feet from patient, equidistant; test 4 quadrants each eye with 1, 2, or 5 fingers; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; vision_change; cn_ii; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pituitary_suprasellar_lesion; visual_fields; Vision change; pituitary/suprasellar lesion; neuro exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test pupillary light response (EXAM-005-pupils) - Screens pupillary asymmetry or reactivity in headache, altered mental status, focal neurologic symptoms, seizure, or neuro-ophthalmic concern.; options Equal and reactive / Unequal / Sluggish / Nonreactive / Unable; use when Neuro exam; altered mental status; eye complaint; head trauma; technique Dim lights; shine light into each eye twice; observe direct and consensual response; do not do same eye twice; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; vision_change; altered_mental_status; cn_ii_iii; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; eye_complaint; head_trauma; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pupillary_light; Neuro exam; altered mental status; eye complaint; head trauma; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test extraocular movements (EXAM-003-extraocular-movements) - Localizes visual pathway, ocular motor, vestibular, brainstem, or postictal findings when vision, diplopia, focal deficits, vertigo, or ataxia are present.; options Intact / Limited / Diplopia / Nystagmus / Unable; use when Diplopia; eye complaint; brainstem/cavernous sinus concern; neuro exam; technique Move finger in large H at eye level; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Palpate gently; stop if marked pain, airway concern, skin breakdown, trauma concern, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; vision_change; brainstem_cavernous_sinus_concern; cn_iii_iv_vi; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; diplopia; extraocular_movements; eye_complaint; heent_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; Diplopia; eye complaint; brainstem/cavernous sinus concern; neuro exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect facial symmetry (EXAM-010-facial-symmetry) - Directly checks facial weakness and cranial nerve VII pattern.; options Symmetric / Droop right / Droop left / Unable; use when Facial weakness; stroke; cranial nerve exam; technique Observe the face at rest, then ask the patient to smile, raise eyebrows, close eyes tightly, and show teeth if able; compare upper and lower facial movement side to side.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; weakness; cn_vii; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; facial_weakness; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; show_teeth; Facial weakness; cranial nerve exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test eye closure strength (EXAM-009-eye-closure-strength) - Directly checks facial weakness and cranial nerve VII pattern.; options Symmetric / Weak right / Weak left / Unable; use when Facial weakness; stroke; cranial nerve exam; technique Try to open closed eyelids; observe wide eye opening; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; weakness; cn_vii; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; eye_closure_strength; facial_weakness; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; Facial weakness; cranial nerve exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Test finger-to-nose (EXAM-040-finger-to-nose) - Adds bed-safe cerebellar/coordination localization for focal neurologic or stroke presentations.; options Normal / Impaired right / Impaired left / Impaired both / Unable; use when Ataxia; cerebellar disease; intoxication; stroke; technique Extend target through all four quadrants; test both hands; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; weakness; ataxia; balance; cerebellar_disease; coordination_and_gait; falls; finger_to_nose; intoxication; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; upper_extremity; Ataxia; cerebellar disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test rapid alternating movements (EXAM-041-rapid-alternating-movements) - Adds bed-safe cerebellar/coordination localization for focal neurologic or stroke presentations.; options Normal / Slow right / Slow left / Impaired both / Unable; use when Ataxia; cerebellar disease; technique Test one hand at a time; at least 5 cycles each; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; weakness; ataxia; balance; cerebellar_disease; coordination_and_gait; falls; functional_status; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; rapid_alternating_movements; upper_extremity; Ataxia; cerebellar disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test heel-to-shin (EXAM-042-heel-to-shin) - Adds bed-safe cerebellar/coordination localization for focal neurologic or stroke presentations.; options Normal / Impaired right / Impaired left / Impaired both / Unable; use when Ataxia; cerebellar disease; technique Ask the patient to place one heel on the opposite knee and slide it down the shin to the ankle, then repeat on the other side; compare smoothness, dysmetria, and ability to complete safely.; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags stroke; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; weakness; ataxia; balance; cerebellar_disease; coordination_and_gait; falls; functional_status; heel_to_shin; lower_extremity; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; Ataxia; cerebellar disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test facial light touch (LEGACY-neuro_cranial_nerves_cn_v_facial_light_touch) - Adds trigeminal or jaw localization when facial sensory symptoms, jaw weakness, or broader cranial nerve concern exists.; options Symmetric / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent / Unable; use when Facial numbness; stroke; cranial nerve exam; technique Use soft cotton-tipped applicator on forehead V1, cheek V2, jaw V3 bilaterally and symmetrically; aim above eyebrow, cheek, and submandibular region; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment penlight; cotton-tipped applicator, cooperation low; limitations Use clean disposable stimulus; avoid skin breakdown or open wounds.; tags stroke; cn_v; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; facial_light_touch; facial_numbness; neuro; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; Facial numbness; cranial nerve exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Test facial sharp sensation (LEGACY-neuro_cranial_nerves_cn_v_facial_sharp_touch) - Adds trigeminal or jaw localization when facial sensory symptoms, jaw weakness, or broader cranial nerve concern exists.; options Symmetric / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent / Unable; use when Facial numbness; trigeminal concern; neuro exam; technique Break cotton-tipped applicator to sharp point; test forehead V1, cheek V2, jaw V3 bilaterally and symmetrically; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment cotton-tipped applicator, cooperation low; limitations Use clean disposable stimulus; avoid skin breakdown or open wounds.; tags stroke; cn_v; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; facial_numbness; facial_sharp_touch; neuro; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; trigeminal_concern; Facial numbness; trigeminal concern; neuro exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test masseter strength (LEGACY-neuro_cranial_nerves_cn_v_masseter_strength) - Adds trigeminal or jaw localization when facial sensory symptoms, jaw weakness, or broader cranial nerve concern exists.; options Symmetric / Weak right / Weak left / Unable; use when Cranial nerve exam; jaw weakness; technique Place fingers on masseter muscles; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags stroke; cn_v; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; jaw_weakness; masseter_strength; neuro; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; Cranial nerve exam; jaw weakness; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
Conditional add-on status: Conditional add-ons active (active_addons_present) - 6 conditional physical exam add-ons activated from the selected validated intent, active modifiers, or source-specific trigger logic.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - New focal neurologic findings can prompt urgent localization, stroke/neuro escalation, fall precautions, imaging review, or therapy planning. (EXAM-018-pronator-drift) - focal motor weakness or upper motor neuron pattern; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Stroke initial tests and timing pathway (REQ-stroke-tests-reference-thresholds) - Stroke mimic exclusion and acute ischemic/hemorrhagic stroke triage.; source AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
3. Management implication - Stroke alert and neurologic danger cues (REQ-stroke-red-flags) - Acute focal neurologic deficit, posterior circulation stroke, hemorrhage, seizure mimic with persistent deficit, or reperfusion-window presentation.; source AHA_ASA_STROKE_2019; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Test pronator drift (EXAM-018-pronator-drift) - focal motor weakness or upper motor neuron pattern; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Interpretation caution - Test visual fields (EXAM-002-visual-fields) - cranial nerve II/III/IV/VI deficit or neuro-ophthalmic localization; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Interpretation caution - Test pupillary light response (EXAM-005-pupils) - cranial nerve II/III/IV/VI deficit or neuro-ophthalmic localization; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### spine_cord_compression_v1: Back pain, incontinence, or cord compression red flags

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: spine_cord_compression

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Count respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation and support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask urinary and flank infection-source symptoms - Cord/cauda equina motor clue: leg weakness, asymmetric strength, foot drop, or motor level pattern.; options No / Leg weakness / Numbness / Saddle numbness / Urinary retention or incontinence / Fever / Cancer or trauma / Other ___; ask when Lower extremity weakness; myelopathy; radiculopathy; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; functional_status; hip_flexor_strength; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; radiculopathy; upper_motor_neuron; Lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; full question Any new leg weakness, numbness, saddle numbness, urinary retention/incontinence, severe back pain, fever, cancer history, or trauma?; details Ask specifically about new leg weakness. | Ask specifically about numbness. | Ask specifically about saddle numbness. | Ask specifically about urinary retention/incontinence. | Ask specifically about severe back pain. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about cancer history. | Ask specifically about trauma.
2. Ask urinary and renal-source symptoms - Cauda equina or cord compression clue: saddle sensory change, urinary retention/incontinence, bowel dysfunction, or progressive bilateral deficit.; options No / Saddle numbness / Urinary retention / Incontinence / Fecal incontinence / Bilateral sciatica / Progressive weakness / Other ___; ask when Spine, myelopathy, or cord-compression red flags; tags cord_compression; cauda_equina; saddle_sensation; source ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Any new numbness around the groin/saddle area, urinary retention, overflow incontinence, fecal incontinence, bilateral sciatica, or progressive leg weakness?; details Ask specifically about new numbness around the groin/saddle area. | Ask specifically about urinary retention. | Ask specifically about overflow incontinence. | Ask specifically about fecal incontinence. | Ask specifically about bilateral sciatica. | Ask specifically about progressive leg weakness.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test extremity light touch (EXAM-030-extremity-light-touch) - Checks sensory level, dorsal-column pattern, and radicular or cauda-equina symptoms.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Sensory level ___; use when Numbness; neuropathy; myelopathy; stroke; technique Use soft cotton-tipped applicator on arms mid-arm, hands/fingers, legs/thighs, feet/toes bilaterally on skin; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight; cotton-tipped applicator, cooperation low; limitations Use clean disposable stimulus; avoid skin breakdown or open wounds.; tags DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; extremities; light_touch_extremities; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; numbness; sensation; upper_motor_neuron; Numbness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test extremity pinprick (EXAM-031-extremity-pinprick) - Checks sensory level, dorsal-column pattern, and radicular or cauda-equina symptoms.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Sensory level ___; use when Numbness; neuropathy; myelopathy; technique Use broken cotton-tipped applicator sharp point on arms, hands, legs, feet bilaterally on skin; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment cotton-tipped applicator, cooperation low; limitations Use clean disposable stimulus; avoid skin breakdown or open wounds.; tags DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; extremities; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; numbness; sensation; sharp_touch_extremities; upper_motor_neuron; Numbness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test hip flexion strength (EXAM-024-hip-flexion-strength) - Localizes motor deficits when spinal cord, cauda equina, radiculopathy, or compressive neurologic disease is possible.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; use when Lower extremity weakness; myelopathy; radiculopathy; technique Push thigh down; test one leg at a time; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; functional_status; hip_flexor_strength; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; radiculopathy; upper_motor_neuron; Lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test knee extension strength (EXAM-025-knee-extension-strength) - Localizes motor deficits when spinal cord, cauda equina, radiculopathy, or compressive neurologic disease is possible.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; use when Lower extremity weakness; technique Stabilize thigh with one hand; push distal leg down; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; functional_status; knee_extensor_strength; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; Lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Test ankle dorsiflexion strength (EXAM-027-ankle-dorsiflexion-strength) - Localizes motor deficits when spinal cord, cauda equina, radiculopathy, or compressive neurologic disease is possible.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; use when Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; technique Stabilize proximal to ankle; test one ankle at a time; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ankle_dorsiflexion_strength; diabetes_foot; foot_drop; functional_status; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; vascular_screen; Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test ankle plantarflexion strength (EXAM-028-ankle-plantarflexion-strength) - Localizes motor deficits when spinal cord, cauda equina, radiculopathy, or compressive neurologic disease is possible.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; use when Lower extremity weakness; technique Stabilize proximal to ankle; test one ankle at a time; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ankle_plantarflexion_strength; diabetes_foot; functional_status; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; vascular_screen; Lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Test patellar reflex (EXAM-037-patellar-reflex) - Screens upper motor neuron, myelopathy, or radiculopathy signs when cord compression is possible.; options Absent / Diminished / Normal / Brisk / Clonus; use when Weakness; myelopathy; radiculopathy; technique Strike patellar tendon below patella; test bilaterally; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment reflex hammer or tongue blade, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; lower_extremity; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; patellar_reflex; radiculopathy; reflexes; upper_motor_neuron; Weakness; source Stanford Medicine 25 deep tendon reflex guide; Merck Manual reflex exam; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Test Achilles reflex (EXAM-038-achilles-reflex) - Screens upper motor neuron, myelopathy, or radiculopathy signs when cord compression is possible.; options Absent / Diminished / Normal / Brisk / Clonus; use when Weakness; myelopathy; neuropathy; technique Hold ball of foot; passively dorsiflex to 90 degrees; strike Achilles tendon; test bilaterally; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment reflex hammer or tongue blade, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; achilles_reflex; diabetes_foot; functional_status; lower_extremity; msk_exam; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; pain; reflexes; upper_motor_neuron; vascular_screen; Weakness; source Stanford Medicine 25 deep tendon reflex guide; Merck Manual reflex exam; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
9. Test Babinski sign (EXAM-039-babinski-sign) - Screens upper motor neuron, myelopathy, or radiculopathy signs when cord compression is possible.; options Downgoing / Upgoing right / Upgoing left / Upgoing both / Equivocal; use when Myelopathy; stroke; upper motor neuron concern; technique Use pointed end of reflex hammer; scrape plantar foot from heel up lateral aspect and across ball toward big toe; test bilaterally; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment reflex hammer or tongue blade, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; diabetes_foot; functional_status; lower_extremity; msk_exam; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; plantar_reflex_babinski_sign; reflexes; upper_motor_neuron; upper_motor_neuron_concern; vascular_screen; Myelopathy; upper motor neuron concern; source Stanford Medicine 25 deep tendon reflex guide; Merck Manual reflex exam; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
10. Test saddle sensation (REQ-cord-saddle-sensation-screen) - Back pain with urinary, bowel, saddle, or progressive neurologic symptoms needs a visible cauda-equina safety screen.; options Normal by history / Decreased perineal sensation / Deferred after consent discussion / Unable; use when Spine, myelopathy, or cord-compression red flags; technique Ask about numbness in the saddle/perineal area first; perform a consented, privacy-protected perineal sensory exam only when clinically appropriate and document deferred or unable-to-assess status.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags cord_compression; cauda_equina; saddle_sensation; source ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Observe toe walking (EXAM-044-toe-walking) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Intact / Impaired / Unable; use when Radiculopathy; weakness; gait exam; technique Only if safe, ask the patient to walk several steps on the toes with support nearby; compare plantarflexion strength and asymmetry and stop for pain, imbalance, or fall risk.; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Guard closely; avoid unsupported testing when fall risk, severe weakness, or unsafe standing.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; coordination_and_gait; falls; gait; gait_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; radiculopathy; toe_walking; Radiculopathy; gait exam; source JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Observe heel walking (EXAM-045-heel-walking) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Intact / Impaired / Unable; use when Foot drop; radiculopathy; gait exam; technique Only if safe, ask the patient to walk several steps on the heels with support nearby; compare dorsiflexion strength and asymmetry and stop for pain, imbalance, or fall risk.; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Guard closely; avoid unsupported testing when fall risk, severe weakness, or unsafe standing.; tags DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; coordination_and_gait; diabetes_foot; falls; foot_drop; functional_status; gait; gait_exam; heel_walking; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; radiculopathy; vascular_screen; Foot drop; gait exam; source JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe tandem gait (EXAM-046-tandem-gait) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Intact / Unsteady / Unable / Deferred for safety; use when Ataxia; falls; intoxication; neuro exam; technique Walk with patient for safety; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Guard closely; avoid unsupported testing when fall risk, severe weakness, or unsafe standing.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; coordination_and_gait; diabetes_foot; falls; functional_status; gait; heel_to_toe_tandem_gait; intoxication; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; vascular_screen; Ataxia; neuro exam; source JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test rapid alternating movements (EXAM-041-rapid-alternating-movements) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Normal / Slow right / Slow left / Impaired both / Unable; use when Ataxia; cerebellar disease; technique Test one hand at a time; at least 5 cycles each; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; cerebellar_disease; coordination_and_gait; falls; functional_status; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; rapid_alternating_movements; upper_extremity; Ataxia; cerebellar disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Test finger-to-nose (EXAM-040-finger-to-nose) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Normal / Impaired right / Impaired left / Impaired both / Unable; use when Ataxia; cerebellar disease; intoxication; stroke; technique Extend target through all four quadrants; test both hands; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; cerebellar_disease; coordination_and_gait; falls; finger_to_nose; intoxication; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; upper_extremity; Ataxia; cerebellar disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test heel-to-shin (EXAM-042-heel-to-shin) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Normal / Impaired right / Impaired left / Impaired both / Unable; use when Ataxia; cerebellar disease; technique Ask the patient to place one heel on the opposite knee and slide it down the shin to the ankle, then repeat on the other side; compare smoothness, dysmetria, and ability to complete safely.; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; cerebellar_disease; coordination_and_gait; falls; functional_status; heel_to_shin; lower_extremity; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; Ataxia; cerebellar disease; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Observe gait (EXAM-043-gait) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Steady / Unsteady / Antalgic / Unable; use when Falls; weakness; dizziness; neuro/MSK exam; technique Only if safe, observe standing, gait initiation, stride, base, arm swing, turning, and need for assistance; document unable-to-walk or fall-risk limitations instead of forcing the maneuver.; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Guard closely; avoid unsupported testing when fall risk, severe weakness, or unsafe standing.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; coordination_and_gait; dizziness; falls; functional_status; gait; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neuro_msk_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; Falls; neuro/MSK exam; source Stanford Medicine 25 gait abnormalities guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Test Romberg (EXAM-047-romberg) - Assesses functional impairment and fall risk when it is safe for the patient to stand.; options Negative / Positive / Deferred for safety; use when Sensory ataxia; neuropathy; balance concern; technique Apply contact guard near patient; observe at least 5 seconds; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Guard closely; avoid unsupported testing when fall risk, severe weakness, or unsafe standing.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ataxia; balance; balance_concern; coordination_and_gait; falls; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; romberg; sensory_ataxia; Sensory ataxia; balance concern; source Stanford Medicine 25 cerebellar exam guide; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
Conditional add-on status: Conditional add-ons active (active_addons_present) - 8 conditional physical exam add-ons activated from the selected validated intent, active modifiers, or source-specific trigger logic.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - A sensory level or saddle-distribution complaint changes urgency of MRI/spine evaluation and bladder/retention workup. (EXAM-030-extremity-light-touch) - Cord/cauda equina sensory clue: sensory level, asymmetric numbness, saddle-area symptoms, or dorsal-column impairment.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - New or progressive weakness can change urgency of MRI/spine consultation, steroids when appropriate, fall precautions, and bladder/retention assessment. (EXAM-024-hip-flexion-strength) - Cord/cauda equina motor clue: leg weakness, asymmetric strength, foot drop, or motor level pattern.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - UMN signs or reflex asymmetry can change spinal imaging urgency, neurology/neurosurgery escalation, and fall precautions. (EXAM-037-patellar-reflex) - Upper motor neuron or radicular clue: upgoing plantar response, hyperreflexia, clonus, or reflex asymmetry.; source Stanford Medicine 25 deep tendon reflex guide; Merck Manual reflex exam; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Abnormal saddle or bladder/bowel findings change urgent MRI, spine/neurosurgical consultation, ED transfer, and documentation of consent/deferral. (REQ-cord-saddle-sensation-screen) - Cauda equina or cord compression clue: saddle sensory change, urinary retention/incontinence, bowel dysfunction, or progressive bilateral deficit.; source ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Management implication - Cord compression imaging and bladder pathway (REQ-cord-imaging-tests) - Compressive neurologic emergency, urinary retention, spinal infection, malignancy, or fracture.; source ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
6. Management implication - Cord compression and serious back-pain red flags (REQ-cord-red-flags) - Cauda equina, cord compression, epidural abscess, spinal malignancy, or fracture red flag.; source ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
7. Management implication - Unsafe gait changes fall precautions, therapy planning, imaging urgency, and disposition. (EXAM-044-toe-walking) - Functional cord/radicular clue: impaired gait, foot drop during heel walking, or inability to safely ambulate.; source JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Test extremity light touch (EXAM-030-extremity-light-touch) - Cord/cauda equina sensory clue: sensory level, asymmetric numbness, saddle-area symptoms, or dorsal-column impairment.; limitations Use clean disposable stimulus; avoid skin breakdown or open wounds.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

### hypoglycemia_jittery_v1: Hypoglycemia-like, jittery, or adrenergic symptoms

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: adrenergic_jittery

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Document mental status (REQ-hypoglycemia-mental-status) - Mental status separates adrenergic symptoms from neuroglycopenic hypoglycemia that needs immediate treatment and monitoring.; options Baseline / Anxious-jittery / Confused / Somnolent / Seizing / Unable; use when Adrenergic, jittery, tremulous, or hypoglycemia-like symptoms; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags hypoglycemia; mental_status; diagnostic_safety; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure bedside glucose (REQ-hypoglycemia-bedside-glucose) - Point-of-care glucose is immediately management-changing in hypoglycemia-like presentations.; options ___ mg/dL / <70 mg/dL / <54 mg/dL clinically significant / Normal / High / Not available; use when Adrenergic, jittery, tremulous, or hypoglycemia-like symptoms; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment glucometer or recent point-of-care value, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags hypoglycemia; glucose; diagnostic_safety; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. 4. 5. 6. Measure oxygen saturation and support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask neuroglycopenic severity and carbohydrate response - polyuria/thirst details - Neuroglycopenia severity: confusion, somnolence, seizure, unsafe self-treatment, or inability to take oral carbohydrates.; options No / Confusion / Sleepy / Seizure / Cannot take PO / Improved after carbs / Other ___; ask when Adrenergic, jittery, tremulous, or hypoglycemia-like symptoms; tags hypoglycemia; mental_status; diagnostic_safety; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Any confusion, sleepiness, seizure, trouble thinking, inability to eat/drink safely, or symptoms improving after carbohydrates?; details Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about sleepiness. | Ask specifically about seizure. | Ask specifically about trouble thinking. | Ask specifically about inability to eat/drink safely. | Ask specifically about symptoms improving after carbohydrates.
2. Ask neuroglycopenic severity and carbohydrate response - labs and severity - Current glucose value and severity category in a patient with adrenergic or neuroglycopenic symptoms.; options Value ___ / Insulin-secretagogue / Missed meal / Alcohol / Exercise / Improved after carbs / Other ___; ask when Adrenergic, jittery, tremulous, or hypoglycemia-like symptoms; tags hypoglycemia; glucose; diagnostic_safety; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question What was the most recent glucose, when were insulin or secretagogues taken, when was the last meal, and did symptoms improve after carbohydrates?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect diaphoresis (REQ-hypoglycemia-diaphoresis-inspection) - Visible diaphoresis is an atomic adrenergic sign and should not be hidden inside a broad endocrine exam.; options Absent / Present / Clammy / Unable; use when Adrenergic, jittery, tremulous, or hypoglycemia-like symptoms; technique Inspect exposed skin, forehead, and palms for visible sweating or clamminess while noting ambient temperature, fever, anxiety, pain, and recent treatment context.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags hypoglycemia; diaphoresis; adrenergic; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Observe tremor (REQ-hypoglycemia-tremor-observation) - Tremor observation captures adrenergic physiology while keeping thyroid-specific tremor assessment conditional on thyroid features.; options Absent / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Unable; use when Adrenergic, jittery, tremulous, or hypoglycemia-like symptoms; technique Observe at rest, then ask the patient to extend both arms with fingers spread if able; document fine versus coarse tremor, asymmetry, functional impact, and inability to participate.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags hypoglycemia; tremor; adrenergic; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Diaphoresis with low or unknown glucose supports immediate glucose check/treatment and reassessment after carbohydrates. (REQ-hypoglycemia-diaphoresis-inspection) - Adrenergic hypoglycemia clue: diaphoresis, clamminess, or visible autonomic activation.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Tremor with low or unknown glucose supports immediate glucose check/treatment, medication review, and symptom reassessment. (REQ-hypoglycemia-tremor-observation) - Adrenergic hypoglycemia clue: visible tremor or shakiness during symptoms.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Hypoglycemia confirmation and cause pathway (REQ-hypoglycemia-diagnostic-pathway) - True hypoglycemia, treatment response, medication-related hypoglycemia, critical illness, adrenal/pregnancy risk, or endogenous hyperinsulinism pattern.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Hypoglycemia danger and disposition cues (REQ-hypoglycemia-red-flags) - Severe, persistent, recurrent, medication-related, pregnancy-related, or unsafe hypoglycemia pattern.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Document mental status (REQ-hypoglycemia-mental-status) - Neuroglycopenia severity: confusion, somnolence, seizure, unsafe self-treatment, or inability to take oral carbohydrates.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
