### thyroid_crisis_v1: Thyroid storm or myxedema coma concern

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: thyroid_endocrine

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure temperature (SAFETY-thyroid-crisis-temperature) - Temperature is required in suspected thyroid storm or myxedema coma because fever or hypothermia changes crisis severity and escalation.; options ___ C/F / Hyperthermia / Hypothermia / Not available; use when Thyroid storm or myxedema coma concern; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags thyroid_storm; myxedema; temperature; vitals; diagnostic_safety; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Document mental status (REQ-thyroid-crisis-mental-status) - Mental status is a severity criterion in thyroid storm and myxedema coma and should be active safety data.; options Baseline / Agitated / Delirious / Somnolent / Comatose / Unable; use when Thyroid storm or myxedema coma concern; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags thyroid_storm; myxedema; mental_status; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Count respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
6. Measure oxygen saturation and support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask pregnancy, postpartum, and fertility-related thyroid safety - Thyroid emergency precipitant, medication exposure, pregnancy/postpartum context, beta-blocker or iodine safety, and adrenal-risk context.; options None / Antithyroid med change / Levothyroxine change / Nonadherence / Iodine-amiodarone exposure / Infection / Surgery-trauma / Pregnancy-postpartum / Beta-blocker use / Steroid use / Adrenal insufficiency concern / Other ___; ask when Ask when thyroid storm, thyrotoxic crisis, myxedema coma, or severe thyroid dysfunction with altered mental status, temperature abnormality, or cardiopulmonary instability is selected.; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_storm; myxedema; medication_safety; precipitant; history_question; thyroid_endocrine; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; full question Any antithyroid medication or levothyroxine change or nonadherence, iodine or amiodarone exposure, infection, recent surgery or trauma, pregnancy or postpartum state, beta-blocker use, steroid use, or adrenal insufficiency concern?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
2. Ask thyroid nodule, pain, and compressive symptoms - Thyroid/neck clue: goiter, tenderness, nodularity, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive finding.; options No / Neck swelling / Neck pain / Hoarseness / Dysphagia / Iodine exposure / Medication change / Other ___; ask when Thyroid storm or myxedema coma concern; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_exam; thyroid_storm; myxedema; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; SM25; full question Any neck swelling, pain, hoarseness, dysphagia, recent iodine exposure, antithyroid medication change, or thyroid hormone change?; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about pain. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about dysphagia. | Ask specifically about recent iodine exposure. | Ask specifically about antithyroid medication change. | Ask specifically about thyroid hormone change.
3. Ask hyperthyroid adrenergic, weight, and GI symptoms - Thyroid phenotype clue from warm moist skin in thyrotoxicosis or dry coarse skin in severe hypothyroidism; options No / Heat intolerance / Sweating / Cold intolerance / Dry or coarse skin / Other ___; ask when Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism heat intolerance cold intolerance diaphoresis or dry skin context; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_storm; myxedema; thyroid_phenotype; heat_intolerance; cold_intolerance; diaphoresis; dry_skin; Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism heat intolerance cold intolerance diaphoresis or dry skin context; Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism or thyroid phenotype concern; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; full question Any heat intolerance or sweating or cold intolerance or dry/coarse skin change?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - Links neck and cardiovascular findings to thyroid severity and escalation.; options Not elevated / Elevated / Unable to assess; use when Heart failure; volume overload; shock; technique Stand on patient's right; observe jugular venous pulsations; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypovolemia; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; diuresis; hemodynamics; jvp; neck; peripheral_pulses; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; volume_status; Heart failure; volume overload; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - Links neck and cardiovascular findings to thyroid severity and escalation.; options Normal / Murmur / Rub / Gallop / Other ___; use when Cardiac exam; chest pain; dyspnea; murmur; technique Use diaphragm and bell over aortic, pulmonic, tricuspid, and mitral areas; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; auscultate_heart_with_diaphragm_and_bell; auscultation; cardiac_auscultation; cardiac_exam; chest_pain; dyspnea; hypoxia; murmur; pulmonary_exam; respiratory_status; valvular_disease; Cardiac exam; chest pain; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - Links neck and cardiovascular findings to thyroid severity and escalation.; options None / Trace / 1+ / 2+ / 3+; use when Heart failure; renal disease; liver disease; edema; technique Apply firm pressure on lower tibia bony surface for at least 5 seconds bilaterally; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags AKI; DKA_HHS; abdominal_pain; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; flank_pain; heart_failure; hypercalcemia; hypovolemia; infection; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; aki; biliary_disease; cholecystitis; diuresis; edema; liver_disease; lower_extremity; peripheral_pulses; pyelonephritis; renal_colic; renal_disease; ruq_pain; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; volume_status; Heart failure; renal disease; liver disease; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect and palpate thyroid (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Directly documents goiter, tenderness, nodularity, or neck mass findings in suspected thyroid emergency.; options Normal / Enlarged / Nodule / Tender / Asymmetric / Unable; use when Thyroid storm or myxedema coma concern; technique Inspect the neck at rest and while swallowing, then palpate the isthmus and both thyroid lobes while the patient swallows; document enlargement, nodules, tenderness, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive concern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_exam; thyroid_storm; myxedema; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect skin for thyroid phenotype (EXAM-ENDO-THYROID-SKIN-PHENOTYPE) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; options Normal / Warm moist skin / Dry coarse skin / Unable; use when Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism heat intolerance cold intolerance diaphoresis or dry skin context; technique Inspect exposed skin for warm moist thyrotoxic phenotype or dry coarse myxedematous phenotype; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Nonspecific supportive phenotype only and must not delay thyroid crisis treatment or source evaluation; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_storm; myxedema; thyroid_phenotype; heat_intolerance; cold_intolerance; diaphoresis; dry_skin; Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism heat intolerance cold intolerance diaphoresis or dry skin context; Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism or thyroid phenotype concern; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context. (EXAM-098-jvp) - elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Management implication - New rhythm, murmur, displaced impulse, or extra sound can change telemetry, ECG/echo need, volume strategy, or escalation. (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - murmur, abnormal heart sound, rhythm irregularity, or S3/S4 clue; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Goiter, tenderness, or nodules can change Graves/toxic nodular/subacute thyroiditis framing, imaging/uptake discussion, airway concern, and endocrine escalation. (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Thyroid/neck clue: goiter, tenderness, nodularity, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive finding.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Thyroid crisis diagnostic and safety tests (REQ-thyroid-crisis-tests) - Thyroid hormone excess/deficiency severity, arrhythmia, electrolyte/glucose disturbance, renal/hepatic dysfunction, infection precipitant, or adrenal risk.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
5. Management implication - Thyroid storm or myxedema escalation cues (REQ-thyroid-crisis-red-flags) - Thyroid emergency with neurologic, thermoregulatory, cardiovascular, respiratory, electrolyte, or precipitant danger.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
6. Management implication - Marked warm moist or dry coarse skin phenotype supports thyroid severity framing and changes thyroid lab urgency crisis screen temperature strategy and endocrine escalation (EXAM-ENDO-THYROID-SKIN-PHENOTYPE) - Thyroid phenotype clue from warm moist skin in thyrotoxicosis or dry coarse skin in severe hypothyroidism; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Inspect jugular venous pressure (EXAM-098-jvp) - elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; source Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension; LR+ 2.47, LR- 0.42; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
2. Interpretation caution - Auscultate heart sounds (EXAM-108-heart-sounds) - murmur, abnormal heart sound, rhythm irregularity, or S3/S4 clue; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; source JAMA Rational Clinical Examination heart failure review; Stanford Medicine 25 cardiac second sounds; LR+ 11, LR- n/a; LR note: Quantitative likelihood-ratio values are available in the curated metadata; interpret with the cited source, pretest probability, and patient context.
3. Interpretation caution - Inspect and press lower extremity edema (EXAM-104-lower-extremity-edema) - peripheral edema pattern and volume overload clue; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

### routine_thyroid_disease_v1: Routine thyroid disease evaluation

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: routine_thyroid

Focused history questions
1. Ask thyroid nodule, pain, and compressive symptoms - Structural thyroid symptoms: goiter/nodule growth, thyroiditis pain, recurrent laryngeal nerve clue, dysphagia, or airway/compressive symptom.; options None / Neck swelling / Rapid growth / Thyroid pain / Hoarseness / Dysphagia / Positional dyspnea / Other ___; ask when Ask when routine thyroid disease, abnormal TSH, hyperthyroidism, hypothyroidism, goiter, nodule, thyroid mass, or thyroid cancer concern is selected.; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_exam; thyroid_nodule; compressive_symptoms; history_question; routine_thyroid; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; full question Any neck swelling, rapid growth, thyroid pain, hoarseness, dysphagia, or positional shortness of breath?; details Ask specifically about neck swelling. | Ask specifically about rapid growth. | Ask specifically about thyroid pain. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about dysphagia. | Ask specifically about positional shortness of breath.
2. Ask thyroid cancer radiation, biopsy, and MEN2 risk - Thyroid malignancy risk context: radiation exposure, inherited medullary thyroid cancer risk, suspicious prior cytology, or aggressive growth.; options None / Childhood head-neck radiation / Family thyroid cancer / MEN2 / Suspicious prior biopsy / Rapidly enlarging mass / Other ___; ask when Ask when a thyroid nodule, goiter, neck mass, suspicious nodes, hoarseness, compressive symptoms, or thyroid cancer concern is selected.; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_nodule; thyroid_cancer; radiation; MEN2; history_question; routine_thyroid; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; full question Any childhood head or neck radiation, family thyroid cancer, MEN2, prior suspicious thyroid biopsy, or rapidly enlarging neck mass?; details Ask specifically about childhood head. | Ask specifically about neck radiation. | Ask specifically about family thyroid cancer. | Ask specifically about MEN2. | Ask specifically about prior suspicious thyroid biopsy. | Ask specifically about rapidly enlarging neck mass.
3. Review thyroid medications, iodine/biotin exposure, and assay interference - Medication or assay context that can cause thyroid dysfunction, distort thyroid tests, or make treatment adjustment unsafe.; options None / Thyroid hormone / Antithyroid drug / Amiodarone / Lithium / Iodine or contrast / High-dose biotin / Thyroid supplement / Missed dose / Recent dose change / Other ___; ask when Ask when thyroid lab interpretation, abnormal TSH, hyperthyroidism, hypothyroidism, goiter, thyroiditis, or thyroid medication adjustment is being considered.; tags thyroid_disease; medication_safety; assay_interference; biotin; amiodarone; lithium; history_question; routine_thyroid; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; full question Any thyroid hormone, antithyroid drug, amiodarone, lithium, iodine/contrast exposure, high-dose biotin, thyroid supplement, missed dose, or recent dose change?; details Ask specifically about thyroid hormone. | Ask specifically about antithyroid drug. | Ask specifically about amiodarone. | Ask specifically about lithium. | Ask specifically about iodine/contrast exposure. | Ask specifically about high-dose biotin. | Ask specifically about thyroid supplement. | Ask specifically about missed dose.
4. Ask pregnancy, postpartum, and fertility-related thyroid safety - Reproductive context that changes TSH targets, medication safety, antibody interpretation, and postpartum thyroiditis likelihood.; options None / Pregnant / Pregnancy plans / Recent postpartum / Breastfeeding / Infertility treatment / Fertility goal / Other ___; ask when Ask when thyroid disease is evaluated in a patient for whom pregnancy, postpartum state, fertility goals, or medication safety could change targets or treatment choices.; tags thyroid_disease; pregnancy; postpartum; fertility; medication_safety; history_question; routine_thyroid; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; full question Any current pregnancy, pregnancy plans, recent postpartum state, breastfeeding, infertility treatment, or fertility goal affected by thyroid status?; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect and palpate thyroid (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Directly evaluates goiter, nodules, tenderness, asymmetry, and compressive neck findings in thyroid disease.; options Normal / Enlarged / Nodule / Tender / Asymmetric; use when Thyroid disease; neck mass; endocrine exam; technique Inspect from front while swallowing; palpate from behind below cricoid cartilage over both lobes while swallowing; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Palpate gently; stop if marked pain, airway concern, skin breakdown, trauma concern, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags thyroid_disease; graves; hashimoto; heent; hyperthyroidism; hypothyroidism; malignancy; neck_mass; thyroid_cancer; thyroid_nodule; endocrine; endocrine_exam; heent_exam; lymph_nodes_and_thyroid; thyroid; thyroid_inspection_and_palpation; Thyroid disease; neck mass; endocrine exam; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect skin for thyroid phenotype (EXAM-ENDO-THYROID-SKIN-PHENOTYPE) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; options Normal / Warm moist skin / Dry coarse skin / Unable; use when Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism heat intolerance cold intolerance diaphoresis or dry skin context; technique Inspect exposed skin for warm moist thyrotoxic phenotype or dry coarse myxedematous phenotype; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Nonspecific supportive phenotype only and must not delay thyroid crisis treatment or source evaluation; tags thyroid_disease; thyroid_storm; myxedema; thyroid_phenotype; heat_intolerance; cold_intolerance; diaphoresis; dry_skin; Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism heat intolerance cold intolerance diaphoresis or dry skin context; Thyroid storm myxedema coma hyperthyroidism hypothyroidism or thyroid phenotype concern; routine_thyroid; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Nodule, tenderness, asymmetry, goiter, or compressive findings can change ultrasound, antibody/uptake workup, FNA pathway, or airway/ENT urgency. (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Thyroid/neck finding: diffuse goiter, nodularity, tenderness, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive clue.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Routine thyroid diagnostic tests and thresholds (REQ-routine-thyroid-tests) - Primary or central hypo/hyperthyroidism, Graves disease, autoimmune thyroiditis, thyroid nodule/cancer risk, or compressive structural disease.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
3. Management implication - Thyroid escalation and structural red flags (REQ-routine-thyroid-red-flags) - Thyroid emergency, pregnancy-critical thyroid disease, cardiac complication, compressive goiter, thyroid malignancy risk, or sight-threatening orbitopathy.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Marked warm moist or dry coarse skin phenotype supports thyroid severity framing and changes thyroid lab urgency crisis screen temperature strategy and endocrine escalation (EXAM-ENDO-THYROID-SKIN-PHENOTYPE) - Thyroid phenotype clue from warm moist skin in thyrotoxicosis or dry coarse skin in severe hypothyroidism; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Inspect and palpate thyroid (EXAM-071-thyroid-exam) - Thyroid/neck finding: diffuse goiter, nodularity, tenderness, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive clue.; limitations Palpate gently; stop if marked pain, airway concern, skin breakdown, trauma concern, or patient preference limits the exam.; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Interpretation caution - Routine thyroid diagnostic tests and thresholds (REQ-routine-thyroid-tests) - Primary or central hypo/hyperthyroidism, Graves disease, autoimmune thyroiditis, thyroid nodule/cancer risk, or compressive structural disease.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Evidence / likelihood-ratio metadata
1. Focused history questions
1. Ask wound infection and limb-threat features - skin/line source - Diabetes foot risk: ulcer/infection, neuropathy, poor protective sensation, falls, vascular risk, footwear mismatch, or self-care barrier.; options None / Ulcer-wound / Drainage / Redness / New pain / Numbness-tingling / Falls / Prior amputation / Footwear issue / Cannot inspect feet / Other ___; ask when Ask when diabetes foot, neuropathy, wound, ulcer, discharge-risk, protective sensation, or perfusion assessment is selected.; tags diabetes_foot; neuropathy; wound; protective_sensation; vascular_exam; history_question; diabetes_foot_neuropathy; ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; full question Any foot ulcer, wound, drainage, redness, new pain, numbness/tingling, loss of protective sensation, falls, prior amputation, poor footwear, or inability to inspect feet?; details Ask about rash, cellulitis, abscess, ulcer, drainage, pain out of proportion, rapidly spreading redness, indwelling line/device, surgical site, and procedure-site tenderness. | Ask whether skin or line findings require culture, source control, antibiotic route/breadth, or urgent escalation.
2. Ask wound infection and limb-threat features - skin/line source 2 - Foot infection, limb ischemia, ulcer severity, offloading feasibility, footwear risk, and discharge safety.; options None / Spreading redness / Drainage-odor / Fever / New ischemic pain / Rest pain / Black tissue / Loss of pulses / Cannot offload / Unsafe footwear / Lacks supplies-follow-up / Other ___; ask when Ask when diabetes foot, neuropathy, ulcer, wound, nonhealing lesion, protective-sensation loss, vascular concern, or discharge-risk planning is selected.; tags diabetes_foot; wound; infection; ischemia; offloading; vascular_exam; history_question; diabetes_foot_neuropathy; ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; full question Any spreading redness, drainage or odor, fever, new ischemic pain, rest pain, black tissue, loss of pulses, inability to offload, unsafe footwear, or lack of wound-care supplies or follow-up?; details Ask about rash, cellulitis, abscess, ulcer, drainage, pain out of proportion, rapidly spreading redness, indwelling line/device, surgical site, and procedure-site tenderness. | Ask whether skin or line findings require culture, source control, antibiotic route/breadth, or urgent escalation.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test vibration sense (EXAM-032-vibration-sense) - Checks sensory neuropathy and protective sensation risk.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Unable; use when Neuropathy; B12 deficiency; dorsal column disease; diabetes; technique Use 128 Hz tuning fork; demonstrate on wrist; test IP joint of big toe; move proximal to lateral malleolus if not sensed; test bilaterally; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment 128 Hz tuning fork, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags inpatient_diabetes; weakness; DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; b12_deficiency; diabetes; diabetes_foot; dorsal_column_disease; functional_status; great_toes; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; pain; sensation; vascular_screen; vibration; Neuropathy; B12 deficiency; dorsal column disease; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test extremity light touch (EXAM-030-extremity-light-touch) - Checks sensory neuropathy and protective sensation risk.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Sensory level ___; use when Numbness; neuropathy; myelopathy; stroke; technique Use soft cotton-tipped applicator on arms mid-arm, hands/fingers, legs/thighs, feet/toes bilaterally on skin; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight; cotton-tipped applicator, cooperation low; limitations Use clean disposable stimulus; avoid skin breakdown or open wounds.; tags inpatient_diabetes; weakness; DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; extremities; light_touch_extremities; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; numbness; sensation; upper_motor_neuron; Numbness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test extremity pinprick (EXAM-031-extremity-pinprick) - Checks sensory neuropathy and protective sensation risk.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Sensory level ___; use when Numbness; neuropathy; myelopathy; technique Use broken cotton-tipped applicator sharp point on arms, hands, legs, feet bilaterally on skin; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment cotton-tipped applicator, cooperation low; limitations Use clean disposable stimulus; avoid skin breakdown or open wounds.; tags inpatient_diabetes; weakness; DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; extremities; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; numbness; sensation; sharp_touch_extremities; upper_motor_neuron; Numbness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test great toe proprioception (EXAM-033-great-toe-proprioception) - Checks sensory neuropathy and protective sensation risk.; options Intact / Diminished / Absent / Unable; use when Neuropathy; dorsal column disease; ataxia; technique Hold big toe by sides; support proximal joint; move slightly up or down; at least 3 movements each great toe; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags inpatient_diabetes; weakness; DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; ataxia; balance; dorsal_column_disease; falls; functional_status; great_toes; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; neuropathy; pain; proprioception; sensation; Neuropathy; dorsal column disease; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Observe heel walking (EXAM-045-heel-walking) - Adds functional foot/ankle assessment when neuropathy, foot drop, or discharge safety matters.; options Intact / Impaired / Unable; use when Foot drop; radiculopathy; gait exam; technique Only if safe, ask the patient to walk several steps on the heels with support nearby; compare dorsiflexion strength and asymmetry and stop for pain, imbalance, or fall risk.; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Guard closely; avoid unsupported testing when fall risk, severe weakness, or unsafe standing.; tags inpatient_diabetes; weakness; DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; ataxia; balance; coordination_and_gait; diabetes_foot; falls; foot_drop; functional_status; gait; gait_exam; heel_walking; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; radiculopathy; vascular_screen; Foot drop; gait exam; source JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Palpate posterior tibial pulses (EXAM-102-posterior-tibial-pulses) - Assesses pedal perfusion relevant to foot ulcer, wound healing, and vascular risk.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; use when Diabetes foot exam; vascular disease; edema; technique Palpate posterior tibial pulses on skin bilaterally on medial side going lateral; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags inpatient_diabetes; AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypoglycemia; hypovolemia; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; diabetes_foot; diabetes_foot_exam; diuresis; edema; functional_status; lower_extremity; msk_exam; pain; peripheral_pulses; posterior_tibial_pulses; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; vascular_screen; volume_status; Diabetes foot exam; vascular disease; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Palpate dorsalis pedis pulses (EXAM-103-dorsalis-pedis-pulses) - Assesses pedal perfusion relevant to foot ulcer, wound healing, and vascular risk.; options Equal / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent; use when Diabetes foot exam; vascular disease; edema; technique Palpate dorsalis pedis pulses bilaterally on skin; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags inpatient_diabetes; AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; cardiopulmonary; diagnostic_safety; heart_failure; hypoglycemia; hypovolemia; inpatient_bedside; perfusion; shock; volume_overload; diabetes_foot; diabetes_foot_exam; diuresis; dorsalis_pedis_pulses; edema; functional_status; lower_extremity; msk_exam; pain; peripheral_pulses; vascular_disease; vascular_exam; vascular_screen; volume_status; Diabetes foot exam; vascular disease; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
8. Test ankle dorsiflexion strength (EXAM-027-ankle-dorsiflexion-strength) - Adds functional foot/ankle assessment when neuropathy, foot drop, or discharge safety matters.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; use when Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; technique Stabilize proximal to ankle; test one ankle at a time; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags inpatient_diabetes; weakness; DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; ankle_dorsiflexion_strength; diabetes_foot; foot_drop; functional_status; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; vascular_screen; Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - New focal neurologic findings can prompt urgent localization, stroke/neuro escalation, fall precautions, imaging review, or therapy planning. (EXAM-032-vibration-sense) - sensory deficit, neuropathy, posterior column, or localization clue; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context. (EXAM-102-posterior-tibial-pulses) - perfusion deficit, vascular disease, or limb ischemia clue; source 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Diabetes foot and neuropathy tests (REQ-diabetes-foot-tests) - Loss of protective sensation, PAD, ulcer infection, osteomyelitis, Charcot arthropathy, deformity, footwear risk, or wound-healing risk.; source ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Diabetes foot urgent-risk cues (REQ-diabetes-foot-red-flags) - Limb-threatening infection, ischemia, osteomyelitis, Charcot neuroarthropathy, or unsafe discharge/self-care context.; source ADA_FOOT_CARE_2026; ADA_STANDARDS_HOSPITAL_2026; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

### rash_skin_v1: Rash, urticaria, wound, or skin lesion

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: dermatology

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure temperature (SAFETY-dermatology-temperature) - Temperature screens for infection, drug reaction, systemic inflammatory disease, or anaphylaxis mimic in dermatologic presentations.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Afebrile / Not available; use when Rash, wound, or dermatologic concern; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags dermatology; skin; rash; wound; vitals; diagnostic_safety; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-dermatology-blood-pressure) - Blood pressure keeps systemic illness, anaphylaxis, severe infection, and medication reaction risk separate from the skin morphology exam.; options ___ / Normal / Low / High / Not available; use when Rash, wound, or dermatologic concern; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags dermatology; skin; rash; wound; vitals; diagnostic_safety; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure heart rate (SAFETY-dermatology-heart-rate) - Heart rate helps distinguish uncomplicated skin findings from systemic allergic, infectious, painful, or toxic presentations.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; use when Rash, wound, or dermatologic concern; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags dermatology; skin; rash; wound; vitals; diagnostic_safety; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Count respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure oxygen saturation and support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask rash danger features and exposure history - host/exposure risk - Anaphylaxis, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, cellulitis or necrotizing infection, meningococcemia/purpura pattern, exposure-related rash, and host risk.; options None / Fever / Mucosal lesions / Facial-tongue swelling / Trouble breathing / Skin pain / Blistering / Purpura / Rapidly spreading redness / New medication / Tick-travel exposure / Immunocompromise / Other ___; ask when Ask when rash, hives, pruritus, skin lesion, cellulitis, wound, ulcer, or systemic skin concern is selected.; tags skin; rash; mucosal; anaphylaxis; infection; medication_safety; history_question; dermatology; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; full question Any fever, mucosal lesions, facial or tongue swelling, trouble breathing, skin pain, blistering, purpura, rapidly spreading redness, new medication, tick or travel exposure, or immunocompromise?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
2. Ask rash danger features and exposure history - host/exposure risk 2 - Morphology and distribution: urticaria, cellulitis, abscess, vesicles, purpura, mucosal disease, ulcer/wound, jaundice, infestation, or suspicious lesion.; options No / New medicine / Fever / Rapid spread / Skin pain / Mucosal sores / Swelling-wheeze / Drainage / Bite-travel / Immunosuppressed / Other ___; ask when Rash, wound, or dermatologic concern; tags skin; rash; skin_inspection; wound; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; full question Any new medicine, fever, rapid spread, skin pain, mucosal sores, facial/lip/tongue swelling, wheeze, wound drainage, travel/bite exposure, or immunosuppression?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
3. Ask GU/STI symptoms and exposure risk - Mucosal involvement suggesting severe drug eruption infection autoimmune disease or STI clue; options No / Mouth sores / Eye pain / Genital sores / Blisters / Skin pain / Fever / New drug / Other ___; ask when Rash hives pruritus STI drug reaction fever or mucosal symptom context; tags infection; mucosal; mucosal_lesion; skin; rash; hives; sti; genital_exam; red_flag; Rash hives pruritus STI drug reaction fever or mucosal symptom context; Rash hives drug reaction STI or systemic mucocutaneous concern; dermatology; drug_eruption; severe_rash; source AAFP_PRURITUS_2022; full question Any mouth sores eye pain genital sores blistering skin pain fever new drug or trouble breathing?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect skin (REQ-derm-skin-inspection) - Rash or wound workups require a real morphology/distribution inspection rather than a single generic skin label.; options Localized / Generalized / Urticarial / Vesicular / Purpuric / Mucosal involvement / Wound or ulcer / Cellulitic / Unable; use when Rash, wound, or dermatologic concern; technique Inspect exposed and symptomatic skin in good light; document morphology, distribution, color, blanching, warmth, tenderness, excoriations, drainage, and mucosal involvement when relevant.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags skin; rash; skin_inspection; wound; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
1. Inspect mucosa for lesions (EXAM-DERM-MUCOSAL-LESIONS) - Not promoted because this maneuver lacks a direct syndrome, organ-system, management, or feasibility match for the selected validated workup; use only after adding a specific patient trigger or secondary validated intent.; options Absent / Oral lesions / Ocular lesions / Genital lesions / Unable; use when Rash hives pruritus STI drug reaction fever or mucosal symptom context; technique Inspect clinically relevant oral ocular or genital mucosa when symptoms suggest mucocutaneous disease; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 1 min, equipment light source, cooperation moderate; limitations Scope exam to symptoms and use privacy safeguards for genital mucosa; tags infection; mucosal; mucosal_lesion; skin; rash; hives; sti; genital_exam; red_flag; Rash hives pruritus STI drug reaction fever or mucosal symptom context; Rash hives drug reaction STI or systemic mucocutaneous concern; dermatology; drug_eruption; severe_rash; source AAFP_PRURITUS_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
Conditional add-on status: Conditional add-ons active (active_addons_present) - 1 conditional physical exam add-on activated from the selected validated intent, active modifiers, or source-specific trigger logic.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Morphology, mucosal involvement, purpura, infection, ulceration, or rapid spread changes isolation, medication-stop decisions, antimicrobial/allergy treatment, biopsy/referral, and escalation. (REQ-derm-skin-inspection) - Morphology and distribution: urticaria, cellulitis, abscess, vesicles, purpura, mucosal disease, ulcer/wound, jaundice, infestation, or suspicious lesion.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Dermatology source-directed tests (REQ-derm-tests) - Benign rash, cellulitis/abscess, drug eruption, vasculitis/purpura, infestation/fungal/viral cause, nonhealing or malignant lesion, or systemic disease.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
3. Management implication - Dermatology danger cues (REQ-derm-red-flags) - Life-threatening rash, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, necrotizing infection, meningococcemia/vasculitis, anaphylaxis, immunocompromised infection, or skin cancer risk.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Mucosal lesions change urgency for severe cutaneous adverse reaction STI testing isolation ophthalmology dermatology or ED escalation (EXAM-DERM-MUCOSAL-LESIONS) - Mucosal involvement suggesting severe drug eruption infection autoimmune disease or STI clue; source AAFP_PRURITUS_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure temperature (SAFETY-dermatology-temperature) - Dermatology safety context: fever, hypothermia, or systemic illness accompanying rash, wound, hives, or skin lesion.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-dermatology-blood-pressure) - Dermatology safety context: hypotension, severe hypertension, or unstable hemodynamics.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Focused history questions
1. Ask vision-threatening red-eye symptoms - Vision-threatening or neurologic eye presentation: painful red eye, corneal/contact-lens risk, orbital/uveitic pattern, diplopia, headache, or focal neurologic clue.; options None / Vision loss / Eye pain / Photophobia / Trauma / Contact lens / Discharge / Severe headache / Diplopia / Neuro symptom / Immunocompromise / Other ___; ask when Ask when eye redness, discharge, eye pain, photophobia, diplopia, or vision change is selected.; tags eye_vision; vision_change; heent_exam; infection; history_question; AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JAMA_RCE; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; JAMA_RCE; full question Any vision loss, eye pain, photophobia, trauma, contact lens use, discharge, severe headache, diplopia, neurologic symptoms, or immunocompromise?; details Ask specifically about vision loss. | Ask specifically about eye pain. | Ask specifically about photophobia. | Ask specifically about trauma. | Ask specifically about contact lens use. | Ask specifically about discharge. | Ask specifically about severe headache. | Ask specifically about diplopia.
2. Ask ocular emergency and systemic red flags - Giant-cell arteritis clues, acute angle-closure pattern, orbital or neurologic emergency, herpes zoster ophthalmicus, traumatic or chemical injury, and severe ocular inflammation.; options None / Severe headache / Jaw claudication / Scalp tenderness / Neurologic deficit / Halos-nausea / Trauma / Chemical exposure / Shingles rash / Immunosuppression / Cannot keep eye open / Other ___; ask when Ask when eye redness, eye pain, photophobia, vision change, diplopia, headache with ocular symptoms, or contact-lens risk is selected.; tags eye_vision; red_eye; vision_change; neuro; heent_exam; red_flag; history_question; AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JAMA_RCE; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; JAMA_RCE; full question Any severe headache, jaw claudication, scalp tenderness, neurologic deficit, halos or nausea, trauma or chemical exposure, shingles rash, immunosuppression, or inability to keep the eye open?; details Ask specifically about severe headache. | Ask specifically about jaw claudication. | Ask specifically about scalp tenderness. | Ask specifically about neurologic deficit. | Ask specifically about halos. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about trauma. | Ask specifically about chemical exposure.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test visual fields (EXAM-002-visual-fields) - Focuses the bedside exam on visual function, pupils, eye movement, and visible ocular inflammation.; options Full / Deficit right / Deficit left / Deficit bilateral / Unable; use when Vision change; pituitary/suprasellar lesion; stroke; neuro exam; technique Examiner covers opposite eye; hands 1-2 feet from patient, equidistant; test 4 quadrants each eye with 1, 2, or 5 fingers; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; tags vision_change; diagnostic_safety; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; cn_ii; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pituitary_suprasellar_lesion; visual_fields; Vision change; pituitary/suprasellar lesion; neuro exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test extraocular movements (EXAM-003-extraocular-movements) - Focuses the bedside exam on visual function, pupils, eye movement, and visible ocular inflammation.; options Intact / Limited / Diplopia / Nystagmus / Unable; use when Diplopia; eye complaint; brainstem/cavernous sinus concern; neuro exam; technique Move finger in large H at eye level; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Palpate gently; stop if marked pain, airway concern, skin breakdown, trauma concern, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags vision_change; diagnostic_safety; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; brainstem_cavernous_sinus_concern; cn_iii_iv_vi; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; diplopia; extraocular_movements; eye_complaint; heent_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; Diplopia; eye complaint; brainstem/cavernous sinus concern; neuro exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test visual acuity (EXAM-001-visual-acuity) - Focuses the bedside exam on visual function, pupils, eye movement, and visible ocular inflammation.; options Baseline / Reduced right / Reduced left / Reduced both / Unable; use when Vision complaint; pituitary or optic pathway disease; stroke; neuro exam; technique Use pocket visual screening chart at listed distance; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment vision chart or near card, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags vision_change; diagnostic_safety; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; cn_ii; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pituitary_or_optic_pathway_disease; vision_complaint; visual_acuity; Vision complaint; pituitary or optic pathway disease; neuro exam; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Test pupillary light response (EXAM-005-pupils) - Focuses the bedside exam on visual function, pupils, eye movement, and visible ocular inflammation.; options Equal and reactive / Unequal / Sluggish / Nonreactive / Unable; use when Neuro exam; altered mental status; eye complaint; head trauma; technique Dim lights; shine light into each eye twice; observe direct and consensual response; do not do same eye twice; feasibility difficulty easy, time 2 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; tags vision_change; diagnostic_safety; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; altered_mental_status; cn_ii_iii; cranial_nerve_exam; cranial_nerves; eye_complaint; head_trauma; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pupillary_light; Neuro exam; altered mental status; eye complaint; head trauma; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect sclerae and conjunctivae (EXAM-054-sclerae-and-conjunctivae) - Focuses the bedside exam on visual function, pupils, eye movement, and visible ocular inflammation.; options Normal / Icteric / Pale / Injected / Discharge; use when HEENT; anemia; jaundice; eye symptoms; technique Inspect the neck at rest and while swallowing, then palpate the isthmus and both thyroid lobes while the patient swallows; document enlargement, nodules, tenderness, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive concern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; tags diagnostic_safety; heent; hypovolemia; infection; inpatient_bedside; thyroid_disease; anemia; cranial_nerve_exam; eye_symptoms; eyes; heent_exam; inspect_sclerae_and_conjunctivae; jaundice; stroke; HEENT; eye symptoms; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect fundus with ophthalmoscope (EXAM-055-ophthalmoscopic-exam) - Focuses the bedside exam on visual function, pupils, eye movement, and visible ocular inflammation.; options Red reflex present / Abnormal / Unable; use when Vision symptoms; diabetes; hypertension; neuro/HEENT exam; technique Dim lights; stand 1-2 feet away and about 15 degrees lateral; use right hand/right eye for right eye and left hand/left eye for left eye; observe red reflex and follow toward patient to 1-2 inches; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment penlight; reflex hammer or tongue blade; ophthalmoscope, cooperation moderate; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; tags DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; heent; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; cranial_nerve_exam; diabetes; diabetes_foot; eyes; functional_status; heent_exam; hypertension; msk_exam; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neuro_heent_exam; neurologic_localization; ophthalmoscope_red_reflex_fundus; pain; upper_motor_neuron; vascular_screen; vision_symptoms; Vision symptoms; neuro/HEENT exam; source JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - New focal neurologic findings can prompt urgent localization, stroke/neuro escalation, fall precautions, imaging review, or therapy planning. (EXAM-002-visual-fields) - cranial nerve II/III/IV/VI deficit or neuro-ophthalmic localization; source Merck Manual cranial nerve exam; Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context. (EXAM-054-sclerae-and-conjunctivae) - Screens anemia, jaundice, conjunctivitis; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Red eye and vision workup tests (REQ-eye-tests) - Conjunctivitis, keratitis/corneal abrasion, uveitis, acute angle closure glaucoma, orbital cellulitis, open globe, retinal/optic nerve disease, or neurologic vision loss.; source AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Eye and vision red flags (REQ-eye-red-flags) - Keratitis, uveitis, acute glaucoma, open globe, chemical injury, orbital cellulitis, optic/retinal disease, or neurologic emergency.; source AAO_CONJUNCTIVITIS_PPP_2023; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

### heent_throat_ear_v1: Sore throat, ear pain, nasal, or focused HEENT concern

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: heent_focused

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
