Measure temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask pain location, trauma, and inflammatory red flags - HEENT source and severity: pharyngeal/tonsillar, otologic, nasal/sinus, airway-adjacent, neck mass, systemic infection, or ocular involvement.; options Sore throat / Ear pain / Hearing change / Nasal congestion / Sinus pain / Hoarseness / Dysphagia / Drooling / Neck swelling / Fever / Eye symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when sore throat, ear pain, nasal/sinus symptoms, dysphagia, hoarseness, otalgia, epistaxis, or focused HEENT concern is selected.; tags heent; heent_exam; sore_throat; ear_pain; sinus; airway; history_question; heent_focused; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Which HEENT symptoms localize the problem: sore throat, ear pain, hearing change, nasal congestion, sinus pain, hoarseness, dysphagia, drooling, neck swelling, fever, or eye symptoms?; details Ask specifically about Which HEENT symptoms localize the problem: sore throat. | Ask specifically about ear pain. | Ask specifically about hearing change. | Ask specifically about nasal congestion. | Ask specifically about sinus pain. | Ask specifically about hoarseness. | Ask specifically about dysphagia. | Ask specifically about drooling.
2. Review hyperglycemic-crisis treatments already started - HEENT infection exposure, treatment failure, host risk, dental source, malignancy risk, deep-space infection clue, and hydration/disposition safety.; options None / Sick contacts / COVID-flu-strep exposure / Immunocompromised / Recurrent ear-sinus infection / Recent antibiotics / Dental infection / Tobacco-alcohol risk / Severe unilateral pain / Muffled voice-trismus / Cannot swallow fluids / Other ___; ask when Ask when sore throat, ear pain, sinus/nasal symptoms, dysphagia, hoarseness, otalgia, epistaxis, fever, or focused HEENT concern is selected.; tags heent; heent_exam; infection; sore_throat; ear_pain; sinus; airway; history_question; heent_focused; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Any sick contacts, recent COVID/flu/strep exposure, immunocompromise, recurrent ear or sinus infection, recent antibiotics, dental infection, tobacco or alcohol risk, severe unilateral pain, muffled voice, trismus, or inability to swallow fluids?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Use otoscope to inspect ears (EXAM-057-otoscope-exam) - Targets external ear, canal, and tympanic membrane findings when ear pain, hearing change, or otalgia is present.; options Normal / Cerumen / Erythema / Effusion / Abnormal TM / Unable; use when Ear pain; hearing loss; infection; technique Place clean speculum; pull pinna up and back; gently insert into canal center; observe tympanic membrane; repeat other ear; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment otoscope, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags ear_pain; diagnostic_safety; heent; inpatient_bedside; ear_canals_and_tms; ear_otoscope; ears; functional_status; hearing_loss; heent_exam; infection; msk_exam; pain; pneumonia; sepsis; Ear pain; hearing loss; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect external ears (EXAM-056-external-ears) - Targets external ear, canal, and tympanic membrane findings when ear pain, hearing change, or otalgia is present.; options Normal / Tender / Lesion / Discharge / Deformity; use when Ear symptoms; HEENT exam; technique Inspect external ears bilaterally; bend pinna to inspect behind each ear; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; heent; inpatient_bedside; ear_symptoms; ears; external_ears; heent_exam; inspect_external_ears; Ear symptoms; HEENT exam; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect nasal mucosa (EXAM-058-nasal-exam) - Targets nasal source or sinus tenderness when epistaxis, congestion, or sinus symptoms are present.; options Normal / Congestion / Rhinorrhea / Epistaxis / Lesion; use when Nasal symptoms; URI; epistaxis; technique Place clean speculum; stabilize head and thumb on nose tip; gently insert into naris; observe; repeat other naris; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment otoscope, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; heent; inpatient_bedside; epistaxis; heent_exam; nares; nasal_symptoms; nose; nose_otoscope; uri; Nasal symptoms; URI; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect oral mucosa (EXAM-060-mouth-exam) - Checks oral, pharyngeal, tonsillar, or airway-adjacent findings when throat, dysphagia, or airway symptoms are present.; options Normal / Dry mucosa / Lesions / Poor dentition / Bleeding; use when HEENT; dehydration; infection; oral symptoms; technique Use tongue blade and light; inspect gingivae, dentition, and subglossal area; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Use a clean light and tongue blade when needed; stop if gagging, trismus, airway concern, severe pain, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; heent; hypovolemia; infection; inpatient_bedside; thyroid_disease; cranial_nerve_exam; dehydration; heent_exam; inspect_mouth; mouth; oral_symptoms; pneumonia; sepsis; stroke; HEENT; oral symptoms; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate sinus tenderness (EXAM-059-sinus-tenderness) - Targets nasal source or sinus tenderness when epistaxis, congestion, or sinus symptoms are present.; options Absent / Frontal / Maxillary / Diffuse; use when Sinus symptoms; headache; URI; technique Palpate frontal sinuses above each eye and maxillary sinuses below each eye bilaterally; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate gently; stop if marked pain, airway concern, skin breakdown, trauma concern, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags diagnostic_safety; heent; inpatient_bedside; cranial_nerve_exam; frontal_and_maxillary_sinus_palpation; headache; heent_exam; nose; sinus_symptoms; sinuses; stroke; uri; Sinus symptoms; URI; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Palpate regional cervical lymph nodes (EXAM-065-tonsillar-nodes) - Adds regional nodes when infection, throat symptoms, or malignancy concern is present.; options Not enlarged / Enlarged / Tender; use when Throat infection; HEENT exam; technique Palpate angle of jaw bilaterally; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Use a clean light and tongue blade when needed; stop if gagging, trismus, airway concern, severe pain, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags diagnostic_safety; heent; infection; inpatient_bedside; malignancy; myxedema; thyroid_disease; thyroid_storm; endocrine; heent_exam; lymph_nodes; lymph_nodes_and_thyroid; pneumonia; sepsis; throat_infection; tonsillar_lymph_nodes; Throat infection; HEENT exam; source JTA_JES_THYROID_STORM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context. (EXAM-057-otoscope-exam) - Screens ear canal and tympanic membrane; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Focal musculoskeletal findings can change imaging threshold, immobilization, activity restriction, infection/inflammatory concern, specialist referral, or follow-up urgency. (EXAM-059-sinus-tenderness) - joint, soft-tissue, tenderness, range-of-motion, or functional limitation clue; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Management implication - Focused HEENT diagnostic tests (REQ-heent-tests) - Strep pharyngitis, viral respiratory infection, otitis/hearing loss, sinusitis complication, deep neck infection, mastoiditis, epistaxis severity, or malignancy/airway risk.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

### bleeding_anemia_v1: Bleeding, bruising, pallor, anemia, or GI bleed

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: gi_bleed_alarm; heme_bleeding_anemia

1. Focused history questions
1. Ask high-risk chest pain warning features - Bleeding/anemia source and severity: GI bleed, mucocutaneous bleeding, medication-related bleeding, hemodynamic symptoms, cardiopulmonary strain, or menstrual source.; options None / Hematemesis / Melena / Hematochezia / Bruising-petechiae / Gum-nose bleeding / Anticoagulant / Dizziness-syncope / Dyspnea / Chest pain / Heavy menses / Other ___; ask when Ask when bleeding, bruising, pallor, anemia, GI bleed, epistaxis, petechiae, or systemic fatigue/weakness with anemia concern is selected.; tags bleeding; anemia; gi_bleed; pallor; bruising; history_question; heme_bleeding_anemia; gi_bleed_alarm; ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; full question Any hematemesis, melena, hematochezia, easy bruising, petechiae, gum/nose bleeding, anticoagulant use, dizziness/syncope, dyspnea, chest pain, or heavy menses?; details Ask specifically about hematemesis. | Ask specifically about melena. | Ask specifically about hematochezia. | Ask specifically about easy bruising. | Ask specifically about petechiae. | Ask specifically about gum/nose bleeding. | Ask specifically about anticoagulant use. | Ask specifically about dizziness/syncope.
2. Ask PE severity, VTE risk factors, and bleeding risk - Medication-related bleeding, coagulopathy risk, hemodynamic/cardiopulmonary instability, pregnancy context, and transfusion or reversal planning risk.; options None / Anticoagulant-antiplatelet / Liver disease / Kidney disease / Bleeding disorder / Procedure-trauma / Syncope / Chest pain / Dyspnea at rest / Pregnancy / Prior transfusion reaction / Other ___; ask when Ask when bleeding, bruising, pallor, anemia, GI bleed, epistaxis, petechiae, or fatigue/weakness with anemia concern is selected.; tags bleeding; anemia; gi_bleed; anticoagulation; transfusion; red_flag; history_question; heme_bleeding_anemia; gi_bleed_alarm; ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; full question Any anticoagulant or antiplatelet use, liver disease, kidney disease, bleeding disorder, recent procedure or trauma, syncope, chest pain, dyspnea at rest, pregnancy, or prior transfusion reaction?; details Ask specifically about anticoagulant. | Ask specifically about antiplatelet use. | Ask specifically about liver disease. | Ask specifically about kidney disease. | Ask specifically about bleeding disorder. | Ask specifically about recent procedure. | Ask specifically about trauma. | Ask specifically about syncope.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect sclerae and conjunctivae (EXAM-054-sclerae-and-conjunctivae) - Checks pallor, mucosal bleeding, oral lesions, or oropharyngeal clues relevant to bleeding or dysphagia.; options Normal / Icteric / Pale / Injected / Discharge; use when HEENT; anemia; jaundice; eye symptoms; technique Inspect the neck at rest and while swallowing, then palpate the isthmus and both thyroid lobes while the patient swallows; document enlargement, nodules, tenderness, asymmetry, fixation, or compressive concern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Limit bright light if severe photophobia or ocular injury; interpret cautiously after eye drops or ocular procedures.; tags diagnostic_safety; heent; hypovolemia; infection; inpatient_bedside; thyroid_disease; anemia; cranial_nerve_exam; eye_symptoms; eyes; heent_exam; inspect_sclerae_and_conjunctivae; jaundice; stroke; HEENT; eye symptoms; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect oral mucosa (EXAM-060-mouth-exam) - Checks pallor, mucosal bleeding, oral lesions, or oropharyngeal clues relevant to bleeding or dysphagia.; options Normal / Dry mucosa / Lesions / Poor dentition / Bleeding; use when HEENT; dehydration; infection; oral symptoms; technique Use tongue blade and light; inspect gingivae, dentition, and subglossal area; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment penlight, cooperation low; limitations Use a clean light and tongue blade when needed; stop if gagging, trismus, airway concern, severe pain, or patient preference limits the exam.; tags AKI; DKA_HHS; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; heent; hypovolemia; infection; inpatient_bedside; thyroid_disease; cranial_nerve_exam; dehydration; heent_exam; inspect_mouth; mouth; oral_symptoms; pneumonia; sepsis; stroke; HEENT; oral symptoms; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect abdomen (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - Assesses tenderness, distension, and abdominal source clues in GI bleeding or alarm symptoms.; options Flat / Distended / Scar / Lesion / Asymmetric; use when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; technique With the patient supine and the abdomen exposed appropriately, inspect contour, distension, scars, visible peristalsis, pulsations, lesions, and symmetry before palpation.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags DKA_HHS; abdominal_pain; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; inspect_abdomen; inspection; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Auscultate bowel sounds (EXAM-074-bowel-sounds) - Assesses tenderness, distension, and abdominal source clues in GI bleeding or alarm symptoms.; options Present / Hypoactive / Hyperactive / Absent; use when Abdominal pain; obstruction/ileus concern; technique Auscultate before percussion/palpation with diaphragm in one quadrant at least 10 seconds; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Clean equipment before patient contact and interpret cautiously in noisy rooms.; tags DKA_HHS; abdominal_pain; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; auscultate_abdomen; auscultation; core_exam; obstruction_ileus_concern; Abdominal pain; obstruction/ileus concern; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Palpate abdomen (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - Assesses tenderness, distension, and abdominal source clues in GI bleeding or alarm symptoms.; options Nontender / Tender ___ / Guarding / Mass ___; use when Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; technique If pain present, start away from painful area; palpate lightly and deeply in all 4 quadrants; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Use gentle technique; stop if severe pain, guarding, instability, or patient preference limits exam.; tags DKA_HHS; abdominal_pain; adrenal_insufficiency; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; inpatient_bedside; abdomen; abdominal_exam; core_exam; gi_gu_exam; light_and_deep_palpation; palpation; Abdominal pain; GI/GU exam; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context. (EXAM-054-sclerae-and-conjunctivae) - Screens anemia, jaundice, conjunctivitis; source SM25; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Abnormal abdominal findings can change pain localization, obstruction/ileus concern, imaging threshold, surgical consultation, or need for serial abdominal exams. (EXAM-073-abdominal-inspection) - tenderness pattern, bowel activity, organomegaly, or ascites clue; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Positive acute-abdomen signs can prompt senior review, imaging, surgical consultation, NPO/analgesia planning, or serial abdominal exams. (EXAM-076-abdominal-palpation) - peritoneal irritation or acute abdomen; source Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Bleeding and anemia tests and thresholds (REQ-bleeding-anemia-tests) - Anemia severity, thrombocytopenia, coagulopathy, hemolysis, nutrient deficiency, active GI bleeding, or transfusion/endoscopy risk.; source ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
5. Management implication - Bleeding and anemia escalation cues (REQ-bleeding-anemia-red-flags) - Hemodynamic instability, active GI bleed, anticoagulant-associated bleeding, thrombocytopenic bleeding, pregnancy-related bleeding, or symptomatic severe anemia.; source ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

10. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; ACG_UGIB_2021; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
11. ### focused_msk_v1: Focused musculoskeletal joint, back, neck, or injury concern

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: focused_msk

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (SAFETY-validated-heart-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Count respiratory rate (SAFETY-validated-respiratory-rate) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Normal / Tachypneic / Bradypneic / Labored / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure oxygen saturation and support (SAFETY-validated-oxygen-saturation) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___% room air / ___% on oxygen ___ L/min / Hypoxemic / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment pulse oximeter, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Measure temperature (SAFETY-validated-temperature) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ C/F / Fever / Hypothermia / Afebrile / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment thermometer, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask urinary and flank infection-source symptoms - Fracture or dislocation risk, septic arthritis, spinal infection or cord compression, malignancy red flags, neurologic compromise, and bleeding risk.; options None / Trauma / Cannot bear weight-use limb / Fever / Hot swollen joint / Immunosuppression / Injection drug use / Cancer history / Neurologic deficit / Bowel-bladder change / Anticoagulant / Other ___; ask when Ask when joint, back, neck, limb, muscle, injury, swelling, or focused musculoskeletal concern is selected.; tags msk_exam; joint_pain; back_pain; septic_arthritis; fracture; cord_compression; history_question; focused_msk; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; JAMA_RCE; full question Any trauma, inability to bear weight or use the limb, fever, hot swollen joint, immunosuppression, injection drug use, cancer history, neurologic deficit, bowel or bladder change, or anticoagulant use?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
2. Ask rash danger features and exposure history - Visible inflammation, deformity, wound, asymmetry, swelling, erythema, or trauma pattern at the symptomatic site.; options Joint/site ___ / Trauma / Fever / Swelling / Redness / Wound / Cannot bear weight / Other ___; ask when Focused musculoskeletal joint or injury concern; tags musculoskeletal; inspection; site_specific; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Which exact joint or body area hurts most, and was there trauma, fever, swelling, redness, wound, or inability to bear weight?; details Ask about rash, cellulitis, abscess, ulcer, drainage, pain out of proportion, rapidly spreading redness, indwelling line/device, surgical site, and procedure-site tenderness. | Ask whether skin or line findings require culture, source control, antibiotic route/breadth, or urgent escalation.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect painful site (REQ-msk-focused-site-inspection) - Every MSK workup needs an atomic inspection step for the symptomatic site before special tests are chosen.; options Normal / Swelling / Deformity / Erythema / Wound / Bruising / Unable; use when Focused musculoskeletal joint or injury concern; technique Focused painful-site inspection; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags musculoskeletal; inspection; site_specific; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate painful site (REQ-msk-focused-site-palpation) - Palpation must be separate from inspection and ROM so warmth, effusion, and bony tenderness remain visible.; options Nontender / Focal tenderness / Warmth / Effusion / Bony tenderness / Unable; use when Focused musculoskeletal joint or injury concern; technique Focused painful-site palpation; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags musculoskeletal; palpation; site_specific; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test painful-site range of motion (REQ-msk-focused-site-rom) - Active/passive motion is its own maneuver and should not be bundled with strength, gait, or special tests.; options Full / Limited active / Limited passive / Painful / Blocked / Unable; use when Focused musculoskeletal joint or injury concern; technique Focused painful-site range of motion; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags musculoskeletal; range_of_motion; site_specific; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Deformity, wound, erythema, or marked swelling changes imaging, infection, trauma, immobilization, aspiration, or urgent referral decisions. (REQ-msk-focused-site-inspection) - Visible inflammation, deformity, wound, asymmetry, swelling, erythema, or trauma pattern at the symptomatic site.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Focal bony tenderness, warmth, or effusion changes imaging threshold, septic/inflammatory arthritis concern, aspiration consideration, and activity restriction. (REQ-msk-focused-site-palpation) - Focal tenderness, warmth, effusion, bony tenderness, or soft-tissue tenderness at the symptomatic site.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Loss of passive ROM, severe pain with movement, or inability to bear/use the site changes concern for septic arthritis, fracture, tendon injury, immobilization, imaging, or urgent evaluation. (REQ-msk-focused-site-rom) - Active or passive range-of-motion limitation, painful arc, mechanical block, weakness-limited movement, or inability to bear/use the site.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Management implication - Focused MSK tests and imaging pathway (REQ-msk-tests) - Fracture/dislocation, septic arthritis, crystal arthritis, osteomyelitis, inflammatory arthritis, malignancy, tendon injury, or serious spine disease.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
5. Management implication - MSK infection (REQ-msk-red-flags) - Septic arthritis, open fracture/dislocation, acute limb ischemia/nerve injury, cauda equina/cord compression, osteomyelitis, malignancy, or compression fracture.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; ACP_LOW_BACK_PAIN_2017; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Inspect painful site (REQ-msk-focused-site-inspection) - Visible inflammation, deformity, wound, asymmetry, swelling, erythema, or trauma pattern at the symptomatic site.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Interpretation caution - Palpate painful site (REQ-msk-focused-site-palpation) - Focal tenderness, warmth, effusion, bony tenderness, or soft-tissue tenderness at the symptomatic site.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Interpretation caution - Test painful-site range of motion (REQ-msk-focused-site-rom) - Active or passive range-of-motion limitation, painful arc, mechanical block, weakness-limited movement, or inability to bear/use the site.; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; source AAFP_MONOARTHRITIS_2025; SM25; MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

7. ### muscle_cramps_neuromuscular_v1: Muscle cramps or neuromuscular irritability

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: neuromuscular_cramps

1. Measure blood pressure (SAFETY-validated-blood-pressure) - Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.; options ___ / Low / Normal / High / Not available; use when Every validated clinical workup; keep separate from physical exam maneuvers.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Confirm abnormal or discordant values with the patient position, device, timing, trend, and clinical context.; tags basic_bedside_data; safety_check; vitals; source AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; MCGEE_EBPD; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask cramp-associated weakness and rhabdo symptoms - Neuromuscular clue: focal weakness, reflex change, upper motor neuron pattern, or radicular deficit.; options No / Weakness / Numbness / Back pain / Dark urine / Trouble walking / Other ___; ask when Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; tags DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ankle_dorsiflexion_strength; diabetes_foot; foot_drop; functional_status; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; vascular_screen; Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; full question Are the cramps associated with true weakness, numbness, back pain, dark urine, or trouble walking?
2. Ask pain location, trauma, and inflammatory red flags - Muscle injury or inflammatory clue: focal tenderness, swelling, diffuse pain, severe tenderness, or compartment concern.; options No / Severe pain / Weakness / Swelling / Dark urine / Exercise-heat / Statin-toxin / Vomiting-diarrhea / Diuretic / Other ___; ask when Muscle cramps or neuromuscular irritability; tags muscle_cramps; myalgia; muscle_tenderness; source MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; full question Any severe muscle pain, weakness, swelling, dark urine, heavy exercise, heat exposure, statin or toxin exposure, vomiting/diarrhea, or diuretic use?; details Ask specifically about severe muscle pain. | Ask specifically about weakness. | Ask specifically about swelling. | Ask specifically about dark urine. | Ask specifically about heavy exercise. | Ask specifically about heat exposure. | Ask specifically about statin. | Ask specifically about toxin exposure.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test ankle dorsiflexion strength (EXAM-027-ankle-dorsiflexion-strength) - Checks for weakness or reflex abnormality when cramps may reflect electrolyte, radicular, neuromuscular, or myelopathic disease.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; use when Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; technique Stabilize proximal to ankle; test one ankle at a time; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags DKA_HHS; diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; inpatient_diabetes; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ankle_dorsiflexion_strength; diabetes_foot; foot_drop; functional_status; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; vascular_screen; Foot drop; lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test ankle plantarflexion strength (EXAM-028-ankle-plantarflexion-strength) - Checks for weakness or reflex abnormality when cramps may reflect electrolyte, radicular, neuromuscular, or myelopathic disease.; options 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5; use when Lower extremity weakness; technique Stabilize proximal to ankle; test one ankle at a time; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1.5 min, equipment none, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; ankle_plantarflexion_strength; diabetes_foot; functional_status; lower_extremity; lower_extremity_weakness; motor; msk_exam; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; pain; vascular_screen; Lower extremity weakness; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test patellar reflex (EXAM-037-patellar-reflex) - Checks for weakness or reflex abnormality when cramps may reflect electrolyte, radicular, neuromuscular, or myelopathic disease.; options Absent / Diminished / Normal / Brisk / Clonus; use when Weakness; myelopathy; radiculopathy; technique Strike patellar tendon below patella; test bilaterally; feasibility difficulty moderate, time 2 min, equipment reflex hammer or tongue blade, cooperation moderate; limitations Interpret in clinical context; patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, and examiner skill may limit reliability.; tags diagnostic_safety; hypercalcemia; hypoglycemia; hyponatremia; inpatient_bedside; neuro; pituitary_sellar; stroke; weakness; lower_extremity; myelopathy; neuro_exam; neurologic_localization; patellar_reflex; radiculopathy; reflexes; upper_motor_neuron; Weakness; source Stanford Medicine 25 deep tendon reflex guide; Merck Manual reflex exam; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Palpate painful site (REQ-cramps-focused-muscle-tenderness) - Cramps/myalgia need an atomic muscle tenderness assessment before broad neuro testing is considered.; options Absent / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Swelling / Compartment firmness / Unable; use when Muscle cramps or neuromuscular irritability; technique Inspect the symptomatic area first, then palpate the painful muscle or site for focal tenderness, swelling, warmth, firmness, fluctuance, and pain out of proportion while comparing with the opposite side when possible.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 0.5 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Interpret in clinical context; abnormal bedside findings should be integrated with the full chart, patient trajectory, and measurement reliability.; tags muscle_cramps; myalgia; muscle_tenderness; source MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Weakness or abnormal reflexes can change urgency of electrolyte correction, CK/renal review, medication/toxin review, spine localization, and neurology escalation. (EXAM-027-ankle-dorsiflexion-strength) - Neuromuscular clue: focal weakness, reflex change, upper motor neuron pattern, or radicular deficit.; source Johns Hopkins neurological exam overview; Merck Manual weakness/neuro exam resources; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management implication - Marked tenderness, swelling, or dark urine changes CK/renal testing, medication/toxin review, hydration strategy, compartment/rhabdomyolysis escalation, and follow-up. (REQ-cramps-focused-muscle-tenderness) - Muscle injury or inflammatory clue: focal tenderness, swelling, diffuse pain, severe tenderness, or compartment concern.; source MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Management implication - Muscle cramps metabolic and injury tests (REQ-cramps-tests) - Electrolyte disturbance, AKI/dehydration, hypoglycemia, rhabdomyolysis, thyroid/parathyroid/vitamin D disease, medication toxicity, or arrhythmia risk.; source MCGEE_EBPD; ES_HYPOGLYCEMIA_2009; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.
4. Management implication - Muscle cramps danger cues (REQ-cramps-red-flags) - Rhabdomyolysis, electrolyte emergency, AKI/dehydration, neurologic disease, compartment syndrome, or arrhythmia risk.; source MCGEE_EBPD; AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this structured workup support item; use the cited guideline or threshold to guide testing, escalation, or management.

### acromegaly_intent_v1: Acromegaly

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Acromegaly (acromegaly_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Acromegaly.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Acromegaly.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; measure; heart; rate; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask pituitary mass-effect and treatment history - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / New headache / Peripheral vision loss / Double vision / Eye movement pain / Sudden severe headache / Nausea / Pituitary surgery/radiation history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Acromegaly is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; headache; vision; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history?; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
2. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - sweating - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Sweating / Enlarged hands/feet / Ring / Shoe-size change / Jaw/facial change / Joint pain / Sleep apnea / Headaches / Other ___; ask when Ask when Acromegaly is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; sweating; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any sweating, enlarged hands/feet, ring or shoe-size change, jaw/facial change, joint pain, sleep apnea, headaches, diabetes, hypertension, or colon-polyp history suggesting acromegaly complications?; details Ask specifically about sweating. | Ask specifically about enlarged hands/feet. | Ask specifically about ring. | Ask specifically about shoe-size change. | Ask specifically about jaw/facial change. | Ask specifically about joint pain. | Ask specifically about sleep apnea. | Ask specifically about headaches.
3. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - sleep apnea symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Loud snoring / Witnessed apneas / Morning headaches / Daytime sleepiness / Resistant hypertension / CPAP / Other ___; ask when Ask when Acromegaly is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; sleep; apnea; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use?; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
4. Ask treatment-limiting hypertension and diabetes complications - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Acromegaly therapy unsafe.; options No / New / Worsening / Resistant / Treatment-limiting hypertension / Diabetes that changes endocrine severity / Complication screening / Medication choice / Other ___; ask when Ask when Acromegaly is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; diabetes; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any new, worsening, resistant, or treatment-limiting hypertension or diabetes that changes endocrine severity, complication screening, or medication choice?; details Ask specifically about worsening. | Ask specifically about resistant. | Ask specifically about treatment-limiting hypertension. | Ask specifically about diabetes.
5. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Acromegaly therapy unsafe.; options No / Spontaneous / Expressible nipple discharge / Breast tenderness / Headaches / Visual symptoms / Medications that raise prolactin / Other ___; ask when Ask when Acromegaly is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; galactorrhea; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin?; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Acromegaly needs urgent evaluation today.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Acromegaly is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; libido; fertility; cycle; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Acromegaly.; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; use when When pituitary mass, macroadenoma, headache, or visual symptoms are relevant; technique Test each eye separately by confrontation, comparing peripheral fields with the examiner's field while the patient fixes on a central target.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Acromegaly, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; test; confrontation; visual; fields; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect hands for acral enlargement (exam_02) - Targets growth hormone excess phenotype with acral soft-tissue or skeletal enlargement; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Acromegaly.; options No acral enlargement / Acral enlargement / Soft-tissue swelling/ring-shoe size clue / Unable to assess; use when When growth hormone excess is possible; technique Inspect and compare hands, fingers, ring/shoe-size clues, acral contours, and facial soft-tissue changes with prior photos or baseline when available.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect hands for acral enlargement is supportive for Acromegaly, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; inspect; hands; acral; enlargement; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect tongue size (exam_03) - Targets macroglossia or soft-tissue enlargement affecting acromegaly phenotype, sleep apnea risk, or airway planning; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Acromegaly.; options Normal size / Macroglossia/dental impressions / Airway/sleep-apnea concern / Unable to assess; use when When acromegaly or macroglossia symptoms are possible; technique Inspect tongue size at rest and with protrusion, noting macroglossia, dental impressions, speech effects, and airway or sleep-apnea relevance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect tongue size is supportive for Acromegaly, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; inspect; tongue; size; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Elevated IGF-1 plus inadequate GH suppression changes to pituitary MRI/surgical pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Acromegaly.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Acromegaly.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; elevated; plus; inadequate; suppression; pituitary; surgical; pathway; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Acromegaly.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Acromegaly.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Acromegaly.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Acromegaly.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Acromegaly.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Acromegaly.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; central; adrenal; insufficiency; must; addressed; before; thyroid; hormone; source ACROMEGALY_COMPLICATIONS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Acromegaly.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Acromegaly.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; acromegaly; confirmed; hormone; excess; deficiency; plus; finding; neurosurgical; fertility; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,acromegaly,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Acromegaly, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. 13. Oral glucose GH suppression test: failure to suppress below assay cutoff, often <1 ng/mL or lower with modern assays. (test_02) - source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence. (test_03) - source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. A1c/BP/OSA/echo/colon risk screening. (test_04) - source ACROMEGALY_COMPLICATIONS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. GH-excess safety tests: check age-adjusted IGF-1, oral-glucose GH suppression when needed, pituitary MRI after biochemical confirmation, and glucose/A1c, sleep apnea, BP/cardiac, colon, and thyroid risk screening. (test_06) - source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_07) - source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated. (test_08) - source ACROMEGALY_COMPLICATIONS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ACROMEGALY_COMPLICATIONS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Visual field loss, pituitary apoplexy, severe cardiomyopathy, uncontrolled diabetes/OSA. (red_flag_01) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy. (red_flag_03) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Elevated IGF-1 plus inadequate GH suppression changes to pituitary MRI/surgical pathway. (management_01) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source ACROMEGALY_COMPLICATIONS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Acromegaly: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### addisons_disease_intent_v1: Addison's Disease

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Addison's Disease (addisons_disease_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2.
