Focused history questions
1. Ask adrenal insufficiency and volume-depletion symptoms - Have you had new salt craving - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; options Have you had new salt craving / Dizziness on standing / Low blood pressure / Vomiting / Abdominal pain / Unusual skin darkening / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; salt; craving; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening?; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
2. Ask adrenal insufficiency and volume-depletion symptoms - abdominal pain - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Abdominal pain / Nausea/vomiting / Poor intake / Salt craving / Orthostasis / Weight loss / New skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency / Crisis physiology / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; hyperpigmentation; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, poor intake, salt craving, orthostasis, weight loss, or new skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency or crisis physiology?; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about orthostasis. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about new skin/oral hyperpigmentation.
3. Ask HEENT infection exposure and deep-space warning symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; options How has weight changed from baseline / Was the change intentional / Associated with appetite / Fluid status / Systemic symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; weight; loss; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?
4. Review hyperglycemic-crisis treatments already started - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Addison's Disease.; options None yet / Fluids given / Insulin started / Potassium/phosphate addressed / Dextrose or bicarbonate used / Antibiotics given / Monitoring level documented / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; orthostasis; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt?
5. Review exogenous steroid exposure - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Addison's Disease therapy unsafe.; options No / Oral / Injected / Inhaled / Topical / Eye-drop / Joint-injection / Supplement steroid exposure / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; exogenous; glucocorticoids; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any oral, injected, inhaled, topical, eye-drop, joint-injection, or supplement steroid exposure?; details Ask specifically about oral. | Ask specifically about injected. | Ask specifically about inhaled. | Ask specifically about topical. | Ask specifically about eye-drop. | Ask specifically about joint-injection. | Ask specifically about supplement steroid exposure.
6. Ask pituitary mass-effect and treatment history - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / New headache / Peripheral vision loss / Double vision / Eye movement pain / Sudden severe headache / Nausea / Pituitary surgery/radiation history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; adrenal; pituitary; surgery; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history?; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
7. Ask infection precipitant or mimic symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Addison's Disease.; options No / Measured fever / Rigors / Hypothermia / Toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant / Mimic / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; fever; rigors; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any measured fever, rigors, hypothermia, or toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant or mimic?; details Ask specifically about measured fever. | Ask specifically about rigors. | Ask specifically about hypothermia. | Ask specifically about toxic appearance.
8. Ask respiratory infection symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Cough / Sputum / Pleuritic pain / Dyspnea / Hypoxemia / Focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia / Respiratory infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; respiratory; infection; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any cough, sputum, pleuritic pain, dyspnea, hypoxemia, or focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia or respiratory infection?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
9. Ask urinary symptoms and renal warning features - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Addison's Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Dysuria / Urinary frequency / Urgency / Suprapubic pain / Hematuria / Flank pain suggesting UTI / Pyelonephritis / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; urinary; flank; infection; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any dysuria, urinary frequency or urgency, suprapubic pain, hematuria, or flank pain suggesting UTI or pyelonephritis?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
10. Ask wound and procedure-site infection symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Addison's Disease therapy unsafe.; options No / Wound / Skin redness / Drainage / Tenderness / Indwelling line/device / Procedure-site concern suggesting infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Addison's Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; skin; wound; line; procedure; site; infection; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any wound, skin redness, drainage, tenderness, indwelling line/device, or procedure-site concern suggesting infection?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01) - Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options Absent / Oral/palmar hyperpigmentation / Diffuse or scar hyperpigmentation / Unable to assess; use when When primary adrenal insufficiency, Addison disease, ACTH excess, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia is possible; technique Inspect oral mucosa, palmar creases, scars, and sun-protected skin for diffuse or patchy hyperpigmentation.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Addison's Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; inspect; oral; mucosa; hyperpigmentation; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_02) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting, vomiting, or poor intake is possible; technique Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Addison's Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; inspect; mucous; membranes; dehydration; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test chair-rise strength (exam_03) - Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Addison's Disease.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting illness, or functional weakness is possible; technique Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Addison's Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; test; chair; rise; strength; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - High ACTH plus low cortisol confirms primary pattern; mineralocorticoid deficiency changes therapy to fludrocortisone/salt guidance. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Addison's Disease.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; high; acth; plus; cortisol; confirms; primary; pattern; mineralocorticoid; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Addison's Disease.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Addison's Disease.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Addison's Disease.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; suspected; adrenal; crisis; treated; immediately; stress; dose; steroids; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Addison's Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Addison's Disease.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; addisons_disease; marked; metanephrine; elevation; suppressed; renin; high; aldosterone; confirmed; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders,addisons_disease,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. (test_05) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock. (test_06) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_07) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_08) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. (reference_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (reference_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. (reference_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Adrenal crisis, severe hyperkalemia, shock, infection. (red_flag_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. High ACTH plus low cortisol confirms primary pattern; mineralocorticoid deficiency changes therapy to fludrocortisone/salt guidance. (management_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Addison's Disease: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### adrenal_insufficiency_intent_v1: Adrenal Insufficiency

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Adrenal Insufficiency (adrenal_insufficiency_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; measure; heart; rate; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, mineralocorticoid disorder, volume depletion, or catecholamine spells are possible; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; measure; orthostatic; blood; pressure; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask hypothyroid symptoms and menstrual pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Fatigue / Cold intolerance / Constipation / Dry skin / Hoarse voice / Slowed thinking / Depression / Heavy menses / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; fatigue; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism?; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
2. Ask HEENT infection exposure and deep-space warning symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; options How has weight changed from baseline / Was the change intentional / Associated with appetite / Fluid status / Systemic symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; weight; loss; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?
3. Ask adrenal insufficiency and volume-depletion symptoms - abdominal pain - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Abdominal pain / Nausea/vomiting / Poor intake / Salt craving / Orthostasis / Weight loss / New skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency / Crisis physiology / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; anorexia; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, poor intake, salt craving, orthostasis, weight loss, or new skin/oral hyperpigmentation suggesting adrenal insufficiency or crisis physiology?; details Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about nausea/vomiting. | Ask specifically about poor intake. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about orthostasis. | Ask specifically about weight loss. | Ask specifically about new skin/oral hyperpigmentation.
4. Ask adrenal insufficiency and volume-depletion symptoms - vomiting - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Adrenal Insufficiency therapy unsafe.; options No / Vomiting / Severe weakness / Abdominal pain / Fever / Missed steroid doses / Recent illness / Inability to keep down stress-dose steroids / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; nausea; vomiting; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any vomiting, severe weakness, abdominal pain, fever, missed steroid doses, recent illness, or inability to keep down stress-dose steroids?; details Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about severe weakness. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about missed steroid doses. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about inability to keep down stress-dose steroids.
5. Ask adrenal insufficiency and volume-depletion symptoms - Have you had new salt craving - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; options Have you had new salt craving / Dizziness on standing / Low blood pressure / Vomiting / Abdominal pain / Unusual skin darkening / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; salt; craving; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening?; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
6. Ask pituitary mass-effect and treatment history - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / New headache / Peripheral vision loss / Double vision / Eye movement pain / Sudden severe headache / Nausea / Pituitary surgery/radiation history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; pituitary; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history?; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
7. Review hyperglycemic-crisis treatments already started - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options None yet / Fluids given / Insulin started / Potassium/phosphate addressed / Dextrose or bicarbonate used / Antibiotics given / Monitoring level documented / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; orthostasis; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt?
8. Ask infection precipitant or mimic symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options No / Measured fever / Rigors / Hypothermia / Toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant / Mimic / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; fever; rigors; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any measured fever, rigors, hypothermia, or toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant or mimic?; details Ask specifically about measured fever. | Ask specifically about rigors. | Ask specifically about hypothermia. | Ask specifically about toxic appearance.
9. Ask respiratory infection symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Cough / Sputum / Pleuritic pain / Dyspnea / Hypoxemia / Focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia / Respiratory infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; respiratory; infection; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any cough, sputum, pleuritic pain, dyspnea, hypoxemia, or focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia or respiratory infection?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
10. Ask urinary symptoms and renal warning features - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Adrenal Insufficiency needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Dysuria / Urinary frequency / Urgency / Suprapubic pain / Hematuria / Flank pain suggesting UTI / Pyelonephritis / Other ___; ask when Ask when Adrenal Insufficiency is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; urinary; flank; infection; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; full question Any dysuria, urinary frequency or urgency, suprapubic pain, hematuria, or flank pain suggesting UTI or pyelonephritis?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01) - Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options Absent / Oral/palmar hyperpigmentation / Diffuse or scar hyperpigmentation / Unable to assess; use when When primary adrenal insufficiency, Addison disease, ACTH excess, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia is possible; technique Inspect oral mucosa, palmar creases, scars, and sun-protected skin for diffuse or patchy hyperpigmentation.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Adrenal Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; inspect; oral; mucosa; hyperpigmentation; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_02) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting, vomiting, or poor intake is possible; technique Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Adrenal Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; inspect; mucous; membranes; dehydration; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test chair-rise strength (exam_03) - Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Adrenal Insufficiency.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting illness, or functional weakness is possible; technique Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Adrenal Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; test; chair; rise; strength; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis should be treated urgently; do not delay hydrocortisone for testing if unstable. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Adrenal Insufficiency.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Adrenal Insufficiency.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; suspected; adrenal; crisis; should; treated; urgently; delay; hydrocortisone; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Adrenal Insufficiency.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Adrenal Insufficiency.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Adrenal Insufficiency.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Adrenal Insufficiency.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Adrenal Insufficiency.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Adrenal Insufficiency.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; suspected; adrenal; crisis; treated; immediately; stress; dose; steroids; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Adrenal Insufficiency.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Adrenal Insufficiency.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; adrenal_insufficiency; marked; metanephrine; elevation; suppressed; renin; high; aldosterone; confirmed; source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

21. Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock. (test_05) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_06) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_07) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. (reference_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (reference_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. (reference_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Shock, vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyponatremia/hyperkalemia, altered mental status. (red_flag_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Suspected adrenal crisis should be treated urgently; do not delay hydrocortisone for testing if unstable. (management_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Adrenal Insufficiency: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ES_ADRENAL_INSUFFICIENCY_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
