### amenorrhea_intent_v1: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) (amenorrhea_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; measure; blood; pressure; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; measure; heart; rate; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; use when During reproductive or gonadal endocrine evaluation; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale and height measurement, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; measure; body; mass; index; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask amenorrhea onset and menstrual pattern - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Did menstrual periods never start by the expected age / Did previously present periods stop / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; primary; secondary; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Did menstrual periods never start by the expected age, or did previously present periods stop?
2. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - When was the last menstrual - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) needs urgent evaluation today.; options When was the last menstrual period / How regular are cycles / Bleeding changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; cycle; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?
3. Ask pregnancy possibility and reproductive plans - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; pregnancy; risk; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Is pregnancy possible now, planned soon, or recently postpartum, and would pregnancy change medication or imaging safety?
4. Ask thyroid symptoms affecting reproductive function - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) needs urgent evaluation today.; options How has weight changed from baseline / Was the change intentional / Associated with appetite / Fluid status / Systemic symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; weight; loss; exercise; stress; eating; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?
5. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; options No / Spontaneous / Expressible nipple discharge / Breast tenderness / Headaches / Visual symptoms / Medications that raise prolactin / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; galactorrhea; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin?; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. Ask gonadotoxic treatment history - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / New headache / Peripheral vision loss / Double vision / Eye movement pain / Sudden severe headache / Nausea / Pituitary surgery/radiation history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; headache; vision; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history?; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
7. Ask PID and pelvic infection features - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options No / New / Rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth / Acne / Scalp hair loss / Deepening voice / Clitoromegaly / Menstrual irregularity / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; hirsutism; acne; flashes; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity?; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.
8. Ask pregnancy and ectopic danger features - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; fertility; goals; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Are fertility goals, pregnancy possibility, partner factors, or treatment choices relevant to the timing and safety of this endocrine workup?
9. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - pregnancy/fertility safety - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; flashes; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, vaginal/genitourinary symptoms, mood change, cycle change, or fertility goal affecting reproductive endocrine management?; details Ask specifically about hot flashes. | Ask specifically about night sweats. | Ask specifically about sleep disruption. | Ask specifically about vaginal/genitourinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about mood change. | Ask specifically about cycle change. | Ask specifically about fertility goal.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen excess or PCOS is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; inspect; acne; distribution; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; use when When hirsutism, PCOS, or androgen excess is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; inspect; terminal; hair; distribution; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_03) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; use when When PCOS, insulin resistance, or hyperandrogenic metabolic risk is possible; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; inspect; neck; acanthosis; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect thyroid gland (exam_04) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when When menstrual, fertility, or menopausal symptoms could reflect thyroid disease; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect thyroid gland is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; inspect; thyroid; gland; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect for galactorrhea (exam_05) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; options Absent / Spontaneous discharge / Expressible discharge / Bloody/unilateral discharge / Unable/deferred; use when When amenorrhea, infertility, breast symptoms, or prolactin excess is possible; technique Inspect for spontaneous discharge and, only when clinically appropriate, gently express from the areola toward the nipple while preserving privacy and consent.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect for galactorrhea is supportive for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; inspect; galactorrhea; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - FSH/E2 pattern separates ovarian failure from central/hypothalamic causes and changes imaging/referral. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; pattern; separates; ovarian; failure; central; hypothalamic; causes; imaging; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; pregnancy; fertility; intent; rapid; virilization; tumor; range; hormones; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; amenorrhea; biochemical; hypogonadism; hyperprolactinemia; pcos; thyroid; counseling; bone; cardiometabolic; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,amenorrhea,measure,blood,pressure; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,amenorrhea,measure,heart,rate; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. 4. 21. Androgen testing when hyperandrogenism: testosterone, DHEAS, 17-OHP. (test_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Gonadal axis safety tests: pregnancy test when possible, prolactin/TSH, LH/FSH with estradiol or repeated early-morning testosterone, and targeted androgen, semen, bone, or metabolic testing based on syndrome and fertility goals. (test_06) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_06) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
32. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, visual loss/headache, rapid virilization, vital sign instability/eating disorder. (red_flag_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
34. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
35. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. FSH/E2 pattern separates ovarian failure from central/hypothalamic causes and changes imaging/referral. (management_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
41. Amenorrhea (Absence of menstruation): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
42. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
43. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Catalog gaps needing review
- none

### congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia_intent_v1: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) (congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; measure; heart; rate; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options No orthostatic drop / Orthostatic hypotension / Symptomatic dizziness/presyncope / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, mineralocorticoid disorder, volume depletion, or catecholamine spells are possible; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; measure; orthostatic; blood; pressure; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask PID and pelvic infection features - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; neonatal; ambiguous; genitalia; salt; wasting; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Any neonatal ambiguous genitalia, salt-wasting episode, early pubarche, rapid growth, virilization, menstrual/fertility issue, or family CAH history?; details Ask specifically about neonatal ambiguous genitalia. | Ask specifically about salt-wasting episode. | Ask specifically about early pubarche. | Ask specifically about rapid growth. | Ask specifically about virilization. | Ask specifically about menstrual/fertility issue. | Ask specifically about family CAH history.
2. Ask partner semen analysis and fertility evaluation - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; infertility; source ES_CAH_2018; full question How long has conception been attempted, are cycles ovulatory/regular, and have partner semen analysis or prior fertility evaluations found abnormalities?; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
3. Ask treatment-limiting hypertension and diabetes complications - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options No / Personal / Family autoimmune thyroid disease / Type 1 diabetes / Celiac disease / Pernicious anemia / Adrenal disease / Pituitary disease / Polyglandular autoimmune syndrome / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; family; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Any personal or family autoimmune thyroid disease, type 1 diabetes, celiac disease, pernicious anemia, adrenal disease, pituitary disease, or polyglandular autoimmune syndrome?; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family autoimmune thyroid disease. | Ask specifically about type 1 diabetes. | Ask specifically about celiac disease. | Ask specifically about pernicious anemia. | Ask specifically about adrenal disease. | Ask specifically about pituitary disease. | Ask specifically about polyglandular autoimmune syndrome.
4. Ask adrenal insufficiency and volume-depletion symptoms - vomiting - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; options No / Vomiting / Severe weakness / Abdominal pain / Fever / Missed steroid doses / Recent illness / Inability to keep down stress-dose steroids / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; adrenal; crisis; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Any vomiting, severe weakness, abdominal pain, fever, missed steroid doses, recent illness, or inability to keep down stress-dose steroids?; details Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about severe weakness. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about fever. | Ask specifically about missed steroid doses. | Ask specifically about recent illness. | Ask specifically about inability to keep down stress-dose steroids.
5. Review hyperglycemic-crisis treatments already started - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options None yet / Fluids given / Insulin started / Potassium/phosphate addressed / Dextrose or bicarbonate used / Antibiotics given / Monitoring level documented / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; orthostasis; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Do you feel lightheaded when standing, faint, unusually weak, or better after fluids or salt?
6. Ask adrenal insufficiency and volume-depletion symptoms - Have you had new salt craving - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) needs urgent evaluation today.; options Have you had new salt craving / Dizziness on standing / Low blood pressure / Vomiting / Abdominal pain / Unusual skin darkening / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; salt; craving; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Have you had new salt craving, dizziness on standing, low blood pressure, vomiting, abdominal pain, or unusual skin darkening?; details Ask specifically about Have you had new salt craving. | Ask specifically about dizziness on standing. | Ask specifically about low blood pressure. | Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about unusual skin darkening.
7. Ask infection precipitant or mimic symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options No / Measured fever / Rigors / Hypothermia / Toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant / Mimic / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; fever; rigors; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Any measured fever, rigors, hypothermia, or toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant or mimic?; details Ask specifically about measured fever. | Ask specifically about rigors. | Ask specifically about hypothermia. | Ask specifically about toxic appearance.
8. Ask respiratory infection symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Cough / Sputum / Pleuritic pain / Dyspnea / Hypoxemia / Focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia / Respiratory infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; respiratory; infection; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Any cough, sputum, pleuritic pain, dyspnea, hypoxemia, or focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia or respiratory infection?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
9. Ask urinary symptoms and renal warning features - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Dysuria / Urinary frequency / Urgency / Suprapubic pain / Hematuria / Flank pain suggesting UTI / Pyelonephritis / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; urinary; flank; infection; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Any dysuria, urinary frequency or urgency, suprapubic pain, hematuria, or flank pain suggesting UTI or pyelonephritis?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
10. Ask wound and procedure-site infection symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) therapy unsafe.; options No / Wound / Skin redness / Drainage / Tenderness / Indwelling line/device / Procedure-site concern suggesting infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; skin; wound; line; procedure; site; infection; source ES_CAH_2018; full question Any wound, skin redness, drainage, tenderness, indwelling line/device, or procedure-site concern suggesting infection?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation (exam_01) - Targets primary adrenal insufficiency, ACTH excess pattern, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia clue; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Absent / Oral/palmar hyperpigmentation / Diffuse or scar hyperpigmentation / Unable to assess; use when When primary adrenal insufficiency, Addison disease, ACTH excess, or congenital adrenal hyperplasia is possible; technique Inspect oral mucosa, palmar creases, scars, and sun-protected skin for diffuse or patchy hyperpigmentation.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect oral mucosa for hyperpigmentation is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; inspect; oral; mucosa; hyperpigmentation; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_02) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting, vomiting, or poor intake is possible; technique Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; inspect; mucous; membranes; dehydration; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test chair-rise strength (exam_03) - Targets clinically important weakness, adrenal-crisis severity, or functional impairment; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When adrenal insufficiency, salt-wasting illness, or functional weakness is possible; technique Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; test; chair; rise; strength; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_04) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen excess, virilization, or nonclassic CAH is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; inspect; terminal; hair; distribution; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Electrolyte crisis or vomiting changes to emergency steroid/fluid pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; electrolyte; crisis; vomiting; emergency; steroid; fluid; pathway; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

4. Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; suspected; adrenal; crisis; treated; immediately; stress; dose; steroids; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia; marked; metanephrine; elevation; suppressed; renin; high; aldosterone; confirmed; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders,congenital_adrenal_hyperplasia,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

24. Adrenal insufficiency safety labs: check sodium, potassium, glucose, bicarbonate, creatinine, morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin testing when stable, and renin/aldosterone or 17-OHP context for mineralocorticoid/CAH decisions; do not delay stress-dose steroids in shock. (test_06) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_07) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_08) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. 17-OHP thresholds vary by age, gestational age, assay, cycle phase, and timing; morning follicular sampling improves adult NCCAH screening. (reference_01) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_02) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(red_flag_02) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Electrolyte crisis or vomiting changes to emergency steroid/fluid pathway. (management_01) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Congenital Adrenal Hyperplasia (CAH): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 17-OHP thresholds vary by age, gestational age, assay, cycle phase, and timing; morning follicular sampling improves adult NCCAH screening. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ES_CAH_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Status: READY

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect skin for bruising (exam_01) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; options Absent / Present / Marked / Unable to assess; use when When Cushing disease or ACTH-dependent hypercortisolism is possible; technique Inspect exposed skin and body habitus for easy bruising, wide purple striae, supraclavicular fullness, and related Cushingoid features.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect skin for bruising is supportive for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; cushings_disease; inspect; skin; bruising; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - ACTH-dependent confirmed Cushing plus pituitary localization changes to pituitary surgery pathway; discordance triggers IPSS. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; cushings_disease; acth; dependent; confirmed; cushing; plus; pituitary; localization; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; cushings_disease; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; cushings_disease; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; cushings_disease; central; adrenal; insufficiency; must; addressed; before; thyroid; hormone; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's).; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; cushings_disease; confirmed; hormone; excess; deficiency; plus; finding; neurosurgical; fertility; source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,cushings_disease,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,cushings_disease,measure,heart,rate; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

16. First confirm endogenous Cushing with UFC, late-night salivary cortisol, or 1-mg DST cortisol >1.8 ug/dL. (test_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. ACTH: normal/high supports ACTH-dependent disease. (test_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Pituitary MRI. (test_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Inferior petrosal sinus sampling when MRI equivocal/discordant. (test_04) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Cushing safety tests: use late-night salivary cortisol, 1-mg dexamethasone suppression, or 24-hour urinary free cortisol after excluding exogenous steroids, and assess glucose, BP, potassium, infection/VTE, and bone risk to determine severity and treatment urgency. (test_06) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_07) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated. (test_08) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Post-1 mg DST cortisol >1.8 ug/dL is a sensitive abnormal screen; late-night salivary and UFC cutoffs are assay-specific. (reference_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Severe hypercortisolism with infection, hypokalemia, psychosis, VTE, uncontrolled hypertension/diabetes. (red_flag_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy. (red_flag_03) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. ACTH-dependent confirmed Cushing plus pituitary localization changes to pituitary surgery pathway; discordance triggers IPSS. (management_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Cushing's Disease (Pituitary-dependent Cushing's): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Post-1 mg DST cortisol >1.8 ug/dL is a sensitive abnormal screen; late-night salivary and UFC cutoffs are assay-specific. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
