### cushings_syndrome_intent_v1: Cushing's Syndrome

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Cushing's Syndrome (cushings_syndrome_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Cushing's Syndrome.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. 2. Ask infection precipitant or mimic symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Cushing's Syndrome.; options No / Measured fever / Rigors / Hypothermia / Toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant / Mimic / Other ___; ask when Ask when Cushing's Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; fever; rigors; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; full question Any measured fever, rigors, hypothermia, or toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant or mimic?; details Ask specifically about measured fever. | Ask specifically about rigors. | Ask specifically about hypothermia. | Ask specifically about toxic appearance.
3. Ask respiratory infection symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Cough / Sputum / Pleuritic pain / Dyspnea / Hypoxemia / Focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia / Respiratory infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Cushing's Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; respiratory; infection; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; full question Any cough, sputum, pleuritic pain, dyspnea, hypoxemia, or focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia or respiratory infection?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
4. Ask urinary symptoms and renal warning features - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Cushing's Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Dysuria / Urinary frequency / Urgency / Suprapubic pain / Hematuria / Flank pain suggesting UTI / Pyelonephritis / Other ___; ask when Ask when Cushing's Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; urinary; flank; infection; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; full question Any dysuria, urinary frequency or urgency, suprapubic pain, hematuria, or flank pain suggesting UTI or pyelonephritis?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.

Ask wound and procedure-site infection symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Cushing's Syndrome therapy unsafe.; options No / Wound / Skin redness / Drainage / Tenderness / Indwelling line/device / Procedure-site concern suggesting infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Cushing's Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; skin; wound; line; procedure; site; infection; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; full question Any wound, skin redness, drainage, tenderness, indwelling line/device, or procedure-site concern suggesting infection?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect skin for bruising (exam_01) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Cushing's Syndrome.; options Absent / Present / Marked / Unable to assess; use when When Cushing syndrome or glucocorticoid excess is possible; technique Inspect exposed skin and body habitus for easy bruising, wide purple striae, supraclavicular fullness, and related Cushingoid features.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect skin for bruising is supportive for Cushing's Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; inspect; skin; bruising; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test chair-rise strength (exam_02) - Targets hypercortisolism-related proximal myopathy and functional severity; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Cushing's Syndrome.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When Cushing syndrome or glucocorticoid myopathy is possible; technique Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Cushing's Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; test; chair; rise; strength; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Conditional exam add-ons
- none

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Abnormal screening plus convincing phenotype triggers confirmatory/endocrine pathway; ACTH directs adrenal vs ACTH-dependent workup. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Syndrome.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Syndrome.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; abnormal; screening; plus; convincing; phenotype; triggers; confirmatory; pathway; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Syndrome.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Syndrome.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Syndrome.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Syndrome.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Syndrome.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Syndrome.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; suspected; adrenal; crisis; treated; immediately; stress; dose; steroids; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Cushing's Syndrome.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Cushing's Syndrome.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; cushings_syndrome; marked; metanephrine; elevation; suppressed; renin; high; aldosterone; confirmed; source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders,cushings_syndrome,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders,cushings_syndrome,measure,heart,rate; source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

4. Do you have trouble rising from a chair, climbing stairs, lifting overhead, or getting up from the floor? (question_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
5. Any measured fever, rigors, hypothermia, or toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant or mimic? (question_02) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
6. Any cough, sputum, pleuritic pain, dyspnea, hypoxemia, or focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia or respiratory infection? (question_03) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.

18. Exclude exogenous steroids. (test_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. 1-mg overnight DST: cortisol >1.8 ug/dL abnormal/sensitive cutoff. (test_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Late-night salivary cortisol, often two samples. (test_03) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. 24-hour urinary free cortisol, at least two collections. (test_04) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. ACTH after confirmed hypercortisolism. (test_05) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_06) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Cushing safety tests: use late-night salivary cortisol, 1-mg dexamethasone suppression, or 24-hour urinary free cortisol after excluding exogenous steroids, and assess glucose, BP, potassium, infection/VTE, and bone risk to determine severity and treatment urgency. (test_07) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_08) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_09) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. DST, late-night salivary cortisol, and UFC cutoffs are assay-specific; abnormal screening generally needs confirmation. (reference_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Severe hypokalemia, uncontrolled hypertension/diabetes, infection, psychosis, VTE. (red_flag_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Abnormal screening plus convincing phenotype triggers confirmatory/endocrine pathway; ACTH directs adrenal vs ACTH-dependent workup. (management_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Cushing's Syndrome: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_CUSHING_DX_2008; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: DST, late-night salivary cortisol, and UFC cutoffs are assay-specific; abnormal screening generally needs confirmation. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ES_CUSHING_TREATMENT_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### diabetes_insipidus_intent_v1: Diabetes Insipidus

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Diabetes Insipidus (diabetes_insipidus_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Diabetes Insipidus.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; measure; blood; pressure; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Diabetes Insipidus.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; measure; heart; rate; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask treatment-limiting hypertension and diabetes complications - What is the approximate 24-hour - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Diabetes Insipidus.; options What is the approximate 24-hour urine volume / Urine output changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Diabetes Insipidus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; hour; urine; volume; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; full question What is the approximate 24-hour urine volume, and has urine output changed from baseline?
2. Ask treatment-limiting hypertension and diabetes complications - How many times do you - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Diabetes Insipidus.; options How many times do you urinate overnight / This new / Worsening / Other ___; ask when Ask when Diabetes Insipidus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; nocturia; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; full question How many times do you urinate overnight, and is this new or worsening?
3. 4. 5. Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_01) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Diabetes Insipidus.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; use when When polyuria, polydipsia, or hypernatremia is present; technique Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Diabetes Insipidus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; inspect; mucous; membranes; dehydration; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test capillary refill (exam_02) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Diabetes Insipidus.; options Brisk / Delayed / Mottled/cool / Unable to assess; use when When water-balance disorder, dehydration, or hypernatremia is possible; technique Apply brief pressure to a fingertip or toe and record return of color while considering temperature, perfusion, and vascular disease.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test capillary refill is supportive for Diabetes Insipidus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; test; capillary; refill; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test skin turgor (exam_03) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Diabetes Insipidus.; options Normal recoil / Reduced recoil / Unable to assess; use when When polyuria, polydipsia, dehydration, or hypernatremia is present; technique Gently pinch skin over a clinically appropriate site and observe recoil, interpreting cautiously in older adults or chronic skin changes.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test skin turgor is supportive for Diabetes Insipidus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; test; skin; turgor; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Hypernatremia or impaired water access changes to urgent monitored fluid/desmopressin pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Diabetes Insipidus.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; hypernatremia; impaired; water; access; urgent; monitored; fluid; desmopressin; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Diabetes Insipidus.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Diabetes Insipidus.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Diabetes Insipidus.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; central; adrenal; insufficiency; must; addressed; before; thyroid; hormone; source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Diabetes Insipidus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Diabetes Insipidus.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; diabetes_insipidus; confirmed; hormone; excess; deficiency; plus; finding; neurosurgical; fertility; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,diabetes_insipidus,measure,blood,pressure; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,diabetes_insipidus,measure,heart,rate; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure orthostatic blood pressure (safety_check_03_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,diabetes_insipidus,measure,orthostatic,blood,pressure; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Interpretation caution - Document mental status (safety_check_04_limitation) - Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.; limitations Global appearance and perfusion are subjective and affected by baseline function, pain, medications, temperature, and examiner experience; pair with objective vitals and reassessment.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,diabetes_insipidus,mental; source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. 17. Exclude osmotic diuresis: glucose, calcium, potassium, renal function. (test_03) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin-based test. (test_04) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Diabetes insipidus safety tests: check serum sodium/osmolality, urine osmolality/specific gravity, urine volume, glucose/calcium/potassium/renal function, and desmopressin, copeptin, or supervised water-deprivation pathway only when stable. (test_06) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_07) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated. (test_08) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Serum sodium normal about 135-145 mmol/L; SFE inpatient CDI guidance: 146-149 mild, 150-159 moderate, >160 severe hypernatremia. (reference_01) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Urine osmolality <300 mOsm/kg is hypotonic; >800 often argues against DI. (reference_02) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_03) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Hypernatremia, inability to drink, impaired consciousness, postoperative pituitary patient without desmopressin access. (red_flag_01) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy. (red_flag_03) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Hypernatremia or impaired water access changes to urgent monitored fluid/desmopressin pathway. (management_01) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Diabetes Insipidus: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ENDOTEXT_DI_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Serum sodium normal about 135-145 mmol/L; SFE inpatient CDI guidance: 146-149 mild, 150-159 moderate, >160 severe hypernatremia. Urine osmolality <300 mOsm/kg is hypotonic; >800 often argues against DI. (differential_02) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source SFE_CDI_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### erectile_dysfunction_intent_v1: Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related) (erectile_dysfunction_v1)

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Measure testicular volume (exam_01) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related).; options Expected volume / Small bilaterally / Asymmetric/mass concern / Unable/deferred; use when When male hypogonadism, infertility, gynecomastia, or erectile dysfunction is relevant; technique Estimate testicular volume with orchidometer when available or careful exam, documenting asymmetry, tenderness, or mass separately.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment orchidometer optional, cooperation low; limitations Measure testicular volume is supportive for Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; erectile_dysfunction; measure; testicular; volume; source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

17. Gonadal axis safety tests: pregnancy test when possible, prolactin/TSH, LH/FSH with estradiol or repeated early-morning testosterone, and targeted androgen, semen, bone, or metabolic testing based on syndrome and fertility goals. (test_05) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_06) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_07) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. (reference_04) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (reference_05) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. (reference_06) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. (reference_07) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_08) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. New ED with chest pain/claudication, neurologic deficit, priapism, severe hypogonadism/pituitary symptoms. (red_flag_01) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Low testosterone changes to hypogonadism pathway; diabetes/ASCVD risk changes cardiovascular management. (management_01) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Erectile Dysfunction (Endocrine-related): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source AUA_ED_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Catalog gaps needing review
- none

### gestational_diabetes_intent_v1: Gestational Diabetes

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Gestational Diabetes (gestational_diabetes_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; measure; blood; pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; measure; heart; rate; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; use when During diabetes or metabolic risk assessment; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; measure; weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Gestational Diabetes.; options ___ cm/in / Above risk threshold / Not above risk threshold / Unable/not available; use when When cardiometabolic risk or metabolic syndrome is being assessed; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment tape measure, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; measure; waist; circumference; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask pregnancy and ectopic danger features - How many weeks pregnant are - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gestational Diabetes therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gestational Diabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; gestational; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question How many weeks pregnant are you, and has pregnancy dating, fetal growth, or obstetric context changed diabetes screening or treatment safety?
2. Ask prior gestational diabetes and macrosomia history - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gestational Diabetes.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gestational Diabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; macrosomia; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any history of prior gestational diabetes or macrosomia that changes Gestational Diabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety?; details Ask specifically about history of prior gestational diabetes. | Ask specifically about macrosomia.
3. Ask pregnancy and ectopic danger features - pregnancy/fertility safety - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gestational Diabetes therapy unsafe.; options Known/reviewed / Hyperglycemia / Ketones elevated / Acidosis or anion gap / Potassium abnormal / Renal/osmolality concern / Not available; ask when Ask when Gestational Diabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; preexisting; diabetes; risk; fetal; growth; polyhydramnios; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any pre-pregnancy diabetes, prior A1c/glucose abnormality, PCOS, obesity, steroid exposure, or strong family history that changes gestational diabetes classification or treatment intensity?; details Ask whether pregnancy is possible now. | Ask about current fertility goals or pregnancy plans. | Ask whether postpartum status, partner factors, imaging, or medications change safety decisions.
4. Ask home glucose pattern and baseline change - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gestational Diabetes.; options Known/reviewed / Hyperglycemia / Ketones elevated / Acidosis or anion gap / Potassium abnormal / Renal/osmolality concern / Not available; ask when Ask when Gestational Diabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; home; glucose; pattern; available; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question What is the current home glucose pattern if available, and how has it changed from baseline?
5. Ask HEENT infection exposure and deep-space warning symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gestational Diabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gestational Diabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; fetal; growth; polyhydramnios; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Have fetal growth, estimated fetal weight, amniotic fluid, or obstetric ultrasound findings raised concern for hyperglycemia-related risk?
6. Ask obstetric plan and fetal surveillance - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gestational Diabetes.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gestational Diabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; obstetric; care; plan; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question What is the current obstetric plan, gestational age, fetal surveillance plan, delivery timing, and maternal-fetal medicine involvement?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Gestational Diabetes.; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; use when When insulin resistance or metabolic syndrome is suspected; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Gestational Diabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; gestational_diabetes; inspect; neck; acanthosis; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,gestational_diabetes,measure,blood,pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

3. Interpretation caution - Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,gestational_diabetes,measure,weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Interpretation caution - Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,gestational_diabetes,measure,waist,circumference; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
5. Interpretation caution - Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Gestational Diabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Gestational Diabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,gestational_diabetes,inspect,neck,acanthosis; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Interpretation caution - Early risk testing if high risk. Interpretation caution - Physical exam scope for Gestational Diabetes (gestational_diabetes_v1_physical_exam_scope) - This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. Check basic bedside data and anthropometrics, document the focused insulin-resistance phenotype exam when relevant, and add foot/vascular, hydration/perfusion, abdominal, cardiopulmonary, or neurologic exams only when symptoms, complications, or a second validated intent trigger them.; limitations This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. 19. One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. 20. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. 27. Hypertension or preeclampsia features. (red_flag_02) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_03) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Ketones, vomiting/dehydration, severe hyperglycemia, altered mental status, hypertension/preeclampsia features, or reduced fetal movement requires urgent obstetric/endocrine evaluation. (red_flag_04) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. OGTT threshold crossing changes maternal/fetal monitoring and therapy. (management_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Postpartum abnormal OGTT changes long-term prevention. (management_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. OGTT threshold crossing changes maternal-fetal monitoring, nutrition therapy, glucose monitoring, medication escalation, and delivery/postpartum planning. (management_05) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Ketones, vomiting/dehydration, severe hyperglycemia, altered mental status, or hypertensive features in pregnancy changes disposition to urgent obstetric/endocrine evaluation. (management_06) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Gestational Diabetes: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. (differential_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

- none
