### gigantism_intent_v1: Gigantism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Gigantism (gigantism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gigantism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; measure; heart; rate; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask HEENT infection exposure and deep-space warning symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gigantism.; options How has height / Growth velocity changed compared with prior measurements / Family height pattern / Puberty timing / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gigantism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; accelerated; linear; growth; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question How has height or growth velocity changed compared with prior measurements, family height pattern, and puberty timing?
2. Ask pituitary mass-effect and treatment history - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gigantism needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / New headache / Peripheral vision loss / Double vision / Eye movement pain / Sudden severe headache / Nausea / Pituitary surgery/radiation history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gigantism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; headache; vision; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history?; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
3. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gigantism needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Loud snoring / Witnessed apneas / Morning headaches / Daytime sleepiness / Resistant hypertension / CPAP / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gigantism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; sleep; apnea; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use?; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
4. Ask pelvic pain location, cycle timing, and GU/GI symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gigantism therapy unsafe.; options No relevant exposure / Medication change / Supplement use / Missed doses / Biotin / Iodine/contrast exposure / Hormone therapy / Recent treatment change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gigantism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; medications; supplements; missed; doses; recent; procedures; acute; illnesses; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Is pregnancy possible now, planned soon, or recently postpartum, and would pregnancy change medication or imaging safety?
5. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gigantism therapy unsafe.; options No / Spontaneous / Expressible nipple discharge / Breast tenderness / Headaches / Visual symptoms / Medications that raise prolactin / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gigantism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; galactorrhea; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin?; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Gigantism needs urgent evaluation today.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gigantism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; libido; fertility; cycle; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; full question When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Gigantism.; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; use when When pituitary mass, macroadenoma, headache, or visual symptoms are relevant; technique Test each eye separately by confrontation, comparing peripheral fields with the examiner's field while the patient fixes on a central target.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Gigantism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; test; confrontation; visual; fields; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect hands for acral enlargement (exam_02) - Targets growth hormone excess phenotype with acral soft-tissue or skeletal enlargement; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options No acral enlargement / Acral enlargement / Soft-tissue swelling/ring-shoe size clue / Unable to assess; use when When growth hormone excess is possible; technique Inspect and compare hands, fingers, ring/shoe-size clues, acral contours, and facial soft-tissue changes with prior photos or baseline when available.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect hands for acral enlargement is supportive for Gigantism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; inspect; hands; acral; enlargement; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect tongue size (exam_03) - Targets macroglossia or soft-tissue enlargement affecting acromegaly phenotype, sleep apnea risk, or airway planning; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options Normal size / Macroglossia/dental impressions / Airway/sleep-apnea concern / Unable to assess; use when When acromegaly or macroglossia symptoms are possible; technique Inspect tongue size at rest and with protrusion, noting macroglossia, dental impressions, speech effects, and airway or sleep-apnea relevance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect tongue size is supportive for Gigantism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; inspect; tongue; size; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Stage pubertal development (exam_04) - Targets pubertal development, delayed/precocious puberty pattern, or hypogonadal phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gigantism.; options Age-appropriate / Delayed/underdeveloped / Advanced/virilized / Unable/deferred; use when When gigantism or pediatric growth concern is relevant; technique Assess Tanner stage or secondary sex characteristics only when clinically appropriate and with consent/chaperone practices.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Stage pubertal development is supportive for Gigantism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; stage; pubertal; development; source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Confirmed GH excess before epiphyseal closure changes urgency of pediatric pituitary treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Gigantism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Gigantism.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; gigantism; central; adrenal; insufficiency; must; addressed; before; thyroid; hormone; source ACROMEGALY_COMPLICATIONS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

31. Gigantism: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_ACROMEGALY_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
32. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Pediatric/puberty-specific reference ranges are mandatory; adult cutoffs are not sufficient. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source PITUITARY_ACROMEGALY_2021; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ACROMEGALY_DIAGNOSIS_REMISSION_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### graves_disease_intent_v1: Graves' Disease

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Graves' Disease (graves_disease_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Graves' Disease.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; measure; blood; pressure; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Graves' Disease.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; measure; heart; rate; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask hyperthyroid adrenergic, weight, and GI symptoms - Have you had heat intolerance - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Graves' Disease needs urgent evaluation today.; options Have you had heat intolerance / Palpitations / Tremor / Anxiety / Unintentional weight loss / Diarrhea / Increased sweating / Other ___; ask when Ask when Graves' Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; hyperthyroid; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; full question Have you had heat intolerance, palpitations, tremor, anxiety, unintentional weight loss, diarrhea, or increased sweating?; details Ask specifically about Have you had heat intolerance. | Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about anxiety. | Ask specifically about unintentional weight loss. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about increased sweating.
2. 3. Ask Graves orbitopathy eye symptoms - Do you currently smoke or - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Graves' Disease.; options Do you currently smoke / Recently smoke / Especially in the context of Graves orbitopathy / Cardiovascular risk / Other ___; ask when Ask when Graves' Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; smoking; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; full question Do you currently smoke or recently smoke, especially in the context of Graves orbitopathy or cardiovascular risk?
4. Ask pregnancy, postpartum, and fertility-related thyroid safety - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Graves' Disease therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Graves' Disease is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; pregnancy; plans; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; full question Is pregnancy possible now, planned soon, or recently postpartum, and would pregnancy change medication or imaging safety?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Graves' Disease.; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Graves' Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; palpate; thyroid; gland; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Graves' Disease.; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant; technique Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate cervical lymph nodes is supportive for Graves' Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; palpate; cervical; lymph; nodes; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Graves' Disease.; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid dysfunction could affect cardiac rhythm; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Graves' Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Graves' Disease.; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis is possible; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Graves' Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect eyes for proptosis (exam_05) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Graves' Disease.; options Absent / Mild / Marked / Vision/exposure concern / Unable to assess; use when When Graves disease or orbitopathy is possible; technique Inspect eyes and eyelids from the front and side for lid lag/retraction, proptosis, conjunctival injection, or exposure signs.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect eyes for proptosis is supportive for Graves' Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; inspect; eyes; proptosis; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test extraocular movements (exam_06) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Graves' Disease.; options Full / Restricted / Diplopia / Painful / Unable to assess; use when When diplopia, eye pain, proptosis, or Graves orbitopathy is possible; technique Ask the patient to follow a target through cardinal gaze positions and observe for limitation, pain, diplopia, or nystagmus.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test extraocular movements is supportive for Graves' Disease, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; test; extraocular; movements; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - TRAb-positive biochemical hyperthyroidism supports Graves. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Graves' Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Graves' Disease.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; trab; positive; biochemical; hyperthyroidism; supports; graves; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Orbitopathy changes radioactive iodine risk and ophthalmology urgency. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Graves' Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Graves' Disease.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; orbitopathy; radioactive; iodine; risk; ophthalmology; urgency; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Graves' Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Graves' Disease.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Graves' Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Graves' Disease.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Graves' Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Graves' Disease.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; suppressed; elevated; thyrotoxicosis; severity; etiology; normal; triggers; central; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Graves' Disease.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Graves' Disease.; tags thyroid_disorders; graves_disease; suspicious; nodule; compressive; abnormal; node; calcitonin; concern; high; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Thyrotoxicosis safety data: check TSH, free T4, total T3, TRAb/TSI or RAIU as indicated, ECG for tachyarrhythmia, CBC/liver baseline before antithyroid drugs, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine or teratogenic treatment decisions. (test_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. Graves' Disease: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### gynecomastia_intent_v1: Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) (gynecomastia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; measure; heart; rate; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; use when During reproductive or gonadal endocrine evaluation; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale and height measurement, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; measure; body; mass; index; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options How long has breast tissue enlargement / Tenderness been present / Is it unilateral / Bilateral / Have rapid growth / Discrete mass / Skin change / Nipple discharge occurred / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; duration; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; full question How long has breast tissue enlargement or tenderness been present, is it unilateral or bilateral, and have rapid growth, discrete mass, skin change, or nipple discharge occurred?; details Ask specifically about How long has breast tissue enlargement. | Ask specifically about tenderness been present. | Ask specifically about is it unilateral. | Ask specifically about bilateral. | Ask specifically about have rapid growth. | Ask specifically about discrete mass. | Ask specifically about skin change. | Ask specifically about nipple discharge occurred.
2. Ask GU/STI symptoms and exposure risk - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; unilateral; mass; nipple; discharge; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; full question Any breast tenderness, discrete mass, nipple discharge, testicular pain or mass, fertility change, libido change, medication exposure, liver/kidney disease, alcohol, or cannabis use?; details Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about discrete mass. | Ask specifically about nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about testicular pain. | Ask specifically about mass. | Ask specifically about fertility change. | Ask specifically about libido change. | Ask specifically about medication exposure.
3. Ask epididymitis/STI and urology-risk features - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; libido; testicular; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; full question Any change in libido, morning erections, erectile function, ejaculation, orgasm, fertility goals, or testicular symptoms?; details Ask specifically about change in libido. | Ask specifically about morning erections. | Ask specifically about erectile function. | Ask specifically about ejaculation. | Ask specifically about orgasm. | Ask specifically about fertility goals. | Ask specifically about testicular symptoms.
4. Ask breast/testicular symptoms and exposure risks - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options How has height / Growth velocity changed compared with prior measurements / Family height pattern / Puberty timing / Other ___; ask when Ask when Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; puberty; timing; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; full question How has height or growth velocity changed compared with prior measurements, family height pattern, and puberty timing?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Measure testicular volume (exam_01) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options Expected volume / Small bilaterally / Asymmetric/mass concern / Unable/deferred; use when When male hypogonadism, infertility, gynecomastia, or erectile dysfunction is relevant; technique Estimate testicular volume with orchidometer when available or careful exam, documenting asymmetry, tenderness, or mass separately.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment orchidometer optional, cooperation low; limitations Measure testicular volume is supportive for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; measure; testicular; volume; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options No glandular tissue / Tender glandular tissue / Discrete mass / Nipple discharge/skin change / Unable/deferred; use when When gynecomastia or hypogonadism is relevant; technique Inspect and palpate breast tissue to distinguish glandular tissue from adiposity and to identify discrete mass, tenderness, or discharge.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect breast tissue is supportive for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; inspect; breast; tissue; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; options Expected distribution / Reduced androgen-dependent hair / Excess/virilizing pattern / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen deficiency or pubertal disorder is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect body hair distribution is supportive for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; inspect; body; hair; distribution; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Testicular mass/hCG elevation changes to urgent tumor pathway; hypogonadism pattern changes endocrine workup. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; testicular; mass; elevation; urgent; tumor; pathway; hypogonadism; pattern; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; gynecomastia; biochemical; hypogonadism; hyperprolactinemia; pcos; thyroid; counseling; bone; cardiometabolic; source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,gynecomastia,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

15. Gynecomastia safety tests: review medication/substance causes and check testosterone, estradiol, LH/FSH, beta-hCG, prolactin/TSH, liver/kidney function, and testicular evaluation when mass, rapid growth, or malignancy concern exists. (test_05) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_06) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_07) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Hard eccentric breast mass, nipple discharge, skin changes, nodes, testicular mass, rapid progression. (red_flag_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
23. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
24. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Testicular mass/hCG elevation changes to urgent tumor pathway; hypogonadism pattern changes endocrine workup. (management_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
26. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
27. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Gynecomastia (Enlarged male breast tissue): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
31. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
32. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hashimotos_thyroiditis_intent_v1: Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism) (hashimotos_thyroiditis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; measure; blood; pressure; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; measure; heart; rate; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; palpate; thyroid; gland; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant; technique Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate cervical lymph nodes is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; palpate; cervical; lymph; nodes; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid dysfunction could affect cardiac rhythm; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis is possible; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Listen to voice quality (exam_05) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options Normal voice / Hoarse / Weak/breathy / Stridor/airway concern / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, or compressive symptoms are present; technique Listen to voice quality during normal speech and ask about new hoarseness, weak voice, or compressive symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Listen to voice quality is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; listen; voice; quality; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect neck for airway narrowing (exam_06) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; options No visible narrowing / Large goiter/mass effect / Stridor/positional dyspnea / Unable to assess; use when When goiter, neck mass, dyspnea, or compressive symptoms are present; technique Inspect neck contour and observe breathing/stridor; ask about positional dyspnea while avoiding provocative maneuvers in unstable patients.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for airway narrowing is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; inspect; neck; airway; narrowing; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Positive TPO Ab supports autoimmune thyroiditis. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; positive; supports; autoimmune; thyroiditis; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Suspicious nodule/compressive findings move to ultrasound/FNA pathway. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; suspicious; nodule; compressive; findings; move; ultrasound; pathway; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; suppressed; elevated; thyrotoxicosis; severity; etiology; normal; triggers; central; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism).; tags thyroid_disorders; hashimotos_thyroiditis; suspicious; nodule; compressive; abnormal; node; calcitonin; concern; high; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. 4.
