22. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Hypothyroid safety data: check TSH/free T4 with TPO antibody when autoimmune disease matters, sodium/CK/lipids if severe or symptomatic, ECG/cardiac context for older or cardiac patients, and pregnancy-specific targets when pregnant or fertility planning. (test_05) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_06) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_07) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_02) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. Rapidly enlarging thyroid, compressive symptoms, hard fixed nodule, cervical nodes. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
33. Positive TPO Ab supports autoimmune thyroiditis. (management_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Suspicious nodule/compressive findings move to ultrasound/FNA pathway. (management_02) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Hashimoto's Thyroiditis (Autoimmune Hypothyroidism): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hirsutism_intent_v1: Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) (hirsutism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hirsutism; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Focused history questions

2. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) needs urgent evaluation today.; options When was the last menstrual period / How regular are cycles / Bleeding changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hirsutism; menstrual; irregularity; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; full question When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?
3. Ask hyperandrogenism and virilization features - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) therapy unsafe.; options No relevant exposure / Medication change / Supplement use / Missed doses / Biotin / Iodine/contrast exposure / Hormone therapy / Recent treatment change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hirsutism; medications; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; full question Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) interpretation or safety?; details Review missed or delayed doses. | Review high-dose biotin or supplement use. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy or androgen/estrogen exposure. | Review recent dose or treatment changes.
4. Ask pregnancy and ectopic danger features - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hirsutism; fertility; goals; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; full question Are fertility goals, pregnancy possibility, partner factors, or treatment choices relevant to the timing and safety of this endocrine workup?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen excess or PCOS is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hirsutism; inspect; acne; distribution; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; use when When hirsutism, PCOS, or androgen excess is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hirsutism; inspect; terminal; hair; distribution; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_03) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth).; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; use when When PCOS, insulin resistance, or hyperandrogenic metabolic risk is possible; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hirsutism; inspect; neck; acanthosis; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hirsutism,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hirsutism,measure,heart,rate; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hirsutism,measure,body,mass,index; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Interpretation caution - Inspect acne distribution (exam_01_limitation) - Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hirsutism,inspect,acne,distribution; source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

16. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Hyperandrogenism/PCOS safety tests: pregnancy test when relevant, total/free testosterone with reliable assay, DHEAS or 17-OHP for mimics, TSH/prolactin as indicated, and A1c/OGTT/lipids for cardiometabolic risk. (test_06) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Rapid onset, virilization, very high testosterone/DHEAS, pelvic/adrenal mass symptoms. (red_flag_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Tumor-range androgens or virilization changes to urgent imaging/endocrine pathway. (management_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Hirsutism (Excessive hair growth): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_HIRSUTISM_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hyperaldosteronism_intent_v1: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup

Installed module: Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome) (hyperaldosteronism_v1)

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect and press lower leg edema (exam_01) - Targets volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options None / Trace / 1+ / 2+ / 3+ / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; use when When hypertension, renal disease, or medication effects could affect volume status; technique Inspect and press over both lower legs or dependent areas for pitting, symmetry, tenderness, and proximal extent.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect and press lower leg edema is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; inspect; press; lower; edema; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Test chair-rise strength (exam_02) - Targets hypokalemic weakness or functional impairment from mineralocorticoid excess; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When hypokalemia, cramps, or weakness is possible; technique Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test chair-rise strength is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; test; chair; rise; strength; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets hypokalemia-related arrhythmia, severe hypertension complication, or ECG-relevant cardiac rhythm abnormality; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When primary aldosteronism, hypokalemia, palpitations, or severe hypertension is possible; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Suppressed renin with clearly elevated aldosterone changes to PA pathway and targeted therapy/subtyping. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; suppressed; renin; clearly; elevated; aldosterone; pathway; targeted; subtyping; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; suspected; adrenal; crisis; treated; immediately; stress; dose; steroids; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome).; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; hyperaldosteronism; marked; metanephrine; elevation; suppressed; renin; high; aldosterone; confirmed; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders,hyperaldosteronism,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

(red_flag_01) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Adrenal crisis, shock, severe vomiting/dehydration, hypoglycemia, severe hyperkalemia/hyponatremia, hypertensive crisis, arrhythmia, stroke/ACS symptoms, psychosis, infection, or VTE. (red_flag_03) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Suppressed renin with clearly elevated aldosterone changes to PA pathway and targeted therapy/subtyping. (management_01) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Hyperaldosteronism (Conn's Syndrome): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: ARR cutoffs are assay/unit/medication-specific; interpret with local lab guidance. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ES_PRIMARY_ALDO_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hyperthyroidism_intent_v1: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid) (hyperthyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; measure; blood; pressure; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; measure; heart; rate; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; palpate; thyroid; gland; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant; technique Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate cervical lymph nodes is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; palpate; cervical; lymph; nodes; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid dysfunction could affect cardiac rhythm; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis is possible; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect eyes for proptosis (exam_05) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; options Absent / Mild / Marked / Vision/exposure concern / Unable to assess; use when When Graves disease or orbitopathy is possible; technique Inspect eyes and eyelids from the front and side for lid lag/retraction, proptosis, conjunctival injection, or exposure signs.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect eyes for proptosis is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; inspect; eyes; proptosis; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; suppressed; plus; high; confirms; overt; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; suppressed; elevated; thyrotoxicosis; severity; etiology; normal; triggers; central; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hyperthyroidism; suspicious; nodule; compressive; abnormal; node; calcitonin; concern; high; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders,hyperthyroidism,measure,blood,pressure; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders,hyperthyroidism,measure,heart,rate; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders,hyperthyroidism,palpate,thyroid,gland; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

20. Interpretation caution - Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism. (management_01_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders,hyperthyroidism,suppressed,plus,high,confirms,overt; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

20. RAIU or Doppler ultrasound when etiology unclear. (test_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Thyrotoxicosis safety data: check TSH, free T4, total T3, TRAb/TSI or RAIU as indicated, ECG for tachyarrhythmia, CBC/liver baseline before antithyroid drugs, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine or teratogenic treatment decisions. (test_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_07) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Thyroid storm: fever, severe tachycardia/AF, heart failure, agitation, GI symptoms, altered mental status. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Suppressed TSH plus high FT4/T3 confirms overt hyperthyroidism. (management_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. TRAb or uptake pattern changes etiology-specific treatment. (management_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Hyperthyroidism (Overactive Thyroid): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Catalog gaps needing review
- none

### hypogonadism_intent_v1: Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men) (hypogonadism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hypogonadism; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hypogonadism; measure; heart; rate; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Measure testicular volume (exam_01) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options Expected volume / Small bilaterally / Asymmetric/mass concern / Unable/deferred; use when When male hypogonadism, infertility, gynecomastia, or erectile dysfunction is relevant; technique Estimate testicular volume with orchidometer when available or careful exam, documenting asymmetry, tenderness, or mass separately.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment orchidometer optional, cooperation low; limitations Measure testicular volume is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hypogonadism; measure; testicular; volume; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect breast tissue (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options No glandular tissue / Tender glandular tissue / Discrete mass / Nipple discharge/skin change / Unable/deferred; use when When gynecomastia or hypogonadism is relevant; technique Inspect and palpate breast tissue to distinguish glandular tissue from adiposity and to identify discrete mass, tenderness, or discharge.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect breast tissue is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hypogonadism; inspect; breast; tissue; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect body hair distribution (exam_03) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men).; options Expected distribution / Reduced androgen-dependent hair / Excess/virilizing pattern / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen deficiency or pubertal disorder is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect body hair distribution is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; hypogonadism; inspect; body; hair; distribution; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Fertility plans change away from exogenous testosterone; pituitary signs trigger MRI/endocrine referral. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hypogonadism,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hypogonadism,measure,heart,rate; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hypogonadism,measure,body,mass,index; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Interpretation caution - Measure testicular volume (exam_01_limitation) - Measure testicular volume is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Measure testicular volume is supportive for Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,hypogonadism,measure,testicular,volume; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
