19. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. New severe headache/vision loss, very low T with low/normal LH/FSH, testicular mass, infertility priority. (red_flag_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Fertility plans change away from exogenous testosterone; pituitary signs trigger MRI/endocrine referral. (management_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Hypogonadism (Low Testosterone in Men): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source AUA_TESTOSTERONE_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hypoparathyroidism_intent_v1: Hypoparathyroidism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypoparathyroidism (hypoparathyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; measure; blood; pressure; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypoparathyroidism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; measure; heart; rate; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When osteomalacia, hypercalcemia, hypocalcemia, falls, or weakness is possible; technique Test hip flexion strength against resistance bilaterally while the patient is seated or supine, adapting to pain and safety.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; test; proximal; flexor; strength; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When bone pain, osteomalacia, fracture, or hyperparathyroidism is possible; technique Palpate the symptomatic bone or spine area gently for focal tenderness and correlate with trauma, fracture risk, and neurologic symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; palpate; focal; bone; tenderness; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When falls, fracture risk, weakness, or bone disease is relevant; technique Observe standing and gait only if safe, noting balance, antalgia, assistive-device need, and fall risk.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe gait stability if safe is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; observe; gait; stability; safe; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options No kyphosis/height-loss concern / Kyphosis / Height-loss concern / Unable to assess; use when When osteoporosis or vertebral compression fracture risk is relevant; technique Inspect standing posture and spinal curvature, noting kyphosis, height-loss concern, or vertebral compression pattern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; inspect; spine; posture; kyphosis; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Elicit Chvostek sign (exam_05) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options Negative / Positive / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When symptomatic hypocalcemia is possible; technique Tap over the facial nerve anterior to the ear and observe for ipsilateral facial twitching, interpreting cautiously because false positives occur.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Elicit Chvostek sign is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; elicit; chvostek; sign; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Elicit Trousseau sign (exam_06) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypoparathyroidism.; options Negative / Positive / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When symptomatic hypocalcemia is possible and safe to perform; technique Inflate a blood-pressure cuff above systolic pressure for up to three minutes if safe and observe for carpopedal spasm.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Elicit Trousseau sign is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Symptomatic or severe hypocalcemia changes to urgent calcium replacement and ECG monitoring. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypoparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypoparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; symptomatic; hypocalcemia; urgent; calcium; replacement; monitoring; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypoparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypoparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypoparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypoparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypoparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypoparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; fragility; fracture; very; score; high; risk; secondary; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypoparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypoparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; hypoparathyroidism; calcium; pattern; renal; involvement; stones; osteoporosis; surgical; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,hypoparathyroidism,measure,blood,pressure; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,hypoparathyroidism,measure,heart,rate; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01_limitation) - Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Hypoparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,hypoparathyroidism,test,proximal,flexor,strength; source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

20. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. 21. Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_05) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool when bone density is in scope; calcium/PTH/phosphorus/magnesium/25(OH)D/renal function for calcium-bone disorders. (test_06) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging when primary hyperparathyroidism, kidney stones, or chronic hypoparathyroidism management is relevant. (test_07) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. (reference_01) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Hypoparathyroidism: low calcium with low or inappropriately normal PTH, often high phosphorus. (reference_05) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_06) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. Seizure, laryngospasm, arrhythmia/QT prolongation, severe symptomatic hypocalcemia. (red_flag_01) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Hip/vertebral fracture, cord/neurologic symptoms with back pain, severe hypercalcemia with dehydration/AKI/confusion, or symptomatic hypocalcemia with seizure/laryngospasm/arrhythmia. (red_flag_03) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
33. Symptomatic or severe hypocalcemia changes to urgent calcium replacement and ECG monitoring. (management_01) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_04) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_05) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Hypoparathyroidism: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03) - source HYPOPARA_TASK_FORCE_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hypopituitarism_intent_v1: Hypopituitarism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypopituitarism (hypopituitarism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypopituitarism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; measure; blood; pressure; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypopituitarism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; measure; heart; rate; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask hypothyroid symptoms and menstrual pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Fatigue / Cold intolerance / Constipation / Dry skin / Hoarse voice / Slowed thinking / Depression / Heavy menses / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; fatigue; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism?; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
2. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; options When was the last menstrual period / How regular are cycles / Bleeding changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; amenorrhea; libido; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?
3. Ask diabetes-insipidus thirst and polyuria safety - urinary/flank symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; options How much are you drinking / Urinating / Including polyuria / Polydipsia / Overnight urination / Thirst intensity / Access to water / Any dehydration / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; polyuria; polydipsia; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion?; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
4. Ask pituitary mass-effect and treatment history - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / New headache / Peripheral vision loss / Double vision / Eye movement pain / Sudden severe headache / Nausea / Pituitary surgery/radiation history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; headache; vision; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history?; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
5. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypopituitarism therapy unsafe.; options No / Spontaneous / Expressible nipple discharge / Breast tenderness / Headaches / Visual symptoms / Medications that raise prolactin / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; galactorrhea; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin?; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
6. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; options Have ring size / Shoe size / Facial features / Jaw spacing / Sweating / Sleep apnea / Joint pain / Headaches changed over time / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; growth; acral; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question Have ring size, shoe size, facial features, jaw spacing, sweating, sleep apnea, joint pain, or headaches changed over time?; details Ask specifically about Have ring size. | Ask specifically about shoe size. | Ask specifically about facial features. | Ask specifically about jaw spacing. | Ask specifically about sweating. | Ask specifically about sleep apnea. | Ask specifically about joint pain. | Ask specifically about headaches changed over time.
7. Ask thyroid nodule, pain, and compressive symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis / Salt craving / Hyperpigmentation / Unexplained weight loss / Thyroid symptoms such as palpitations / Tremor / Heat/cold intolerance / Bowel change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; adrenal; thyroid; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question Any adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis, salt craving, hyperpigmentation, unexplained weight loss, or thyroid symptoms such as palpitations, tremor, heat/cold intolerance, bowel change, or neck swelling?; details Ask specifically about adrenal symptoms such as orthostasis. | Ask specifically about salt craving. | Ask specifically about hyperpigmentation. | Ask specifically about unexplained weight loss. | Ask specifically about thyroid symptoms such as palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat/cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about bowel change.
8. Ask prolactin symptoms and medication causes - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Hypopituitarism therapy unsafe.; options No relevant exposure / Medication change / Supplement use / Missed doses / Biotin / Iodine/contrast exposure / Hormone therapy / Recent treatment change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; medication; causes; prolactin; elevation; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Hypopituitarism interpretation or safety?; details Review missed or delayed doses. | Review high-dose biotin or supplement use. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy or androgen/estrogen exposure. | Review recent dose or treatment changes.
9. Ask diabetes-insipidus thirst and polyuria safety - Do you have reliable access - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypopituitarism needs urgent evaluation today.; options Do you have reliable access to water / Are thirst / Intake / Mental status limiting safe hydration / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypopituitarism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; access; water; desmopressin; diabetes; insipidus; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; full question Do you have reliable access to water, and are thirst, intake, or mental status limiting safe hydration?

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Hypopituitarism.; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; use when When pituitary mass, macroadenoma, headache, or visual symptoms are relevant; technique Test each eye separately by confrontation, comparing peripheral fields with the examiner's field while the patient fixes on a central target.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Hypopituitarism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; test; confrontation; visual; fields; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypopituitarism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypopituitarism.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; central; adrenal; insufficiency; must; addressed; before; thyroid; hormone; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypopituitarism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypopituitarism.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; central; adrenal; insufficiency; must; addressed; before; thyroid; hormone; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypopituitarism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypopituitarism.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; hypopituitarism; confirmed; hormone; excess; deficiency; plus; finding; neurosurgical; fertility; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,hypopituitarism,measure,blood,pressure; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders,hypopituitarism,measure,heart,rate; source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Hypopituitarism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. 22. Pituitary safety tests: check 8 AM cortisol/ACTH before thyroid replacement decisions, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, sodium/osmolality, and MRI/visual-field pathway when mass effect is possible. (test_07) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_08) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated. (test_09) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. (reference_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (reference_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Sodium about 135-145 mmol/L; potassium about 3.5-5.0 mmol/L. (reference_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_06) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_07) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
32. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_08) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_09) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
34. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_10) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
35. Adrenal crisis, pituitary apoplexy, visual loss, severe hyponatremia. (red_flag_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
37. Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy. (red_flag_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
38. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation. (management_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
41. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
42. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
43. Hypopituitarism: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
44. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: 8 AM cortisol <3 ug/dL strongly suggests adrenal insufficiency; >15-18 ug/dL often makes it unlikely, context dependent. Cosyntropin peak cortisol <18 ug/dL is a traditional abnormal cutoff; modern assays may use about 14-15 ug/dL. (differential_02) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
45. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPOPITUITARISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### hypothyroidism_intent_v1: Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) (hypothyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; measure; blood; pressure; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; measure; heart; rate; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask hypothyroid symptoms and menstrual pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Fatigue / Cold intolerance / Constipation / Dry skin / Hoarse voice / Slowed thinking / Depression / Heavy menses / Other ___; ask when Ask when Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; fatigue; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; full question Any fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation, dry skin, hoarse voice, slowed thinking, depression, heavy menses, or weight gain suggesting hypothyroidism?; details Ask specifically about fatigue. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about dry skin. | Ask specifically about hoarse voice. | Ask specifically about slowed thinking. | Ask specifically about depression. | Ask specifically about heavy menses.
2. Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; palpate; thyroid; gland; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant; technique Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate cervical lymph nodes is supportive for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; palpate; cervical; lymph; nodes; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid dysfunction could affect cardiac rhythm; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis is possible; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - High TSH plus low FT4 confirms overt primary hypothyroidism. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; high; plus; confirms; overt; primary; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism/pituitary pathway. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; normal; triggers; central; pituitary; pathway; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; suppressed; elevated; thyrotoxicosis; severity; etiology; normal; triggers; central; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders; hypothyroidism; suspicious; nodule; compressive; abnormal; node; calcitonin; concern; high; source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders,hypothyroidism,measure,blood,pressure; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders,hypothyroidism,measure,heart,rate; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. 18. Interpretation caution - High TSH plus low FT4 confirms overt primary hypothyroidism. (management_01_limitation) - This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid).; tags thyroid_disorders,hypothyroidism,high,plus,confirms,overt,primary; source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
19. Interpretation caution - Low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism/pituitary pathway.
