16. TPO antibody for autoimmune etiology. (test_02) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Pregnancy test when relevant; pituitary/adrenal evaluation if central pattern. (test_03) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Hypothyroid safety data: check TSH/free T4 with TPO antibody when autoimmune disease matters, sodium/CK/lipids if severe or symptomatic, ECG/cardiac context for older or cardiac patients, and pregnancy-specific targets when pregnant or fertility planning. (test_05) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_06) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_07) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_02) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_03) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Myxedema coma features: hypothermia, bradycardia, hypotension, hypoventilation, hyponatremia, altered mental status. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. High TSH plus low FT4 confirms overt primary hypothyroidism. (management_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism/pituitary pathway. (management_02) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Hypothyroidism (Underactive Thyroid): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source AACE_ATA_HYPOTHYROIDISM_2012; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### infertility_intent_v1: Infertility (Hormonal causes)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Infertility (Hormonal causes) (infertility_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; measure; blood; pressure; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; measure; heart; rate; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure body mass index (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options ___ kg/m2 / Underweight / Overweight/obesity range / Unable/not available; use when During reproductive or gonadal endocrine evaluation; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale and height measurement, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; measure; body; mass; index; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - When was the last menstrual - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Infertility (Hormonal causes) needs urgent evaluation today.; options When was the last menstrual period / How regular are cycles / Bleeding changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; cycle; regularity; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?
2. Ask ovulation pattern and cycle tracking - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Infertility (Hormonal causes) needs urgent evaluation today.; options Are cycles predictably ovulatory / With mid-cycle symptoms / LH-kit evidence / Luteal symptoms / Cycle tracking suggesting regular ovulation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; ovulation; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Are cycles predictably ovulatory, with mid-cycle symptoms, LH-kit evidence, luteal symptoms, or cycle tracking suggesting regular ovulation?
3. Ask partner semen analysis and fertility evaluation - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; partner; semen; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Has a semen analysis or partner fertility evaluation been completed, and were any abnormalities found?
4. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - prolactin symptoms/medications - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; options No / Spontaneous / Expressible nipple discharge / Breast tenderness / Headaches / Visual symptoms / Medications that raise prolactin / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; galactorrhea; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any spontaneous or expressible nipple discharge, breast tenderness, headaches, visual symptoms, or medications that raise prolactin?; details Ask specifically about spontaneous. | Ask specifically about expressible nipple discharge. | Ask specifically about breast tenderness. | Ask specifically about headaches. | Ask specifically about visual symptoms. | Ask specifically about medications that raise prolactin.
5. Ask thyroid nodule, pain, and compressive symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Infertility (Hormonal causes) needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Palpitations / Tremor / Heat / Cold intolerance / Constipation / Diarrhea / Weight change / Fatigue / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; thyroid; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any palpitations, tremor, heat or cold intolerance, constipation or diarrhea, weight change, fatigue, mood/cognitive change, neck swelling, or eye symptoms?; details Ask specifically about palpitations. | Ask specifically about tremor. | Ask specifically about heat. | Ask specifically about cold intolerance. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about diarrhea. | Ask specifically about weight change. | Ask specifically about fatigue.
6. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - hirsutism - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; options No / Hirsutism / Acne / Scalp hair loss / Deepening voice / Clitoromegaly / Rapid virilization / Cycle irregularity / Androgenic medication exposure / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; hyperandrogenism; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any hirsutism, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, rapid virilization, cycle irregularity, or androgenic medication exposure?; details Ask specifically about hirsutism. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about rapid virilization. | Ask specifically about cycle irregularity. | Ask specifically about androgenic medication exposure.
7. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - prolactin symptoms/medications 2 - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; loss; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any prior pregnancy loss, recurrent loss pattern, infertility evaluation, cycle abnormality, thyroid/prolactin issue, or uterine/ovulatory diagnosis affecting fertility planning?; details Ask specifically about prior pregnancy loss. | Ask specifically about recurrent loss pattern. | Ask specifically about infertility evaluation. | Ask specifically about cycle abnormality. | Ask specifically about thyroid/prolactin issue. | Ask specifically about uterine/ovulatory diagnosis.
8. Ask PID and pelvic infection features - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options No / New / Rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth / Acne / Scalp hair loss / Deepening voice / Clitoromegaly / Menstrual irregularity / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; hirsutism; acne; alopecia; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity?; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.
9. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - pregnancy/fertility safety - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Infertility (Hormonal causes) therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Infertility (Hormonal causes) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; flashes; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; full question Any hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, vaginal/genitourinary symptoms, mood change, cycle change, or fertility goal affecting reproductive endocrine management?; details Ask specifically about hot flashes. | Ask specifically about night sweats. | Ask specifically about sleep disruption. | Ask specifically about vaginal/genitourinary symptoms. | Ask specifically about mood change. | Ask specifically about cycle change. | Ask specifically about fertility goal.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen excess or PCOS is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Infertility (Hormonal causes), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; inspect; acne; distribution; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; use when When hirsutism, PCOS, or androgen excess is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Infertility (Hormonal causes), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; inspect; terminal; hair; distribution; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_03) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; use when When PCOS, insulin resistance, or hyperandrogenic metabolic risk is possible; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Infertility (Hormonal causes), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; inspect; neck; acanthosis; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect thyroid gland (exam_04) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when When menstrual, fertility, or menopausal symptoms could reflect thyroid disease; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect thyroid gland is supportive for Infertility (Hormonal causes), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; inspect; thyroid; gland; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect for galactorrhea (exam_05) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; options Absent / Spontaneous discharge / Expressible discharge / Bloody/unilateral discharge / Unable/deferred; use when When amenorrhea, infertility, breast symptoms, or prolactin excess is possible; technique Inspect for spontaneous discharge and, only when clinically appropriate, gently express from the areola toward the nipple while preserving privacy and consent.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect for galactorrhea is supportive for Infertility (Hormonal causes), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; inspect; galactorrhea; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Anovulation, thyroid/prolactin abnormality, POI pattern, or male factor changes treatment/referral. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; anovulation; thyroid; prolactin; abnormality; pattern; male; factor; referral; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; pregnancy; fertility; intent; rapid; virilization; tumor; range; hormones; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Infertility (Hormonal causes).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; infertility; biochemical; hypogonadism; hyperprolactinemia; pcos; thyroid; counseling; bone; cardiometabolic; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,infertility,measure,blood,pressure; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,infertility,measure,heart,rate; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure body mass index (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,infertility,measure,body,mass,index; source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

24. Infertility hormone safety tests: pregnancy test when relevant, ovulatory timing, TSH/prolactin, LH/FSH with estradiol or testosterone, AMH/ovarian reserve or semen analysis as appropriate, and urgent ectopic/red-flag testing for pain or bleeding. (test_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_08) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_09) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_05) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
32. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_06) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
33. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_07) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
34. Amenorrhea with visual symptoms, POI <40, severe hyperandrogenism, recurrent pregnancy loss needing specialist workup. (red_flag_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
35. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
37. Anovulation, thyroid/prolactin abnormality, POI pattern, or male factor changes treatment/referral. (management_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
41. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
42. Infertility (Hormonal causes): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
43. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
44. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source ES_TESTOSTERONE_2018; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Catalog gaps needing review
- none

### menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency_intent_v1: Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency (menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency; measure; blood; pressure; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen excess or PCOS is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency; inspect; acne; distribution; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; use when When hirsutism, PCOS, or androgen excess is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency; inspect; terminal; hair; distribution; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect thyroid gland (exam_03) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when When menstrual, fertility, or menopausal symptoms could reflect thyroid disease; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect thyroid gland is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency; inspect; thyroid; gland; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect for galactorrhea (exam_04) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; options Absent / Spontaneous discharge / Expressible discharge / Bloody/unilateral discharge / Unable/deferred; use when When amenorrhea, infertility, breast symptoms, or prolactin excess is possible; technique Inspect for spontaneous discharge and, only when clinically appropriate, gently express from the areola toward the nipple while preserving privacy and consent.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect for galactorrhea is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency; inspect; galactorrhea; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - POI changes routine menopause counseling to etiologic evaluation, fertility counseling, and bone/cardiovascular prevention. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency; routine; menopause; counseling; etiologic; evaluation; fertility; bone; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency,measure,blood,pressure; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders,menopause_premature_ovarian_insufficiency,measure,heart,rate; source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

(test_04) - source MENOPAUSE_SOCIETY_HT_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. POI/menopause safety tests: pregnancy test when uncertain, FSH/estradiol confirmation for POI, TSH/prolactin when cycles are abnormal, and bone/cardiometabolic risk labs that affect hormone therapy and prevention decisions. (test_06) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07) - source IMS_MENOPAUSE_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source MENOPAUSE_SOCIETY_HT_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source IMS_MENOPAUSE_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source MENOPAUSE_SOCIETY_HT_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Pregnancy, heavy bleeding, pelvic mass symptoms, POI <40 needing bone/cardiovascular/fertility counseling. (red_flag_01) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. POI changes routine menopause counseling to etiologic evaluation, fertility counseling, and bone/cardiovascular prevention. (management_01) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source IMS_MENOPAUSE_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Pregnancy, fertility intent, rapid virilization, tumor-range hormones, or pituitary symptoms changes medication safety, imaging urgency, and referral pathway. (management_04) - source MENOPAUSE_SOCIETY_HT_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Biochemical hypogonadism, POI, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, or thyroid disease changes counseling, bone/cardiometabolic prevention, and treatment selection. (management_05) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ESHRE_POI_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
38. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ASRM_AMENORRHEA_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
39. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source IMS_MENOPAUSE_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Catalog gaps needing review
- none

### metabolic_syndrome_intent_v1: Metabolic Syndrome

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Metabolic Syndrome (metabolic_syndrome_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; measure; blood; pressure; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; measure; heart; rate; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; use when During diabetes or metabolic risk assessment; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; measure; weight; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Metabolic Syndrome.; options ___ cm/in / Above risk threshold / Not above risk threshold / Unable/not available; use when When cardiometabolic risk or metabolic syndrome is being assessed; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment tape measure, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; measure; waist; circumference; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask how have weight - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; options How have weight / Waist circumference changed over time / The change intentional / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; weight; waist; trajectory; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question How have weight and waist circumference changed over time, and is the change intentional?
2. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Loud snoring / Witnessed apneas / Morning headaches / Daytime sleepiness / Resistant hypertension / CPAP / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; sleep; apnea; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use?; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
3. Ask physical activity level and treatment barriers - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; options What is the usual physical activity level / It changed recently because of symptoms / Function / Treatment barriers / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; activity; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question What is the usual physical activity level, and has it changed recently because of symptoms, function, or treatment barriers?
4. Ask ASCVD, heart failure, and vascular disease history - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Metabolic Syndrome therapy unsafe.; options No / History of ASCVD / Heart failure / Stroke/TIA / Peripheral arterial disease / Claudication / Vascular procedures / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; family; ascvd; diabetes; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question Any history of ASCVD, heart failure, stroke/TIA, peripheral arterial disease, claudication, or vascular procedures?; details Ask specifically about history of ASCVD. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about stroke/TIA. | Ask specifically about peripheral arterial disease. | Ask specifically about claudication. | Ask specifically about vascular procedures.
5. Ask PCOS and reproductive-metabolic history - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Metabolic Syndrome.; options No / History of PCOS that changes Metabolic Syndrome risk / Test interpretation / Treatment safety / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; pcos; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question Any history of PCOS that changes Metabolic Syndrome risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety?
6. Ask euglycemic DKA and special-population risks - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Metabolic Syndrome.; options No / Fatty liver disease / Elevated liver enzymes / Central adiposity / Dyslipidemia / Hypertension / Insulin resistance / Alcohol / Cardiometabolic treatment history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; fatty; liver; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question Any known fatty liver disease, elevated liver enzymes, central adiposity, dyslipidemia, hypertension, insulin resistance, alcohol use, or cardiometabolic treatment history?; details Ask specifically about known fatty liver disease. | Ask specifically about elevated liver enzymes. | Ask specifically about central adiposity. | Ask specifically about dyslipidemia. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about insulin resistance. | Ask specifically about alcohol use. | Ask specifically about cardiometabolic treatment history.
7. Review cardiometabolic medications and adverse effects - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Metabolic Syndrome therapy unsafe.; options What blood-pressure / Lipid / Kidney-protective / Cardiometabolic medications are being used / Are there adherence / Adverse-effect concerns / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; lipid; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question What blood-pressure, lipid, kidney-protective, or cardiometabolic medications are being used, and are there adherence or adverse-effect concerns?
8. Ask home glucose pattern and baseline change - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Metabolic Syndrome needs urgent evaluation today.; options What is the current weight trajectory / How has it changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; weight; trajectory; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question What is the current weight trajectory, and how has it changed from baseline?
9. Ask prior gestational diabetes and macrosomia history - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Metabolic Syndrome.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Metabolic Syndrome is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; gestational; diabetes; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; full question Any history of prior gestational diabetes that changes Metabolic Syndrome risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety?; details Ask specifically about history of prior gestational diabetes.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Metabolic Syndrome.; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; use when When insulin resistance or metabolic syndrome is suspected; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Metabolic Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; metabolic_syndrome; inspect; neck; acanthosis; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

- none

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

1. Management implication - 3 or more criteria shifts to intensive cardiometabolic risk reduction. 6. Management implication - Diabetes-range glycemia, ASCVD/HF/CKD risk, fatty liver, obstructive sleep apnea, PCOS, or pregnancy context changes diagnostic classification, treatment safety, and referral or follow-up urgency. Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,metabolic_syndrome,measure,blood,pressure; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,metabolic_syndrome,measure,heart,rate; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,metabolic_syndrome,measure,weight; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Interpretation caution - Physical exam scope for Metabolic Syndrome (metabolic_syndrome_v1_physical_exam_scope) - This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. Check basic bedside data and anthropometrics, document the focused insulin-resistance phenotype exam when relevant, and add foot/vascular, hydration/perfusion, abdominal, cardiopulmonary, or neurologic exams only when symptoms, complications, or a second validated intent trigger them.; limitations This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. Check basic bedside data and anthropometrics, document the focused insulin-resistance phenotype exam when relevant, and add foot/vascular, hydration/perfusion, abdominal, cardiopulmonary, or neurologic exams only when symptoms, complications, or a second validated intent trigger them.; tags exam_scope,exam_light,endocrine,laboratory_guided,diagnostic_safety; source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to exam-scope cautions; use this row to audit why the workup remains focused rather than comprehensive-by-default.

22. Diagnosis commonly requires 3 or more: waist >102 cm men or >88 cm women in ATP III US cut points, TG >=150, HDL <40 men/<50 women, BP >=130/85 or treatment, fasting glucose >=100 or treatment. (reference_01) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_02) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Diabetes-range glycemia, severe hypertension, ASCVD symptoms. (red_flag_01) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.
