26. Diabetes-range glycemia with classic symptoms, severe hypertension, ASCVD symptoms, pregnancy-context hyperglycemia, or dehydration requires escalation beyond routine prevention care. (red_flag_03) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. 3 or more criteria shifts to intensive cardiometabolic risk reduction. (management_01) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Diabetes thresholds change diagnosis. (management_02) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Markedly abnormal glucose, severe hypertension, pregnancy-context concern, or cardiopulmonary symptoms changes disposition from prevention visit to urgent evaluation. (management_03) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Prediabetes or metabolic syndrome thresholds change prevention intensity, lifestyle intervention, cardiometabolic risk-factor treatment, and surveillance interval. (management_05) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Diabetes-range glycemia, ASCVD/HF/CKD risk, fatty liver, obstructive sleep apnea, PCOS, or pregnancy context changes diagnostic classification, treatment safety, and referral or follow-up urgency. (management_06) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Metabolic Syndrome: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source NHLBI_METABOLIC_SYNDROME; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Diagnosis commonly requires 3 or more: waist >102 cm men or >88 cm women in ATP III US cut points, TG >=150, HDL <40 men/<50 women, BP >=130/85 or treatment, fasting glucose >=100 or treatment. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### osteopenia_intent_v1: Osteopenia

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Osteopenia (osteopenia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; measure; blood; pressure; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Osteopenia.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; measure; heart; rate; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask fracture, fall, and vertebral-compression symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteopenia needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Low-trauma fracture / Focal bone pain / Height loss / Falls / Gait instability / Back pain / Glucocorticoid / Malabsorption risk / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteopenia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; fracture; fall; risk; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any low-trauma fracture, focal bone pain, height loss, falls, gait instability, back pain, glucocorticoid use, or malabsorption risk?; details Ask specifically about low-trauma fracture. | Ask specifically about focal bone pain. | Ask specifically about height loss. | Ask specifically about falls. | Ask specifically about gait instability. | Ask specifically about back pain. | Ask specifically about glucocorticoid use. | Ask specifically about malabsorption risk.
2. Ask vasomotor and genitourinary symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Osteopenia.; options No relevant exposure / Medication change / Supplement use / Missed doses / Biotin / Iodine/contrast exposure / Hormone therapy / Recent treatment change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteopenia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; menopause; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question What was the menopause or ovarian-insufficiency timing, and are there fragility fracture, vasomotor, genitourinary, or hormone-therapy considerations?; details Ask specifically about What was the menopause. | Ask specifically about ovarian-insufficiency timing. | Ask specifically about are there fragility fracture. | Ask specifically about vasomotor. | Ask specifically about genitourinary. | Ask specifically about hormone-therapy considerations.
3. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - kidney stones - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteopenia needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Kidney stones / Nephrocalcinosis / Hematuria / Flank pain / Recurrent UTIs / Polyuria / Dehydration / Renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteopenia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; stones; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any kidney stones, nephrocalcinosis, hematuria, flank pain, recurrent UTIs, polyuria, dehydration, or renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
4. Ask hypocalcemia neuromuscular symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Osteopenia therapy unsafe.; options No / Antiseizure / Enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism / Bone density / Calcium interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteopenia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; anticonvulsants; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any antiseizure or enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism, bone density, or calcium interpretation?; details Ask specifically about antiseizure. | Ask specifically about enzyme-inducing medication use.
5. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - calcium-symptom pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteopenia needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Kidney stones / Constipation / Polyuria / Polydipsia / Bone pain / Confusion / Dehydration / Reduced kidney function / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteopenia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; hypo; hypercalcemia; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function?; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When osteomalacia, hypercalcemia, hypocalcemia, falls, or weakness is possible; technique Test hip flexion strength against resistance bilaterally while the patient is seated or supine, adapting to pain and safety.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; test; proximal; flexor; strength; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When bone pain, osteomalacia, fracture, or hyperparathyroidism is possible; technique Palpate the symptomatic bone or spine area gently for focal tenderness and correlate with trauma, fracture risk, and neurologic symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; palpate; focal; bone; tenderness; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When falls, fracture risk, weakness, or bone disease is relevant; technique Observe standing and gait only if safe, noting balance, antalgia, assistive-device need, and fall risk.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe gait stability if safe is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; observe; gait; stability; safe; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteopenia.; options No kyphosis/height-loss concern / Kyphosis / Height-loss concern / Unable to assess; use when When osteoporosis or vertebral compression fracture risk is relevant; technique Inspect standing posture and spinal curvature, noting kyphosis, height-loss concern, or vertebral compression pattern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Osteopenia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; inspect; spine; posture; kyphosis; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Osteopenia plus high fracture risk or fragility fracture can warrant pharmacologic therapy. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteopenia.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteopenia.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; plus; high; fracture; risk; fragility; warrant; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteopenia.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteopenia.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteopenia.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteopenia.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteopenia.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteopenia.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; fragility; fracture; very; score; high; risk; secondary; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteopenia.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteopenia.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteopenia; calcium; pattern; renal; involvement; stones; osteoporosis; surgical; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_05) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool when bone density is in scope; calcium/PTH/phosphorus/magnesium/25(OH)D/renal function for calcium-bone disorders. (test_06) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging when primary hyperparathyroidism, kidney stones, or chronic hypoparathyroidism management is relevant. (test_07) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. (reference_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_05) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Fragility fracture despite osteopenic T-score, rapid bone loss, secondary cause signs. (red_flag_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. Hip/vertebral fracture, cord/neurologic symptoms with back pain, severe hypercalcemia with dehydration/AKI/confusion, or symptomatic hypocalcemia with seizure/laryngospasm/arrhythmia. (red_flag_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Osteopenia plus high fracture risk or fragility fracture can warrant pharmacologic therapy. (management_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_04) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. ### osteoporosis_intent_v1: Osteoporosis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Osteoporosis (osteoporosis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; measure; blood; pressure; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Osteoporosis.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; measure; heart; rate; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask fracture, fall, and vertebral-compression symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Osteoporosis therapy unsafe.; options No / Height loss / New back pain / Kyphosis / Low-trauma fracture / Fall / Neurologic symptom / Steroid exposure suggesting vertebral compression / Bone fragility / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteoporosis is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; height; loss; back; pain; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any height loss, new back pain, kyphosis, low-trauma fracture, fall, neurologic symptom, or steroid exposure suggesting vertebral compression or bone fragility?; details Ask specifically about height loss. | Ask specifically about new back pain. | Ask specifically about kyphosis. | Ask specifically about low-trauma fracture. | Ask specifically about fall. | Ask specifically about neurologic symptom. | Ask specifically about steroid exposure.
2. Ask hypocalcemia neuromuscular symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Osteoporosis therapy unsafe.; options No / Aromatase inhibitor / Androgen-deprivation therapy / Glucocorticoid / Antiseizure medication / PPI / Transplant medication / Other bone-active medication exposure / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteoporosis is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; aromatase; inhibitors; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any aromatase inhibitor, androgen-deprivation therapy, glucocorticoid, antiseizure medication, PPI, transplant medication, or other bone-active medication exposure?; details Ask specifically about aromatase inhibitor. | Ask specifically about androgen-deprivation therapy. | Ask specifically about glucocorticoid. | Ask specifically about antiseizure medication. | Ask specifically about transplant medication. | Ask specifically about other bone-active medication exposure.
3. Ask vasomotor and genitourinary symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Osteoporosis.; options No relevant exposure / Medication change / Supplement use / Missed doses / Biotin / Iodine/contrast exposure / Hormone therapy / Recent treatment change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteoporosis is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; menopause; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question What was the menopause or ovarian-insufficiency timing, and are there fragility fracture, vasomotor, genitourinary, or hormone-therapy considerations?; details Ask specifically about What was the menopause. | Ask specifically about ovarian-insufficiency timing. | Ask specifically about are there fragility fracture. | Ask specifically about vasomotor. | Ask specifically about genitourinary. | Ask specifically about hormone-therapy considerations.
4. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - kidney stones - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteoporosis needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Kidney stones / Nephrocalcinosis / Hematuria / Flank pain / Recurrent UTIs / Polyuria / Dehydration / Renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteoporosis is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; stones; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any kidney stones, nephrocalcinosis, hematuria, flank pain, recurrent UTIs, polyuria, dehydration, or renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
5. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - calcium-symptom pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Osteoporosis needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Kidney stones / Constipation / Polyuria / Polydipsia / Bone pain / Confusion / Dehydration / Reduced kidney function / Other ___; ask when Ask when Osteoporosis is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; hypo; hypercalcemia; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; full question Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function?; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When osteomalacia, hypercalcemia, hypocalcemia, falls, or weakness is possible; technique Test hip flexion strength against resistance bilaterally while the patient is seated or supine, adapting to pain and safety.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; test; proximal; flexor; strength; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When bone pain, osteomalacia, fracture, or hyperparathyroidism is possible; technique Palpate the symptomatic bone or spine area gently for focal tenderness and correlate with trauma, fracture risk, and neurologic symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; palpate; focal; bone; tenderness; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When falls, fracture risk, weakness, or bone disease is relevant; technique Observe standing and gait only if safe, noting balance, antalgia, assistive-device need, and fall risk.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe gait stability if safe is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; observe; gait; stability; safe; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Osteoporosis.; options No kyphosis/height-loss concern / Kyphosis / Height-loss concern / Unable to assess; use when When osteoporosis or vertebral compression fracture risk is relevant; technique Inspect standing posture and spinal curvature, noting kyphosis, height-loss concern, or vertebral compression pattern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; inspect; spine; posture; kyphosis; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - T-score <= -2.5 or fragility fracture confirms osteoporosis. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteoporosis.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; score; fragility; fracture; confirms; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Hip/vertebral fracture or very low T-score changes to high/very-high-risk pathway. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteoporosis.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; vertebral; fracture; very; score; high; risk; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteoporosis.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteoporosis.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteoporosis.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; fragility; fracture; very; score; high; risk; secondary; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Osteoporosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Osteoporosis.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; osteoporosis; calcium; pattern; renal; involvement; stones; surgical; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,measure,blood,pressure; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,measure,heart,rate; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01_limitation) - Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,test,proximal,flexor,strength; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Interpretation caution - Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02_limitation) - Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,palpate,focal,bone,tenderness; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Interpretation caution - Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03_limitation) - Observe gait stability if safe is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Observe gait stability if safe is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,observe,gait,stability,safe; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Interpretation caution - Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04_limitation) - Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; limitations Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Osteoporosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,inspect,spine,posture,kyphosis; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
7. Interpretation caution - DXA and vertebral imaging when indicated. (test_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,vertebral,imaging,indicated; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
8. Interpretation caution - Calcium (test_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,calcium,phosphorus,creatinine,egfr,alkaline,phosphatase,secondary,cause; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
9. Interpretation caution - Use local assay methods (test_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,compare,results,repeat,discordant,nonurgent,tests,using,correct; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
10. Interpretation caution - Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 (test_04_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,thyroid,pair,free,specific,such,antibodies,ultrasound,radionuclide; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
11. Interpretation caution - DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool (test_05_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,vertebral,imaging,fracture,risk,tool,bone,density,scope; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
12. Interpretation caution - 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging (test_06_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,hour,urine,calcium,renal,imaging,primary,hyperparathyroidism,kidney; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
13. Interpretation caution - Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,corrected,calcium,often,about,ionized,mmol; source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Interpretation caution - Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Osteoporosis test or threshold.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,osteoporosis,intact,often,about,assay,specific; source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Interpretation caution - DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. 12. DXA and vertebral imaging when indicated. (test_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
13. Calcium, phosphorus, creatinine/eGFR, alkaline phosphatase, 25(OH)D, PTH, TSH/CBC/CMP and secondary-cause labs as indicated. (test_02) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
14. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_04) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool when bone density is in scope; calcium/PTH/phosphorus/magnesium/25(OH)D/renal function for calcium-bone disorders. (test_05) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging when primary hyperparathyroidism, kidney stones, or chronic hypoparathyroidism management is relevant. (test_06) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. (reference_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_05) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. New severe back pain, neurologic symptoms, hip fracture, multiple fragility fractures. (red_flag_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
24. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
25. Hip/vertebral fracture, cord/neurologic symptoms with back pain, severe hypercalcemia with dehydration/AKI/confusion, or symptomatic hypocalcemia with seizure/laryngospasm/arrhythmia. (red_flag_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
26. T-score <= -2.5 or fragility fracture confirms osteoporosis. (management_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
27. Hip/vertebral fracture or very low T-score changes to high/very-high-risk pathway. (management_02) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
28. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
29. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_05) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_06) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Osteoporosis: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ENDO_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03) - source AACE_OSTEOPOROSIS_2020; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_01) - Targets catecholamine-related tachyarrhythmia or severe hypertension complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Pheochromocytoma.; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When palpitations or catecholamine excess are possible; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Pheochromocytoma, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; pheochromocytoma; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect for diaphoresis (exam_02) - Targets catecholamine excess, adrenergic spell severity, or autonomic instability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Pheochromocytoma.; options Absent / Present / Profuse/during spell / Unable to assess; use when When catecholamine spells, episodic symptoms, or hypertensive crisis concern is present; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect for diaphoresis is supportive for Pheochromocytoma, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; pheochromocytoma; inspect; diaphoresis; source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_03) - Targets catecholamine excess, adrenergic spell severity, or autonomic instability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Pheochromocytoma.; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When catecholamine spells, adrenergic symptoms, or hypertensive crisis concern is present; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Pheochromocytoma, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; pheochromocytoma; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - >3x ULN metanephrines changes to localization/genetic/preoperative alpha-blockade pathway. 4. Management implication - Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Pheochromocytoma.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Pheochromocytoma.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; pheochromocytoma; suspected; adrenal; crisis; treated; immediately; stress; dose; steroids; source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Pheochromocytoma.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Pheochromocytoma.; tags adrenal_gland_disorders; pheochromocytoma; marked; metanephrine; elevation; suppressed; renin; high; aldosterone; confirmed; source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

22. PPGL safety tests: obtain plasma free or urine fractionated metanephrines under appropriate collection conditions, add ECG/cardiac injury testing for crisis symptoms, and reserve imaging/genetic pathway for biochemical framing unless emergency stabilization is needed. (test_05) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Morning cortisol/ACTH or cosyntropin for adrenal insufficiency; aldosterone-renin ratio with potassium correction for primary aldosteronism; metanephrines for PPGL. (test_06) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Cushing screening with 1-mg DST, late-night salivary cortisol, or 24-hour UFC after excluding exogenous steroids; adrenal imaging follows biochemical framing except emergency/known incidentaloma context. (test_07) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive. (reference_01) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

31. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Suspected adrenal crisis is treated immediately with stress-dose steroids and fluids; testing must not delay treatment when unstable. (management_04) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Marked metanephrine elevation, suppressed renin with high aldosterone, or confirmed hypercortisolism changes to specialty localization/subtyping and perioperative planning. (management_05) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Pheochromocytoma: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ES_PHEO_PPGL_2014; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Metanephrine intervals are lab and sampling-position-specific; >3x ULN is highly suggestive. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Important mimics/exclusions: exogenous glucocorticoid exposure or withdrawal, primary versus central adrenal insufficiency, adrenal incidentaloma, medication-altered renin/aldosterone testing, catecholamine excess, severe illness, infection, and electrolyte mimics. (differential_03) - source ESE_ADRENAL_INCIDENTALOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### polycystic_ovary_syndrome_intent_v1: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) (polycystic_ovary_syndrome_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; measure; blood; pressure; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; measure; heart; rate; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
