Focused history questions
1. Ask menstrual timing and bleeding pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) needs urgent evaluation today.; options When was the last menstrual period / How regular are cycles / Bleeding changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; cycle; irregularity; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; full question When was the last menstrual period, how regular are cycles, and has bleeding changed from baseline?
2. Ask PID and pelvic infection features - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options No / New / Rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth / Acne / Scalp hair loss / Deepening voice / Clitoromegaly / Menstrual irregularity / Other ___; ask when Ask when Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; hirsutism; acne; alopecia; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; full question Any new or rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth, acne, scalp hair loss, deepening voice, clitoromegaly, or menstrual irregularity?; details Ask specifically about rapidly progressive facial/body hair growth. | Ask specifically about acne. | Ask specifically about scalp hair loss. | Ask specifically about deepening voice. | Ask specifically about clitoromegaly. | Ask specifically about menstrual irregularity.
3. 4. Ask prolactin symptoms and mass-effect risk - sleep apnea symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Loud snoring / Witnessed apneas / Morning headaches / Daytime sleepiness / Resistant hypertension / CPAP / Other ___; ask when Ask when Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; sleep; apnea; mood; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; full question Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use?; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect acne distribution (exam_01) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options Absent/minimal / Inflammatory acne / Severe/nodulocystic / Unable/deferred; use when When androgen excess or PCOS is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; inspect; acne; distribution; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Inspect terminal hair distribution (exam_02) - Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options Expected distribution / Mild excess / Marked excess / Virilization concern / Unable/deferred; use when When hirsutism, PCOS, or androgen excess is possible; technique Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect terminal hair distribution is supportive for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; inspect; terminal; hair; distribution; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_03) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; use when When PCOS, insulin resistance, or hyperandrogenic metabolic risk is possible; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; inspect; neck; acanthosis; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Tumor-range androgens or rapid virilization changes to urgent imaging/endocrine evaluation. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS).; tags reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; polycystic_ovary_syndrome; tumor; range; androgens; rapid; virilization; urgent; imaging; evaluation; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

16. Exclude mimics: TSH, prolactin, 17-OHP; DHEAS/androstenedione if severe. 19. Hyperandrogenism/PCOS safety tests: pregnancy test when relevant, total/free testosterone with reliable assay, DHEAS or 17-OHP for mimics, TSH/prolactin as indicated, and A1c/OGTT/lipids for cardiometabolic risk. (test_06) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Pregnancy test first when amenorrhea or pregnancy is possible; TSH, prolactin, gonadotropins/estradiol or testosterone/LH/FSH as syndrome-specific anchors. (test_07) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Androgen testing with reliable assays for hyperandrogenism; metabolic screening for PCOS; semen analysis and partner factors when infertility is in scope. (test_08) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Adults: PCOS generally requires 2 of 3 Rotterdam features after excluding mimics: ovulatory dysfunction, hyperandrogenism, PCOM/elevated AMH. (reference_01) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Rapid virilization, very high testosterone/DHEAS, pelvic mass, severe abnormal uterine bleeding. (red_flag_01) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

26. Positive pregnancy with pain/bleeding, rapid virilization, tumor-range androgens, severe abnormal bleeding, visual symptoms/headache, testicular mass, hard breast mass, or priapism. (red_flag_03) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

32. Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
33. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Adults: PCOS generally requires 2 of 3 Rotterdam features after excluding mimics: ovulatory dysfunction, hyperandrogenism, PCOM/elevated AMH. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (differential_02) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
34. Important mimics/exclusions: pregnancy, hypothalamic/pituitary disease, thyroid disease, hyperprolactinemia, PCOS, androgen-secreting tumor, medication or substance effects, primary gonadal failure, menopause/POI, and structural pelvic or testicular disease. (differential_03) - source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Not recommended - Murphy sign (polycystic_ovary_syndrome_intent_v1_suppress_murphy_sign_9) - Murphy sign is outside the validated scope for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS) unless patient modifiers or another validated intent explicitly support that organ-system context.; source PCOS_GUIDELINE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to suppression rules; use this row to audit why a maneuver was not recommended.; tags validated_guideline_workup; reproductive_and_gonadal_disorders; endocrine_metabolic; reproductive_endocrine; gynecology; androgen; vitals; vital_signs; polycystic; ovary; syndrome; pcos; abdominal_pain; vomiting; diarrhea; jaundice; gi_bleed; flank_pain; dysuria; peritonitis; biliary_disease; appendicitis; pyelonephritis; suppressed_not_recommended; authorization validated_clinical_intent_suppress_rule

### prediabetes_intent_v1: Prediabetes

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Prediabetes (prediabetes_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Prediabetes.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; measure; blood; pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Prediabetes.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; measure; heart; rate; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Prediabetes.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; use when During diabetes or metabolic risk assessment; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; measure; weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. Measure waist circumference (safety_check_04) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Prediabetes.; options ___ cm/in / Above risk threshold / Not above risk threshold / Unable/not available; use when When cardiometabolic risk or metabolic syndrome is being assessed; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment tape measure, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; measure; waist; circumference; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask weight trajectory and systemic symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; options How has weight changed from baseline / Was the change intentional / Associated with appetite / Fluid status / Systemic symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?
2. Ask physical activity level and treatment barriers - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; options What is the usual physical activity level / It changed recently because of symptoms / Function / Treatment barriers / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; activity; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question What is the usual physical activity level, and has it changed recently because of symptoms, function, or treatment barriers?
3. Ask ASCVD, heart failure, and vascular disease history - family history of type 2 diabetes - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; family; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any family history of type 2 diabetes, gestational diabetes, premature ASCVD, dyslipidemia, hypertension, or obesity that changes cardiometabolic risk?; details Ask specifically about family history of type 2 diabetes. | Ask specifically about gestational diabetes. | Ask specifically about premature ASCVD. | Ask specifically about dyslipidemia. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about obesity.
4. Ask prior gestational diabetes and macrosomia history - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any history of prior gestational diabetes that changes Prediabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety?; details Ask specifically about history of prior gestational diabetes.
5. Ask OSA symptoms, drowsy-driving risk, and CPAP context - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Loud snoring / Witnessed apneas / Morning headaches / Daytime sleepiness / Resistant hypertension / CPAP / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; sleep; apnea; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any loud snoring, witnessed apneas, morning headaches, daytime sleepiness, resistant hypertension, or CPAP use?; details Ask specifically about loud snoring. | Ask specifically about witnessed apneas. | Ask specifically about morning headaches. | Ask specifically about daytime sleepiness. | Ask specifically about resistant hypertension. | Ask specifically about CPAP use.
6. Ask euglycemic DKA and special-population risks - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; options No / Fatty liver disease / Elevated liver enzymes / Central adiposity / Dyslipidemia / Hypertension / Insulin resistance / Alcohol / Cardiometabolic treatment history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; fatty; liver; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any known fatty liver disease, elevated liver enzymes, central adiposity, dyslipidemia, hypertension, insulin resistance, alcohol use, or cardiometabolic treatment history?; details Ask specifically about known fatty liver disease. | Ask specifically about elevated liver enzymes. | Ask specifically about central adiposity. | Ask specifically about dyslipidemia. | Ask specifically about hypertension. | Ask specifically about insulin resistance. | Ask specifically about alcohol use. | Ask specifically about cardiometabolic treatment history.
7. Ask PCOS and reproductive-metabolic history - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Prediabetes.; options No / History of PCOS that changes Prediabetes risk / Test interpretation / Treatment safety / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; pcos; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any history of PCOS that changes Prediabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety?
8. Ask home glucose pattern and baseline change - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Prediabetes needs urgent evaluation today.; options What is the current weight trajectory / How has it changed from baseline / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; weight; trajectory; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question What is the current weight trajectory, and how has it changed from baseline?
9. Ask ASCVD, heart failure, and vascular disease history - fluid-risk comorbidities - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Prediabetes therapy unsafe.; options No / History of ASCVD / Heart failure / Stroke/TIA / Peripheral arterial disease / Claudication / Vascular procedures / Other ___; ask when Ask when Prediabetes is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; family; diabetes; ascvd; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any history of ASCVD, heart failure, stroke/TIA, peripheral arterial disease, claudication, or vascular procedures?; details Ask specifically about history of ASCVD. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about stroke/TIA. | Ask specifically about peripheral arterial disease. | Ask specifically about claudication. | Ask specifically about vascular procedures.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect neck for acanthosis (exam_01) - Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Prediabetes.; options Absent / Mild neck folds / Marked neck/axillary involvement / Unable to assess; use when When insulin resistance or metabolic syndrome is suspected; technique Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Prediabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; inspect; neck; acanthosis; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - A1c 5.7-6.4, FPG 100-125, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 triggers prevention and surveillance. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Prediabetes.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Prediabetes.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; hour; ogtt; triggers; prevention; surveillance; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Diabetes-range values change diagnosis. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Prediabetes.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Prediabetes.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; diabetes; range; values; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Markedly abnormal glucose, severe hypertension, pregnancy-context concern, or cardiopulmonary symptoms changes disposition from prevention visit to urgent evaluation. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Prediabetes.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Prediabetes.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; markedly; abnormal; glucose; hypertension; pregnancy; concern; cardiopulmonary; disposition; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Prediabetes.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Prediabetes.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Prediabetes or metabolic syndrome thresholds change prevention intensity, lifestyle intervention, cardiometabolic risk-factor treatment, and surveillance interval. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Prediabetes.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Prediabetes.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; metabolic; thresholds; prevention; intensity; lifestyle; intervention; cardiometabolic; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Diabetes-range glycemia, ASCVD/HF/CKD risk, fatty liver, obstructive sleep apnea, PCOS, or pregnancy context changes diagnostic classification, treatment safety, and referral or follow-up urgency. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Prediabetes.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Prediabetes.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; prediabetes; diabetes; range; glycemia; ascvd; risk; fatty; liver; obstructive; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,prediabetes,measure,blood,pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. 3. Interpretation caution - Physical exam scope for Prediabetes (prediabetes_v1_physical_exam_scope) - This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. Check basic bedside data and anthropometrics, document the focused insulin-resistance phenotype exam when relevant, and add foot/vascular, hydration/perfusion, abdominal, cardiopulmonary, or neurologic exams only when symptoms, complications, or a second validated intent trigger them.; limitations This workup is intentionally exam-light: diagnosis and risk stratification are driven by guideline laboratory thresholds, pregnancy/medication context, and anthropometrics rather than a broad physical exam. (red_flag_02) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

(management_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

(management_04) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Prediabetes: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (differential_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### primary_hyperparathyroidism_intent_v1: Primary Hyperparathyroidism

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Primary Hyperparathyroidism (primary_hyperparathyroidism_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; measure; blood; pressure; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; measure; heart; rate; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - calcium-symptom pattern - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Primary Hyperparathyroidism needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Kidney stones / Constipation / Polyuria / Polydipsia / Bone pain / Confusion / Dehydration / Reduced kidney function / Other ___; ask when Ask when Primary Hyperparathyroidism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; kidney; stones; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; full question Any kidney stones, constipation, polyuria, polydipsia, bone pain, confusion, dehydration, or reduced kidney function?; details Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about constipation. | Ask specifically about polyuria. | Ask specifically about polydipsia. | Ask specifically about bone pain. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about reduced kidney function.
2. Ask menopause timing and fracture-risk modifiers - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Primary Hyperparathyroidism therapy unsafe.; options No relevant exposure / Medication change / Supplement use / Missed doses / Biotin / Iodine/contrast exposure / Hormone therapy / Recent treatment change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Primary Hyperparathyroidism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; lithium; thiazides; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; full question Which medications, supplements, missed doses, biotin, iodine/contrast exposure, hormone therapy, or recent treatment changes could alter Primary Hyperparathyroidism interpretation or safety?; details Review missed or delayed doses. | Review high-dose biotin or supplement use. | Review iodine/contrast exposure. | Review hormone therapy or androgen/estrogen exposure. | Review recent dose or treatment changes.
3. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - kidney stones - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options No / Family history of MEN / Familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia / Parathyroid disease / Kidney stones / Pituitary tumors / Pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors / Jaw tumors / Other ___; ask when Ask when Primary Hyperparathyroidism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; family; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; full question Any family history of MEN, familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia, parathyroid disease, kidney stones, pituitary tumors, pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors, or jaw tumors?; details Ask specifically about family history of MEN. | Ask specifically about familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia. | Ask specifically about parathyroid disease. | Ask specifically about kidney stones. | Ask specifically about pituitary tumors. | Ask specifically about pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors. | Ask specifically about jaw tumors.
4. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - kidney stones 2 - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Primary Hyperparathyroidism needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Kidney stones / Nephrocalcinosis / Hematuria / Flank pain / Recurrent UTIs / Polyuria / Dehydration / Renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Primary Hyperparathyroidism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; stones; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; full question Any kidney stones, nephrocalcinosis, hematuria, flank pain, recurrent UTIs, polyuria, dehydration, or renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
5. Ask hypocalcemia neuromuscular symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Primary Hyperparathyroidism therapy unsafe.; options No / Antiseizure / Enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism / Bone density / Calcium interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Primary Hyperparathyroidism is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; anticonvulsants; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; full question Any antiseizure or enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism, bone density, or calcium interpretation?; details Ask specifically about antiseizure. | Ask specifically about enzyme-inducing medication use.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When osteomalacia, hypercalcemia, hypocalcemia, falls, or weakness is possible; technique Test hip flexion strength against resistance bilaterally while the patient is seated or supine, adapting to pain and safety.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Primary Hyperparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; test; proximal; flexor; strength; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When bone pain, osteomalacia, fracture, or hyperparathyroidism is possible; technique Palpate the symptomatic bone or spine area gently for focal tenderness and correlate with trauma, fracture risk, and neurologic symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Primary Hyperparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; palpate; focal; bone; tenderness; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When falls, fracture risk, weakness, or bone disease is relevant; technique Observe standing and gait only if safe, noting balance, antalgia, assistive-device need, and fall risk.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe gait stability if safe is supportive for Primary Hyperparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; observe; gait; stability; safe; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options No kyphosis/height-loss concern / Kyphosis / Height-loss concern / Unable to assess; use when When osteoporosis or vertebral compression fracture risk is relevant; technique Inspect standing posture and spinal curvature, noting kyphosis, height-loss concern, or vertebral compression pattern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Primary Hyperparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; inspect; spine; posture; kyphosis; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration (exam_05) - Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; options Moist / Dry / Very dry / Unable to assess; use when When hypercalcemia symptoms or poor intake are present; technique Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Primary Hyperparathyroidism, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; inspect; mucous; membranes; dehydration; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Ca >1 mg/dL above ULN, osteoporosis/fracture, stones, reduced renal function, or age criteria can change to surgical pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; above; osteoporosis; fracture; stones; reduced; renal; function; criteria; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; fragility; fracture; very; score; high; risk; secondary; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Primary Hyperparathyroidism.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; primary_hyperparathyroidism; calcium; pattern; renal; involvement; stones; osteoporosis; surgical; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,primary_hyperparathyroidism,measure,blood,pressure; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders,primary_hyperparathyroidism,measure,heart,rate; source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

(test_04) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_05) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. DXA/vertebral imaging or fracture-risk tool when bone density is in scope; calcium/PTH/phosphorus/magnesium/25(OH)D/renal function for calcium-bone disorders. (test_06) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. 24-hour urine calcium and renal imaging when primary hyperparathyroidism, kidney stones, or chronic hypoparathyroidism management is relevant. (test_07) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. (reference_01) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (reference_02) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. PHPT: high calcium with elevated or inappropriately normal PTH; FHH often Ca/Cr clearance ratio <0.01 but overlap exists. (reference_05) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_06) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Severe hypercalcemia, dehydration, AKI, confusion, pancreatitis. (red_flag_01) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
27. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Hip/vertebral fracture, cord/neurologic symptoms with back pain, severe hypercalcemia with dehydration/AKI/confusion, or symptomatic hypocalcemia with seizure/laryngospasm/arrhythmia. (red_flag_03) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Ca >1 mg/dL above ULN, osteoporosis/fracture, stones, reduced renal function, or age criteria can change to surgical pathway. (management_01) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
30. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Fragility fracture, very low T-score, high fracture risk, or secondary cause changes therapy intensity and fall-prevention urgency. (management_04) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Calcium-PTH pattern, renal involvement, stones, osteoporosis, or severe calcium symptoms changes endocrine/surgical pathway. (management_05) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Primary Hyperparathyroidism: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03) - source PHPT_WORKSHOP_2022; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### prolactinoma_intent_v1: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor)

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor) (prolactinoma_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; prolactinoma; measure; blood; pressure; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags pituitary_gland_disorders; prolactinoma; measure; heart; rate; source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test confrontation visual fields (exam_01) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor).; options Full / Bitemporal deficit / Other field deficit / Unable to assess; use when When pituitary mass, macroadenoma, headache, or visual symptoms are relevant; technique Test each eye separately by confrontation, comparing peripheral fields with the examiner's field while the patient fixes on a central target.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test confrontation visual fields is supportive for Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. 16. Serum prolactin; repeat/macroprolactin if mild/discordant. (test_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Pregnancy test, TSH/free T4, renal function. (test_02) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Pituitary MRI after persistent unexplained elevation or mass symptoms. (test_03) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. Prolactin/pituitary mass safety tests: repeat fasting prolactin with macroprolactin, medication, pregnancy, TSH, and renal context, obtain pituitary MRI for persistent or mass-effect concern, and check gonadal/adrenal/thyroid axes for macroadenoma. (test_05) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. Pituitary-axis panel guided by presentation: AM cortisol/ACTH, free T4/TSH, prolactin, IGF-1, LH/FSH with sex steroid, serum/urine osmolality and sodium when DI is possible. (test_06) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Pituitary MRI after biochemical evidence or mass-effect symptoms; dynamic testing such as cosyntropin, OGTT/GH suppression, water deprivation/desmopressin or copeptin testing when indicated. (test_07) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. (reference_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (reference_02) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges. (reference_03) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Visual field deficit, severe headache/apoplexy, cranial nerve palsy, pregnancy with macroadenoma symptoms. (red_flag_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
28. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
29. Pituitary apoplexy, acute severe headache, visual field deficit, cranial nerve palsy, adrenal crisis, severe hypernatremia, inability to drink, or macroadenoma symptoms in pregnancy. (red_flag_03) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. Mass effect or macroadenoma changes urgency and ophthalmology/neurosurgery involvement. (management_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
31. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
32. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
33. Central adrenal insufficiency must be addressed before thyroid hormone escalation; pituitary mass effect changes ophthalmology/neurosurgery urgency. (management_04) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Confirmed hormone excess/deficiency plus MRI finding changes endocrine, neurosurgical, fertility, and replacement-therapy pathway. (management_05) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Prolactinoma (Benign pituitary tumor): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source PITUITARY_PROLACTINOMA_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay. (differential_02) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
37. Important mimics/exclusions: medication effects, pregnancy/lactation, renal/hepatic disease, sellar or parasellar mass, pituitary apoplexy, primary target-gland disease, central versus nephrogenic water-balance disorder, and age/puberty-specific normal variants. (differential_03) - source ES_HYPERPROLACTINEMIA_2011; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

Status: READY
