Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; palpate; thyroid; gland; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant; technique Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate cervical lymph nodes is supportive for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; palpate; cervical; lymph; nodes; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid dysfunction could affect cardiac rhythm; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis is possible; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Listen to voice quality (exam_05) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; options Normal voice / Hoarse / Weak/breathy / Stridor/airway concern / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, or compressive symptoms are present; technique Listen to voice quality during normal speech and ask about new hoarseness, weak voice, or compressive symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Listen to voice quality is supportive for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; listen; voice; quality; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect neck for airway narrowing (exam_06) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; options No visible narrowing / Large goiter/mass effect / Stridor/positional dyspnea / Unable to assess; use when When goiter, neck mass, dyspnea, or compressive symptoms are present; technique Inspect neck contour and observe breathing/stridor; ask about positional dyspnea while avoiding provocative maneuvers in unstable patients.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for airway narrowing is supportive for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; inspect; neck; airway; narrowing; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none

The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Bethesda V/VI, suspicious nodes, calcitonin/CEA elevation, or RET positivity changes surgical/genetic pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; bethesda; suspicious; nodes; calcitonin; elevation; positivity; surgical; genetic; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; suppressed; elevated; thyrotoxicosis; severity; etiology; normal; triggers; central; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_cancer; suspicious; nodule; compressive; abnormal; node; calcitonin; concern; high; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders,thyroid_cancer,measure,blood,pressure; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders,thyroid_cancer,measure,heart,rate; source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. 25. Structural thyroid safety workup: use TSH to route radionuclide scan versus FNA pathway, thyroid ultrasound with cervical-node mapping for suspicious nodules/cancer, calcitonin/RET context when medullary cancer or MEN2 is possible, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine decisions. (test_07) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_08) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_09) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

33. Airway compromise, vocal cord paralysis, bulky nodes, rapidly enlarging mass, suspected anaplastic transformation. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

35. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
36. Bethesda V/VI, suspicious nodes, calcitonin/CEA elevation, or RET positivity changes surgical/genetic pathway. (management_01) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
41. Thyroid Cancer (Papillary, Follicular, Medullary): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_THYROID_CANCER_2025; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
42. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
43. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

- none

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; palpate; thyroid; gland; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant; technique Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate cervical lymph nodes is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; palpate; cervical; lymph; nodes; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid dysfunction could affect cardiac rhythm; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis is possible; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Listen to voice quality (exam_05) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options Normal voice / Hoarse / Weak/breathy / Stridor/airway concern / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, or compressive symptoms are present; technique Listen to voice quality during normal speech and ask about new hoarseness, weak voice, or compressive symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Listen to voice quality is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; listen; voice; quality; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Inspect neck for airway narrowing (exam_06) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; options No visible narrowing / Large goiter/mass effect / Stridor/positional dyspnea / Unable to assess; use when When goiter, neck mass, dyspnea, or compressive symptoms are present; technique Inspect neck contour and observe breathing/stridor; ask about positional dyspnea while avoiding provocative maneuvers in unstable patients.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect neck for airway narrowing is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; inspect; neck; airway; narrowing; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; suppressed; elevated; thyrotoxicosis; severity; etiology; normal; triggers; central; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyroid Nodules (Benign).; tags thyroid_disorders; thyroid_nodules; suspicious; nodule; compressive; abnormal; node; calcitonin; concern; high; source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders,thyroid_nodules,measure,blood,pressure; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders,thyroid_nodules,measure,heart,rate; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01_limitation) - Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyroid Nodules (Benign), not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders,thyroid_nodules,palpate,thyroid,gland; source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Evidence / likelihood-ratio metadata
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Has the thyroid nodule or neck mass grown rapidly, become painful, fixed, or associated with hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes? (question_01) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
4. Any neck swelling, rapid growth, pain, hoarseness, trouble swallowing, positional shortness of breath, radiation exposure, or family thyroid cancer/MEN2 history? (question_02) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
5. Any childhood head/neck radiation, therapeutic radiation, occupational exposure, or family thyroid cancer risk factor? (question_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
6. Any personal or family history of MEN, VHL, NF1, SDHx-related tumors, medullary thyroid cancer, pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, or adrenal/pituitary tumors? (question_04) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
7. Have you had heat intolerance, palpitations, tremor, anxiety, unintentional weight loss, diarrhea, or increased sweating? (question_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.
8. How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms? (question_06) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize the source, assess severity, and guide management.

19. TSH. (test_01) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. High-quality thyroid ultrasound with risk stratification. (test_02) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. FNA when indicated by size/risk system; low TSH prompts radionuclide scan. (test_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Structural thyroid safety workup: use TSH to route radionuclide scan versus FNA pathway, thyroid ultrasound with cervical-node mapping for suspicious nodules/cancer, calcitonin/RET context when medullary cancer or MEN2 is possible, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine decisions. (test_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_06) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_07) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_01) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_02) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. FNA size thresholds are risk-system-specific; high-risk nodules are sampled at smaller sizes than low-risk nodules. (reference_04) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. Hard fixed nodule, rapid growth, vocal cord symptoms, suspicious lymph nodes, childhood radiation, MEN2 family history. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
33. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
34. Suspicious ultrasound/Bethesda cytology changes cancer pathway. (management_01) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Low TSH with hot nodule shifts to autonomous-function pathway. (management_02) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_05) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_06) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
40. Thyroid Nodules (Benign): diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_THYROID_NODULE_DTC_2015; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
42. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ETA_THYROID_NODULE_2023; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### thyrotoxicosis_intent_v1: Thyrotoxicosis

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Thyrotoxicosis (thyrotoxicosis_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; measure; blood; pressure; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Thyrotoxicosis.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; measure; heart; rate; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask thyroid nodule, pain, and compressive symptoms - Do you have anterior neck - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Thyrotoxicosis therapy unsafe.; options Not possible / Possible / Known pregnant/postpartum / Timing/dating known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Thyrotoxicosis is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; neck; pain; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; full question Do you have anterior neck pain or thyroid tenderness, painful swallowing, recent viral illness, fever, or postpartum timing suggesting thyroiditis?
2. Review thyroid medications, iodine/biotin exposure, and assay interference - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Thyrotoxicosis therapy unsafe.; options No relevant exposure / Medication change / Supplement use / Missed doses / Biotin / Iodine/contrast exposure / Hormone therapy / Recent treatment change / Other ___; ask when Ask when Thyrotoxicosis is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; iodine; exposure; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; full question Any thyroid hormone, antithyroid drug, amiodarone, lithium, iodine/contrast exposure, biotin, supplement use, missed dose, or recent dose change affecting thyroid interpretation?; details Ask specifically about thyroid hormone. | Ask specifically about antithyroid drug. | Ask specifically about amiodarone. | Ask specifically about lithium. | Ask specifically about iodine/contrast exposure. | Ask specifically about biotin. | Ask specifically about supplement use. | Ask specifically about missed dose.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Palpate thyroid gland (exam_01) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Normal size / Goiter / Nodule/asymmetry / Tender / Fixed/hard / Unable to assess; use when During thyroid hormone or thyroid structural evaluation; technique Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; palpate; thyroid; gland; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate cervical lymph nodes (exam_02) - Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options No concerning nodes / Tender/mobile nodes / Firm/fixed nodes / Supraclavicular node / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid nodule, thyroid cancer, goiter, neck symptoms, or autoimmune thyroid disease is relevant; technique Palpate cervical and supraclavicular nodal chains systematically, noting size, tenderness, mobility, firmness, and laterality.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate cervical lymph nodes is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; palpate; cervical; lymph; nodes; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Auscultate heart rhythm (exam_03) - Targets thyroid-related tachycardia, atrial fibrillation, bradycardia, or heart-failure complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Regular / Irregular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Unable to assess; use when When thyroid dysfunction could affect cardiac rhythm; technique Auscultate the precordium with diaphragm and bell as appropriate, noting rate regularity, extra sounds, murmurs, rubs, or gallop.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment stethoscope, cooperation low; limitations Auscultate heart rhythm is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; auscultate; heart; rhythm; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Observe outstretched-hands tremor (exam_04) - Targets thyrotoxicosis adrenergic signs, medication effect, or endocrine-related neuromuscular irritability; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Thyrotoxicosis.; options No tremor / Fine tremor / Coarse tremor / Functionally limiting tremor / Unable to assess; use when When hyperthyroidism or thyrotoxicosis is possible; technique Ask the patient to extend both arms and spread fingers; observe for fine tremor and functional impact.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe outstretched-hands tremor is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; observe; outstretched; hands; tremor; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Inspect eyes for proptosis (exam_05) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Absent / Mild / Marked / Vision/exposure concern / Unable to assess; use when When Graves disease or orbitopathy is possible; technique Inspect eyes and eyelids from the front and side for lid lag/retraction, proptosis, conjunctival injection, or exposure signs.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect eyes for proptosis is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; inspect; eyes; proptosis; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Test extraocular movements (exam_06) - Checks visual, ocular, or cranial-nerve complications that can change imaging urgency, specialty escalation, treatment safety, or monitoring for Thyrotoxicosis.; options Full / Restricted / Diplopia / Painful / Unable to assess; use when When diplopia, eye pain, proptosis, or Graves orbitopathy is possible; technique Ask the patient to follow a target through cardinal gaze positions and observe for limitation, pain, diplopia, or nystagmus.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test extraocular movements is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; test; extraocular; movements; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Low uptake changes management away from antithyroid drugs toward thyroiditis/supportive pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyrotoxicosis.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; uptake; management; away; antithyroid; drugs; toward; thyroiditis; supportive; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyrotoxicosis.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyrotoxicosis.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyrotoxicosis.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; suppressed; elevated; severity; etiology; normal; triggers; central; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Thyrotoxicosis.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Thyrotoxicosis.; tags thyroid_disorders; thyrotoxicosis; suspicious; nodule; compressive; abnormal; node; calcitonin; concern; high; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags thyroid_disorders,thyrotoxicosis,measure,blood,pressure; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags thyroid_disorders,thyrotoxicosis,measure,heart,rate; source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; limitations Palpate thyroid gland is supportive for Thyrotoxicosis, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. 21. RAIU: high uptake suggests overproduction; low uptake suggests thyroiditis/exogenous/iodine-related in many cases. (test_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. ESR/CRP if painful thyroid. (test_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Thyrotoxicosis safety data: check TSH, free T4, total T3, TRAb/TSI or RAIU as indicated, ECG for tachyarrhythmia, CBC/liver baseline before antithyroid drugs, and pregnancy testing before radioiodine or teratogenic treatment decisions. (test_06) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. TSH with free T4; add T3 for thyrotoxicosis, TPO Ab for autoimmune hypothyroidism, TRAb/TSI for Graves, and thyroid ultrasound/FNA pathway for nodules. (test_07) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. Hold high-dose biotin before susceptible assays when feasible and interpret thyroid labs with pregnancy, acute illness, and central hypothyroidism context. (test_08) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. (reference_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (reference_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. T3, TRAb/TSI, TPO Ab, and thyroglobulin Ab are assay-specific. (reference_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
30. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
31. Storm physiology, AF, heart failure, severe weight loss, altered mental status. (red_flag_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
33. Thyroid storm, myxedema coma, atrial fibrillation/heart failure, vision-threatening orbitopathy, rapidly enlarging neck mass, hoarseness, dysphagia, dyspnea, or suspicious nodes. (red_flag_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
34. Low uptake changes management away from antithyroid drugs toward thyroiditis/supportive pathway. (management_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Suppressed TSH with elevated T4/T3 changes to thyrotoxicosis severity and etiology workup; low FT4 with low/normal TSH triggers central hypothyroidism evaluation. (management_04) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. Suspicious nodule, compressive symptom, abnormal node, calcitonin/RET concern, or high-risk ultrasound feature changes imaging/FNA/surgical referral pathway. (management_05) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Thyrotoxicosis: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: TSH typical adult range about 0.4-4.0 mIU/L; use local lab, pregnancy, and illness context. Free T4 typical adult range about 0.8-1.8 ng/dL; use local assay. (differential_02) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Important mimics/exclusions: Graves disease, toxic nodule/multinodular goiter, thyroiditis, exogenous thyroid hormone or supplements, iodine/amiodarone effects, nonthyroidal illness, pregnancy-related thyroid disease, malignancy, and compressive nonthyroid neck disease. (differential_03) - source ATA_HYPERTHYROIDISM_2016; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
