### type_1_diabetes_mellitus_intent_v1: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus (type_1_diabetes_mellitus_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; measure; blood; pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; measure; heart; rate; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; use when During diabetes or metabolic risk assessment; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; measure; weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask hyperglycemia thirst, polyuria, and dehydration symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options How much are you drinking / Urinating / Including polyuria / Polydipsia / Overnight urination / Thirst intensity / Access to water / Any dehydration / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; rapid; polyuria; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion?; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
2. Ask weight trajectory and systemic symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options How has weight changed from baseline / Was the change intentional / Associated with appetite / Fluid status / Systemic symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; weight; loss; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?
3. Ask hyperglycemia dehydration symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; options Diabetes type known / Insulin regimen known / Pump/CGM use / Last insulin dose known / Unknown / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; vomiting; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any vomiting, abdominal pain, deep or labored breathing, confusion, dehydration, infection symptoms, missed insulin, pump failure, steroid use, or very high glucose/ketones suggesting hyperglycemic crisis?; details Ask specifically about vomiting. | Ask specifically about abdominal pain. | Ask specifically about deep. | Ask specifically about labored breathing. | Ask specifically about confusion. | Ask specifically about dehydration. | Ask specifically about infection symptoms. | Ask specifically about missed insulin.
4. Ask thyroid cancer radiation, biopsy, and MEN2 risk - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options No / Personal / Family autoimmune disease / Endocrine disease / Thyroid cancer/MEN2 / Adrenal disease / Related inherited condition / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; personal; family; autoimmune; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any personal or family autoimmune disease, endocrine disease, thyroid cancer/MEN2, adrenal disease, or related inherited condition?; details Ask specifically about personal. | Ask specifically about family autoimmune disease. | Ask specifically about endocrine disease. | Ask specifically about thyroid cancer/MEN2. | Ask specifically about adrenal disease. | Ask specifically about related inherited condition.
5. Ask infection precipitant or mimic symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options No / Measured fever / Rigors / Hypothermia / Toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant / Mimic / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; fever; rigors; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any measured fever, rigors, hypothermia, or toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant or mimic?; details Ask specifically about measured fever. | Ask specifically about rigors. | Ask specifically about hypothermia. | Ask specifically about toxic appearance.
6. Ask respiratory infection symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Cough / Sputum / Pleuritic pain / Dyspnea / Hypoxemia / Focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia / Respiratory infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; respiratory; infection; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any cough, sputum, pleuritic pain, dyspnea, hypoxemia, or focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia or respiratory infection?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
7. Ask urinary symptoms and renal warning features - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Dysuria / Urinary frequency / Urgency / Suprapubic pain / Hematuria / Flank pain suggesting UTI / Pyelonephritis / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; urinary; flank; infection; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any dysuria, urinary frequency or urgency, suprapubic pain, hematuria, or flank pain suggesting UTI or pyelonephritis?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
8. Ask foot/wound infection symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; options No / Foot ulcer / Wound / Skin redness / Drainage / Tenderness / Indwelling line/device / Procedure-site concern suggesting infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; skin; foot; wound; line; infection; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any foot ulcer, wound, skin redness, drainage, tenderness, indwelling line/device, or procedure-site concern suggesting infection?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect feet for ulcers (exam_01) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options No ulcer / Ulcer/wound / Callus/deformity / Infection concern / Unable to assess; use when When established diabetes, neuropathy, infection, vascular disease, or foot symptoms are relevant; technique Inspect both feet including interdigital spaces and pressure points for ulcer, callus, erythema, drainage, deformity, or infection.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect feet for ulcers is supportive for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; inspect; feet; ulcers; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate pedal pulses (exam_02) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options Symmetric/normal / Diminished / Absent / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; use when When established diabetes, vascular symptoms, foot symptoms, or chronic complications are relevant; technique Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate pedal pulses is supportive for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; palpate; pedal; pulses; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test monofilament sensation (exam_03) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; options Protective sensation intact / Reduced / Absent / Unable to assess; use when When neuropathy risk, established diabetes duration, foot symptoms, or fall risk is relevant; technique Apply a 10-g monofilament perpendicular to standard plantar sites until it bends, with eyes closed, documenting sites felt or missed.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment 10-g monofilament, cooperation low; limitations Test monofilament sensation is supportive for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; test; monofilament; sensation; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Ketosis plus acidosis requires urgent DKA pathway. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; ketosis; plus; acidosis; requires; urgent; pathway; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - Positive antibodies or low C-peptide supports autoimmune insulin-deficient diabetes. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; positive; antibodies; peptide; supports; autoimmune; insulin; deficient; diabetes; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Ketosis/acidosis, HHS osmolality, or severe hypoglycemia triggers urgent protocolized treatment rather than routine outpatient adjustment. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; ketosis; acidosis; osmolality; hypoglycemia; triggers; urgent; protocolized; rather; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - CKD, albuminuria, ASCVD/HF risk, pregnancy, foot infection, or insulin access barriers change medication choice and follow-up urgency. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_1_diabetes_mellitus; albuminuria; ascvd; risk; pregnancy; foot; infection; insulin; access; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_1_diabetes_mellitus,measure,blood,pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_1_diabetes_mellitus,measure,heart,rate; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_1_diabetes_mellitus,measure,weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. 14. Interpretation caution - A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_1_diabetes_mellitus,normal,prediabetes,diabetes; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Interpretation caution - Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (reference_02_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_1_diabetes_mellitus,fasting,plasma,glucose,normal,prediabetes,diabetes; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Interpretation caution - 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. (reference_03_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_1_diabetes_mellitus,hour,ogtt,normal,prediabetes,diabetes; source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Interpretation caution - Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. 15. A1c/glucose criteria plus ketones/acid-base if symptomatic. (test_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
16. Beta-hydroxybutyrate, BMP, venous/arterial pH, bicarbonate, anion gap. (test_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
17. Islet autoantibodies: GAD65, IA-2, ZnT8, insulin Ab if needed; C-peptide with concurrent glucose. (test_03) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
18. Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. (test_04) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
19. Diabetes safety tests: review POC/plasma glucose trend, BMP with potassium/bicarbonate/anion gap and creatinine, beta-hydroxybutyrate or urine ketones when acutely ill, and UACR/lipids for complication-directed therapy. (test_05) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
20. A1c or plasma glucose criteria for diagnosis/classification, plus CGM/glucometer pattern when available. (test_06) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
21. CMP/eGFR, urine albumin-creatinine ratio, lipids, and ketones/anion gap/beta-hydroxybutyrate when acute symptoms or insulin deficiency are possible. (test_07) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. (reference_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (reference_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. (reference_03) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. (reference_04) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
26. DKA: diabetes/hyperglycemia plus ketosis plus metabolic acidosis; glucose may be lower in euglycemic DKA. (reference_05) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
27. DKA beta-hydroxybutyrate often >=3.0 mmol/L with pH <7.3 or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L. (reference_06) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
28. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_07) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
29. Vomiting, dehydration, hypotension, Kussmaul respirations, altered mental status. (red_flag_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
30. SGLT2 inhibitor with ketotic symptoms even if glucose is not very high. (red_flag_02) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
31. Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation. (red_flag_03) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
32. DKA/HHS physiology, recurrent severe hypoglycemia, vomiting/dehydration, altered mental status, infected foot wound, or pregnancy with hyperglycemia. (red_flag_04) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.
33. Ketosis plus acidosis requires urgent DKA pathway. (management_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
34. Positive antibodies or low C-peptide supports autoimmune insulin-deficient diabetes. (management_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
35. Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
36. Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
37. Ketosis/acidosis, HHS osmolality, or severe hypoglycemia triggers urgent protocolized treatment rather than routine outpatient adjustment. (management_05) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
38. CKD, albuminuria, ASCVD/HF risk, pregnancy, foot infection, or insulin access barriers change medication choice and follow-up urgency. (management_06) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
39. Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
40. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (differential_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_HYPERGLYCEMIC_CRISES_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### type_2_diabetes_mellitus_intent_v1: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (type_2_diabetes_mellitus_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; measure; blood; pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; measure; heart; rate; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Measure current weight (safety_check_03) - Quantifies anthropometric and cardiometabolic risk so abnormal values can change diagnostic criteria, treatment selection, dosing context, or prevention intensity for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options ___ kg/lb / Recent weight gain / Recent weight loss / Unable/not available; use when During diabetes or metabolic risk assessment; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment scale, cooperation low; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; measure; weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask hyperglycemia thirst, polyuria, and dehydration symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options How much are you drinking / Urinating / Including polyuria / Polydipsia / Overnight urination / Thirst intensity / Access to water / Any dehydration / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; polyuria; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question How much are you drinking and urinating, including polyuria, polydipsia, overnight urination, thirst intensity, access to water, and any dehydration or confusion?; details Clarify urine volume, nocturia, and urinary frequency. | Ask about thirst intensity and access to water. | Ask about dehydration, confusion, and inability to keep up with intake.
2. Ask weight trajectory and systemic symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options How has weight changed from baseline / Was the change intentional / Associated with appetite / Fluid status / Systemic symptoms / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?
3. Ask retinopathy and dilated-eye exam status - new headache - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / New headache / Peripheral vision loss / Double vision / Eye movement pain / Sudden severe headache / Nausea / Pituitary surgery/radiation history / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; blurred; vision; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any new headache, peripheral vision loss, double vision, eye movement pain, sudden severe headache, nausea, or pituitary surgery/radiation history?; details Ask specifically about new headache. | Ask specifically about peripheral vision loss. | Ask specifically about double vision. | Ask specifically about eye movement pain. | Ask specifically about sudden severe headache. | Ask specifically about nausea. | Ask specifically about pituitary surgery/radiation history.
4. Ask infection precipitant or mimic symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options No / Measured fever / Rigors / Hypothermia / Toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant / Mimic / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; fever; rigors; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any measured fever, rigors, hypothermia, or toxic appearance suggesting infection as a precipitant or mimic?; details Ask specifically about measured fever. | Ask specifically about rigors. | Ask specifically about hypothermia. | Ask specifically about toxic appearance.
5. Ask respiratory infection symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Cough / Sputum / Pleuritic pain / Dyspnea / Hypoxemia / Focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia / Respiratory infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; respiratory; infection; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any cough, sputum, pleuritic pain, dyspnea, hypoxemia, or focal chest symptoms suggesting pneumonia or respiratory infection?; details Ask about new cough, sputum color/amount, dyspnea, pleuritic pain, wheeze, aspiration risk, sick respiratory contacts, and oxygen requirement. | Ask whether symptoms are focal, worsening, associated with rigors, or severe enough to change chest imaging, testing, isolation, antibiotics, or disposition.
6. Ask urinary symptoms and renal warning features - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Dysuria / Urinary frequency / Urgency / Suprapubic pain / Hematuria / Flank pain suggesting UTI / Pyelonephritis / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; urinary; flank; infection; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any dysuria, urinary frequency or urgency, suprapubic pain, hematuria, or flank pain suggesting UTI or pyelonephritis?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.
7. Ask foot/wound infection symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; options No / Foot ulcer / Wound / Skin redness / Drainage / Tenderness / Indwelling line/device / Procedure-site concern suggesting infection / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; skin; foot; wound; line; infection; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any foot ulcer, wound, skin redness, drainage, tenderness, indwelling line/device, or procedure-site concern suggesting infection?; details Ask about immunosuppression, transplant/chemotherapy, high-dose steroids/biologics, asplenia, frailty, pregnancy, and recent healthcare exposure. | Ask about travel, outdoor or vector exposure, animal exposure, food/water exposure, sick contacts, sexual exposure risk, injection drug use, and new medications.
8. Ask ASCVD, heart failure, and vascular disease history - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus therapy unsafe.; options No / History of ASCVD / Heart failure / Stroke/TIA / Peripheral arterial disease / Claudication / Vascular procedures / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; ascvd; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any history of ASCVD, heart failure, stroke/TIA, peripheral arterial disease, claudication, or vascular procedures?; details Ask specifically about history of ASCVD. | Ask specifically about heart failure. | Ask specifically about stroke/TIA. | Ask specifically about peripheral arterial disease. | Ask specifically about claudication. | Ask specifically about vascular procedures.
9. Ask diabetic foot wound, neuropathy, and self-care risk - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Numbness / Tingling / Burning pain / Loss of protective sensation / Falls / Ulcers / Neuropathy diagnosis / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; neuropathy; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any numbness, tingling, burning pain, loss of protective sensation, falls, ulcers, or neuropathy diagnosis?; details Ask specifically about numbness. | Ask specifically about tingling. | Ask specifically about burning pain. | Ask specifically about loss of protective sensation. | Ask specifically about falls. | Ask specifically about ulcers. | Ask specifically about neuropathy diagnosis.
10. Ask retinopathy and dilated-eye exam status - known retinopathy - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Retinopathy / Vision changes / Eye injections/laser / Overdue dilated eye examination / Other ___; ask when Ask when Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; retinopathy; source ADA_SOC_2026; full question Any known retinopathy, vision changes, eye injections/laser, or overdue dilated eye examination?; details Ask specifically about known retinopathy. | Ask specifically about vision changes. | Ask specifically about eye injections/laser. | Ask specifically about overdue dilated eye examination.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Inspect feet for ulcers (exam_01) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options No ulcer / Ulcer/wound / Callus/deformity / Infection concern / Unable to assess; use when When established diabetes, neuropathy, infection, vascular disease, or foot symptoms are relevant; technique Inspect both feet including interdigital spaces and pressure points for ulcer, callus, erythema, drainage, deformity, or infection.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect feet for ulcers is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; inspect; feet; ulcers; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate pedal pulses (exam_02) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options Symmetric/normal / Diminished / Absent / Asymmetric / Unable to assess; use when When established diabetes, vascular symptoms, foot symptoms, or chronic complications are relevant; technique Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate pedal pulses is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; palpate; pedal; pulses; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Test monofilament sensation (exam_03) - Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; options Protective sensation intact / Reduced / Absent / Unable to assess; use when When neuropathy risk, established diabetes duration, foot symptoms, or fall risk is relevant; technique Apply a 10-g monofilament perpendicular to standard plantar sites until it bends, with eyes closed, documenting sites felt or missed.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment 10-g monofilament, cooperation low; limitations Test monofilament sensation is supportive for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; test; monofilament; sensation; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Diabetes-range A1c/FPG/OGTT changes diagnosis and treatment intensity. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; diabetes; range; ogtt; intensity; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
2. Management implication - UACR >=30 or reduced eGFR changes kidney-protective therapy. (management_02) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; uacr; reduced; egfr; kidney; protective; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
3. Management implication - Unstable physiology, endocrine crisis, pregnancy-critical medication issue, or dangerous electrolyte/glucose abnormality changes disposition to urgent monitored care. (management_03) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; unstable; physiology; crisis; pregnancy; critical; medication; issue; dangerous; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
4. Management implication - Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment. (management_04) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; discordant; borderline; tests; should; confirmed; interpreted; timing; assay; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
5. Management implication - Ketosis/acidosis, HHS osmolality, or severe hypoglycemia triggers urgent protocolized treatment rather than routine outpatient adjustment. (management_05) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; ketosis; acidosis; osmolality; hypoglycemia; triggers; urgent; protocolized; rather; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.
6. Management implication - CKD, albuminuria, ASCVD/HF risk, pregnancy, foot infection, or insulin access barriers change medication choice and follow-up urgency. (management_06) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders; type_2_diabetes_mellitus; albuminuria; ascvd; risk; pregnancy; foot; infection; insulin; access; source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Limitations and interpretation cautions
1. Interpretation caution - Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01_limitation) - Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_2_diabetes_mellitus,measure,blood,pressure; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Interpretation caution - Measure heart rate (safety_check_02_limitation) - Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_2_diabetes_mellitus,measure,heart,rate; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
3. Interpretation caution - Measure current weight (safety_check_03_limitation) - Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; limitations Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_2_diabetes_mellitus,measure,weight; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
4. 14. Interpretation caution - A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. (reference_01_limitation) - Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; limitations Use local assay methods, reference intervals, imaging availability, specimen quality, timing, medications, pregnancy status, and baseline values when interpreting this Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus test or threshold.; tags diabetes_and_blood_sugar_disorders,type_2_diabetes_mellitus,normal,prediabetes,diabetes; source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
15. Interpretation caution - Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. 16. Interpretation caution - 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. 40. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ADA_SOC_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
41. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. (differential_02) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
42. Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes. (differential_03) - source ADA_DIAGNOSIS_2026; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.

### vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia_intent_v1: Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia

Status: READY
Readiness: Complete validated workup
Validated bundles: installed_guideline_module
Installed module: Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia (vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia_v1)

Basic bedside data / safety checks
1. Measure blood pressure (safety_check_01) - Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options ___/___ mmHg / Hypotensive / Severe hypertension / Orthostatic concern / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; measure; blood; pressure; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
2. Measure heart rate (safety_check_02) - Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options ___ bpm regular / Tachycardic / Bradycardic / Irregular / Not available; use when At every endocrine workup encounter; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment watch/timer or bedside monitor, cooperation low; limitations Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; measure; heart; rate; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

Focused history questions
1. Ask proximal muscle weakness symptoms - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options Do you have trouble rising from a chair / Climbing stairs / Lifting overhead / Getting up from the floor / Other ___; ask when Ask when Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; proximal; weakness; source ENDO_VITD_2024; full question Do you have trouble rising from a chair, climbing stairs, lifting overhead, or getting up from the floor?
2. Ask malabsorption and nutrient-absorption risks - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia therapy unsafe.; options No / Malabsorption / Bariatric surgery / Inflammatory bowel disease / Celiac disease / Pancreatic disease / Chronic diarrhea affecting nutrient / Medication absorption / Other ___; ask when Ask when Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; malabsorption; bariatric; surgery; source ENDO_VITD_2024; full question Any malabsorption, bariatric surgery, inflammatory bowel disease, celiac disease, pancreatic disease, or chronic diarrhea affecting nutrient or medication absorption?; details Ask specifically about malabsorption. | Ask specifically about bariatric surgery. | Ask specifically about inflammatory bowel disease. | Ask specifically about celiac disease. | Ask specifically about pancreatic disease. | Ask specifically about chronic diarrhea.
3. Ask renal, liver, and mineral-metabolism modifiers - Defines the patient-specific context needed to interpret endocrine tests, avoid common mimics, and choose the safest next step for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options No / Chronic kidney disease / Liver disease / Malabsorption / Bariatric surgery / Other condition that could alter vitamin D / Calcium / Phosphorus / PTH interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; liver; source ENDO_VITD_2024; full question Any chronic kidney disease, liver disease, malabsorption, bariatric surgery, or other condition that could alter vitamin D, calcium, phosphorus, or PTH interpretation?; details Ask specifically about chronic kidney disease. | Ask specifically about liver disease. | Ask specifically about malabsorption. | Ask specifically about bariatric surgery. | Ask specifically about other condition.
4. Ask hypocalcemia neuromuscular symptoms - Identifies exposures, physiologic states, or treatment changes that can explain abnormal results or make Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia therapy unsafe.; options No / Antiseizure / Enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism / Bone density / Calcium interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; anticonvulsants; source ENDO_VITD_2024; full question Any antiseizure or enzyme-inducing medication use that could affect vitamin D metabolism, bone density, or calcium interpretation?; details Ask specifically about antiseizure. | Ask specifically about enzyme-inducing medication use.
5. Ask renal stone and calcium-complication symptoms - Defines symptom severity, tempo, complications, and whether Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia needs urgent evaluation today.; options No / Kidney stones / Nephrocalcinosis / Hematuria / Flank pain / Recurrent UTIs / Polyuria / Dehydration / Renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation / Other ___; ask when Ask when Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia is the selected validated clinical intent or when this feature would change endocrine test interpretation, urgency, or treatment safety.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; stones; source ENDO_VITD_2024; full question Any kidney stones, nephrocalcinosis, hematuria, flank pain, recurrent UTIs, polyuria, dehydration, or renal-function changes affecting calcium/PTH interpretation?; details Ask about dysuria, frequency, urgency, hematuria, suprapubic pain, flank pain, catheter/procedure context, prior resistant organisms, pregnancy possibility, vomiting, and reduced urine output. | Ask whether urinary/flank symptoms are paired with rigors, hypotension, confusion, obstruction concern, renal dysfunction, or inability to tolerate oral intake.

Core physical exam maneuvers
1. Test proximal hip flexor strength (exam_01) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options Normal strength / Mild weakness / Needs arms/assistance / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When osteomalacia, hypercalcemia, hypocalcemia, falls, or weakness is possible; technique Test hip flexion strength against resistance bilaterally while the patient is seated or supine, adapting to pain and safety.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Test proximal hip flexor strength is supportive for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; test; proximal; flexor; strength; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
2. Palpate focal bone tenderness (exam_02) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options No focal tenderness / Focal tenderness / Diffuse tenderness / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When bone pain, osteomalacia, fracture, or hyperparathyroidism is possible; technique Palpate the symptomatic bone or spine area gently for focal tenderness and correlate with trauma, fracture risk, and neurologic symptoms.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Palpate focal bone tenderness is supportive for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; palpate; focal; bone; tenderness; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
3. Observe gait stability if safe (exam_03) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options Stable / Unsteady / Needs assistive device/help / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When falls, fracture risk, weakness, or bone disease is relevant; technique Observe standing and gait only if safe, noting balance, antalgia, assistive-device need, and fall risk.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Observe gait stability if safe is supportive for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; observe; gait; stability; safe; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
4. Inspect spine posture for kyphosis (exam_04) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options No kyphosis/height-loss concern / Kyphosis / Height-loss concern / Unable to assess; use when When osteoporosis or vertebral compression fracture risk is relevant; technique Inspect standing posture and spinal curvature, noting kyphosis, height-loss concern, or vertebral compression pattern.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Inspect spine posture for kyphosis is supportive for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; inspect; spine; posture; kyphosis; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
5. Elicit Chvostek sign (exam_05) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options Negative / Positive / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When symptomatic hypocalcemia is possible; technique Tap over the facial nerve anterior to the ear and observe for ipsilateral facial twitching, interpreting cautiously because false positives occur.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment none, cooperation low; limitations Elicit Chvostek sign is supportive for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; elicit; chvostek; sign; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
6. Elicit Trousseau sign (exam_06) - Targets hypocalcemia, osteomalacia/osteoporosis complication, fracture risk, or fall risk; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; options Negative / Positive / Unable/unsafe to assess; use when When symptomatic hypocalcemia is possible and safe to perform; technique Inflate a blood-pressure cuff above systolic pressure for up to three minutes if safe and observe for carpopedal spasm.; feasibility difficulty easy, time 1 min, equipment blood pressure cuff, cooperation low; limitations Elicit Trousseau sign is supportive for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; elicit; trousseau; sign; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

Conditional exam add-ons
- none
Conditional add-on status: No conditional add-ons active (none_active_for_current_context) - No patient modifier, source-specific symptom, or complication trigger activated a reviewed conditional physical exam add-on for this selected validated intent. The core exam remains the validated minimum for the current context.

Management-changing findings
1. Management implication - Low 25(OH)D with symptoms or osteomalacia labs changes from prevention advice to treatment and etiology evaluation. (management_01) - Links a result or bedside finding to a concrete management change for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; limitations This rule supports but does not replace clinician judgment, local protocols, patient preferences, access constraints, comorbidities, and reassessment for Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia.; tags bone_and_parathyroid_disorders; vitamin_d_deficiency_osteomalacia; osteomalacia; labs; prevention; advice; etiology; evaluation; source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management-change row; use it to connect a finding or result to treatment, monitoring, referral, or disposition decisions.

Thyroid safety data: pair TSH/free T4 with syndrome-specific add-ons such as T3, antibodies, ultrasound/FNA, or radionuclide scan, and account for pregnancy, biotin, acute illness, central disease, and medication effects before treatment changes. (test_04) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(reference_02) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
22. DXA T-score: normal >= -1.0; osteopenia -1.0 to -2.5; osteoporosis <= -2.5. (reference_03) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
23. 25(OH)D thresholds vary; many labs flag <20 ng/mL as low and <12 ng/mL as severe deficiency, while 2024 Endocrine Society guidance avoids universal screening/target thresholds for healthy adults. (reference_04) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
24. Osteomalacia pattern can include low/normal calcium, low phosphorus, high alkaline phosphatase, and secondary high PTH. (reference_05) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.
25. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects. (reference_06) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not encoded in this workup-support row unless the cited source validates the exact test threshold; use the cited guideline, local assay characteristics, and pretest probability.

(red_flag_01) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag/escalation row; use it to identify urgency and safety risk rather than to quantify diagnostic probability.

28. Hip/vertebral fracture, cord/neurologic symptoms with back pain, severe hypercalcemia with dehydration/AKI/confusion, or symptomatic hypocalcemia with seizure/laryngospasm/arrhythmia. 34. Vitamin D Deficiency / Osteomalacia: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup (differential_01) - source ENDO_VITD_2024; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
35. Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Corrected calcium often about 8.5-10.5 mg/dL; ionized calcium about 1.12-1.32 mmol/L; use local lab. Intact PTH often about 10-65 pg/mL but assay-specific. (differential_02) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
36. Important mimics/exclusions: primary versus secondary or tertiary parathyroid disease, malignancy-associated calcium disorder, renal disease, vitamin D deficiency or excess, medication effects, malabsorption, osteomalacia, osteoporosis, and acute fracture. (differential_03) - source NIH_VITD_FACTSHEET; LR+ n/a, LR- n/a; LR note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this diagnostic-frame row; use it to structure the differential and avoid premature closure before applying source-backed tests and exam findings.
